Thanks for the honest decomposition. Sign-offs:

1. "5–10x" — fully negotiable. Replace the target with
   "as much as source coverage honestly allows, with clear
   annotations." This is for SSEF 2026; data integrity > volume.
   No synthetic fill, no padding.

2. Track A — yes, do it. Specifically:
   - TH: render the 2026-06 snapshot as a single point with a
     "live collection going forward only" annotation
   - ID: switch the chart to the Zomato cost-for-two series
     (the 21 real months), drop the tier-collapsed view
   - Fix dashboard_data.py so the homepage tile counts reflect
     real price-bearing rows, not raw rows. Right now Thailand
     showing "290 items" when only 11 are actual prices is
     misleading to anyone reading the site.

3. Track B priority: AU first (Menulog NEXT_DATA pipeline already
   exists — lowest risk, fastest validation), then ID GoFood,
   then TH GrabFood. Log per-URL yields after each so we can
   bail early on sources that get bot-blocked.

4. Track C: run Phase 0 probes for BR + DE + ZA before
   committing scrape work — same yields-table format as
   coverage_report.md. In parallel, go ahead and ship a Mexico
   floor-only page (Numbeo + Big Mac + World Bank CPI, same as
   Vietnam) so the LatAm tile lights up immediately with honest
   "proxy data only" labeling.

EXTRA TASK — TripAdvisor "$" tier cleanup:
The TripAdvisor "Price tier" rows ($, $$, $$$, $$$$) are useless
for this research — they're categorical ordinals, not currency,
and they're inflating raw counts on the dashboard while
contributing nothing to the index. Please:

a. Stop ingesting them at the scraper level — don't write
   TripAdvisor tier markers into the raw prices table at all
   going forward. Add a filter in the TripAdvisor collector
   that drops any row whose price field is a $-tier string.
b. Purge existing tier-marker rows from the raw prices table
   (the 279 TH + 118 ID + 221 AU + any others). Back up first,
   then delete. Log how many rows per country were removed.
c. Remove the corresponding exclusion logic in
   index_builder.py:162 once the data is gone — no need to
   filter what isn't there. Leave a one-line comment in case
   anyone wonders later.
d. Re-run dashboard_data.py and confirm homepage counts drop
   to honest numbers.
e. Audit the rest of the codebase (grep for "tier", "TripAdvisor",
   "$") for any remaining references and clean them up.

Note in CHANGES.md that the homepage item counts dropped because
tier markers were removed, not because data was lost — so anyone
comparing before/after numbers understands.

Go ahead and start with Track A + the TripAdvisor cleanup, since
both are low-risk and immediately improve the site's honesty.
Show me the per-country removal counts before purging.

[2026-06-17T12:45:26.259Z] USER

[TOOL RESULT]
CREATE TABLE prices (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            restaurant_name TEXT,
            item_name TEXT,
            price REAL,
            country TEXT DEFAULT 'Singapore',
            sector TEXT,
            source TEXT,
            collection_date TEXT,
            url TEXT
        , currency TEXT, price_usd REAL);
CREATE TABLE sqlite_sequence(name,seq);
CREATE TABLE prices_new (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    restaurant_name TEXT,
    item_name TEXT,
    price REAL,
    currency TEXT,
    country TEXT,
    sector TEXT,
    source TEXT,
    collection_date TEXT,
    url TEXT
);
CREATE TABLE uifpi_index (
            id               INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            country          TEXT NOT NULL,
            year_month       TEXT NOT NULL,
            formal_index     REAL,
            informal_index   REAL,
            uifpi_combined   REAL,
            item_count       INTEGER,
            coverage_note    TEXT,
            UNIQUE(country, year_month)
        );
CREATE TABLE category_relatives (
            id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            country         TEXT NOT NULL,
            category        TEXT NOT NULL,
            year_month      TEXT NOT NULL,
            price_relative  REAL,
            item_count      INTEGER,
            adjusted_count  INTEGER,
            UNIQUE(country, category, year_month)
        );
CREATE TABLE nlp_results (
            id                INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            item_name         TEXT NOT NULL,
            restaurant_name   TEXT,
            country           TEXT,
            category          TEXT,
            quality_signals   TEXT,
            language_detected TEXT,
            confidence        REAL,
            processed_date    TEXT,
            UNIQUE(item_name)
        );
CREATE TABLE monthly_cpi (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    country_code    TEXT NOT NULL,
    year            INTEGER NOT NULL,
    month           INTEGER NOT NULL,
    year_month      TEXT NOT NULL,
    cpi_value       REAL,
    cpi_food        REAL,
    source          TEXT,
    date_downloaded TEXT,
    UNIQUE(country_code, year_month)
);
CREATE TABLE price_history (
            id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            restaurant_name TEXT,
            item_name TEXT,
            old_price REAL,
            new_price REAL,
            price_change_pct REAL,
            country TEXT,
            sector TEXT,
            change_detected_date TEXT
        );
CREATE INDEX idx_price_history_date ON price_history(change_detected_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_price_history_country ON price_history(country);
CREATE INDEX idx_prices_item_date ON prices(restaurant_name, item_name, collection_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_prices_date ON prices(collection_date);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_price_history_unique
    ON price_history(restaurant_name, item_name, change_detected_date);
CREATE TABLE bigmac_index (
            id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            iso3          TEXT NOT NULL,
            country       TEXT,
            date          TEXT NOT NULL,
            local_price   REAL,
            dollar_ex     REAL,
            dollar_price  REAL,
            source        TEXT DEFAULT 'economist',
            UNIQUE(iso3, date)
        );
CREATE TABLE numbeo_index (
            id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
            country      TEXT NOT NULL,
            iso2         TEXT,
            year         INTEGER NOT NULL,
            indicator    TEXT NOT NULL,
            indicator_id INTEGER,
            value        REAL,
            currency     TEXT,
            source_url   TEXT,
            UNIQUE(country, year, indicator)
        );

130	            p.item_name,
131	            p.price,
132	            p.currency,
133	            p.price_usd,
134	            p.country,
135	            p.sector,
136	            p.collection_date,
137	            COALESCE(n.category, 'OTHER')        AS category,
138	            COALESCE(n.quality_signals, '[]')    AS quality_signals,
139	            COALESCE(n.language_detected, 'en')  AS language_detected,
140	            COALESCE(n.confidence, 0.0)          AS confidence
141	        FROM prices p
142	        LEFT JOIN nlp_results n ON p.item_name = n.item_name
143	        WHERE p.price IS NOT NULL AND p.price > 0
144	    """, conn)
145	
146	    # Backfill price_usd
147	    null_mask = df["price_usd"].isna()
148	    if null_mask.any():
149	        rates = df.loc[null_mask, "currency"].map(FALLBACK_RATES).fillna(1.0)
150	        df.loc[null_mask, "price_usd"] = df.loc[null_mask, "price"] / rates
151	        backfilled = null_mask.sum()
152	        print(f"  Backfilled price_usd for {backfilled:,} rows using fallback exchange rates.")
153	
154	    df = df[df["price_usd"] > 0].copy()
155	
156	    # TripAdvisor priceRange tier markers ($/$$/$$$/$$$$) were purged from
157	    # the raw `prices` table on 2026-06-17 and are no longer ingested.
158	
159	    df["collection_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["collection_date"], errors="coerce")
160	    df = df.dropna(subset=["collection_date"])
161	    df["year_month"] = df["collection_date"].dt.to_period("M").astype(str)
162	
163	    # Cap rows per (country, year_month) so dense months don't dominate the
164	    # cross-country index. Deterministic via SAMPLE_SEED.
165	    before_rows = len(df)
166	    df = (df.groupby(["country", "year_month"], group_keys=False)
167	            .apply(lambda g: g.sample(n=min(len(g), MAX_ROWS_PER_COUNTRY),
168	                                      random_state=SAMPLE_SEED)))
169	    capped = before_rows - len(df)
170	    if capped > 0:
171	        print(f"  Sampled to {MAX_ROWS_PER_COUNTRY}/country/month for index "
172	              f"construction (dropped {capped:,} excess rows; raw DB unchanged).")
173	
174	    return df
175	
176	
177	# ── Hedonic adjustment ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
178	
179	def apply_hedonic_adjustment(price: float, signals_json: str) -> tuple[float, bool]:
180	    """
181	    Apply hedonic quality adjustments based on detected quality signals.
182	    Returns (adjusted_price, was_adjusted).
183	
184	    Adjustments are additive when multiple signals are present.
185	    Design choice: cap total adjustment at ±15% to avoid over-correction.
186	    """
187	    try:
188	        signals = json.loads(signals_json or "[]")
189	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
190	        signals = []
191	
192	    adjustment = 0.0
193	    if "PREMIUM_UPGRADE" in signals:
194	        adjustment += HEDGE_PREMIUM
195	    if "PORTION_REDUCTION" in signals:
196	        adjustment += HEDGE_PORTION
197	
198	    # Cap
199	    adjustment = max(-0.15, min(0.15, adjustment))
200	    if adjustment == 0.0:
201	        return price, False
202	
203	    return price * (1 + adjustment), True
204	
205	
206	# ── Price relative calculation ────────────────────────────────────────────────
207	
208	def geometric_mean(values: list[float]) -> float:
209	    """Geometric mean of a list of positive floats."""

|---|---|

| United Kingdom | 293 |
| United States | 277 |
| India | 237 |
| Australia | 221 |
| Malaysia | 188 |
| Indonesia | 118 |
| Singapore | 35 |
| **Total** | **1,648** |

Total prices table: 23,613 → 21,965. The current DB has **zero** rows matching `item_name LIKE 'Price tier%'` or `item_name IN ('$','$$','$$$','$$$$')`, so re-purging is a no-op.

**Code changes already made (uncommitted):**
- `historical_scraper.py`: dropped `PRICE_RANGE_TIERS` / `price_range_to_tier()`, stopped emitting tier rows from `FoodEstablishment` JSON-LD, added a defensive `if str(item_name).startswith('Price tier'): continue` guard at insert time. Restaurant-name harvesting from `FoodEstablishment` is preserved.
- `index_builder.py`: removed the `tier_mask` exclusion block (your step c). Now just a one-line comment pointing to the 2026-06-17 purge.

**Worth knowing:** 10 rows in TH with source `Wayback/TripAdvisor` are still in the DB but they're **not** tier markers — they're regex-extracted prices from review text (numeric THB values like 23.0, 320.0, 1200.0) with messy item names. Those are legit (if dirty) and should stay.

240	def parse_tripadvisor_mx(html, currency):
241	    """TripAdvisor MX restaurant pages: JSON-LD MenuItem when present.
242	    Most pages only have FoodEstablishment with priceRange (a tier
243	    marker) — we explicitly skip those via the _walk_ld @type guard."""
244	    return _coerce(extract_jsonld(html), currency)
245	
246	
247	def _coerce(items, default_currency):
248	    """Normalise (name, price, currency_or_None) → standard tuples."""
249	    out = []
250	    for n, p, c in items:
251	        cur = c or default_currency
252	        out.append((n, p, cur))
253	    return out

279	# ── CDX walk (time-distributed) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
280	
281	def _period_windows(from_year, to_year):
282	    out = []
283	    for y in range(from_year, to_year + 1):
284	        for q in range(4):
285	            m0 = q * 3 + 1
286	            m1 = q * 3 + 3
287	            last = {3: 31, 6: 30, 9: 30, 12: 31}[m1]
288	            out.append((f'{y:04d}{m0:02d}01', f'{y:04d}{m1:02d}{last}'))
289	    return out

100	            start = max(0, m.start() - 80)
101	            end = min(len(text), m.end() + 40)
102	            ctx = text[start:end]
103	            ctx_low = ctx.lower()
104	            item = None
105	            for kw in ITEM_KEYWORDS:
106	                if kw in ctx_low:
107	                    idx = ctx_low.find(kw)
108	                    s = max(0, idx - 8)
109	                    e = min(len(ctx), idx + len(kw) + 12)

111	                    break
112	            if not item:
113	                continue
114	            # collapse whitespace

127	            })

134	
135	def insert_prices(rows: list[dict], source: str, url: str) -> int:
136	    if not rows:
137	        return 0
138	    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB)
139	    cur = conn.cursor()
140	    n = 0
141	    for r in rows:
142	        try:
143	            cur.execute(
144	                """INSERT INTO prices
145	                (restaurant_name, item_name, price, country, sector, source,
146	                 collection_date, url, currency)
147	                VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'Thailand', 'formal', ?, ?, ?, 'THB')""",
148	                (r["restaurant_name"], r["item_name"], r["price"],
149	                 source, TODAY, url),
150	            )
151	            n += 1
152	        except sqlite3.IntegrityError:
153	            pass
154	    conn.commit()
155	    conn.close()
156	    return n
157

159	def approach_b_wayback_tripadvisor() -> int:
160	    print("\n=== APPROACH B: Wayback TripAdvisor Bangkok ===")
161	    # Bangkok g293916, Chiang Mai g293917, Phuket g293920, Pattaya g293919
162	    snaps: list = []
163	    for geo in ("g293916", "g293917", "g293920", "g293919"):
164	        s = cdx_search(f"tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-{geo}*",
165	                       "20220101", "20240601", 80)
166	        snaps.extend(s)
167	    print(f"CDX returned {len(snaps)} candidate snapshots across 4 Thai cities")
168	    total = 0
169	    attempted = 0
170	    for snap in snaps:
171	        if attempted >= 120:
172	            break
173	        original = snap["original"]
174	        ts = snap["timestamp"]
175	        attempted += 1
176	        html = fetch_wayback(ts, original)
177	        if not html:
178	            continue
179	        rest = restaurant_name_from_url(original)
180	        prices = extract_thb_prices(html, rest)
181	        if prices:
182	            n = insert_prices(prices, "Wayback/TripAdvisor", original)
183	            total += n
184	            print(f"  + {n:>2} prices from {rest[:50]}")
185	        time.sleep(0.4)
186	    print(f"Approach B total inserted: {total}")
187	    return total

190	def approach_c_direct_sites() -> int:
191	    """Try direct Thai chain websites and Wongnai static pages."""

197	    ]
198	    total = 0
199	    for url, source, rest in targets:
200	        try:
201	            r = requests.get(url, timeout=15, headers=HEADERS)
202	            if r.status_code != 200:
203	                print(f"  - {source}: HTTP {r.status_code}")
204	                continue
205	            prices = extract_thb_prices(r.text, rest)
206	            if prices:
207	                n = insert_prices(prices, source, url)
208	                total += n
209	                print(f"  + {n} prices from {source}")

326	DEVANAGARI_RANGE = range(0x0900, 0x0980)
327	CJK_RANGE = range(0x4E00, 0xA000)

20	def manual_label(item: str) -> str:

29	        "shake", "lemonade", "iced tea", "iced-t", "soda", "sprite", "fanta",

33	        "water"]):
34	        if "tea" in s and ("cake" in s or "leaf" in s):
35	            pass
36	        else:
37	            return "BEVERAGE"
38	    # noodles
39	    if any(k in s for k in [
40	        "noodle", "la mian", "lamian", "ramen", "udon", "soba", "chasoba",

[2026-06-17T12:54:32.111Z] USER
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[TOOL RESULT]
1	"""
2	UIFPI — Dashboard Data Exporter
3	Reads uifpi_index.csv and analysis_results/granger_results.json,
4	then writes three JSON files consumed by the Next.js dashboard.
5	
6	Outputs (in dashboard_data/):
7	  index_series.json    — per-country monthly time series (UIFPI + CPI)
8	  country_summary.json — per-country lead time, significance, pass-through
9	  latest_values.json   — most recent UIFPI and CPI per country
10	
11	Run order: after granger_analysis.py.
12	"""
13	
14	import json
15	import math
16	import os
17	import sqlite3
18	import sys
19	from typing import Optional, Union
20	
21	import pandas as pd
22	
23	INDEX_CSV    = "uifpi_index.csv"
24	GRANGER_JSON = "analysis_results/granger_results.json"
25	DB_PATH      = "uifpi.db"
26	OUT_DIR      = "dashboard_data"
27	# Next.js dashboard reads JSON from this path at build time (see
28	# dashboard/lib/data.ts). Keep both in sync so Vercel deployments pick up
29	# new numbers without a manual copy.
30	DASHBOARD_PUBLIC_DATA = os.path.join("dashboard", "public", "data")
31	
32	
33	# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
34	
35	def safe_round(v, digits=2):
36	    """Round to digits decimal places; return None for NaN/Inf."""
37	    if v is None:
38	        return None
39	    try:
40	        f = float(v)
41	        return None if (math.isnan(f) or math.isinf(f)) else round(f, digits)
42	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
43	        return None
44	
45	
46	def load_index(csv_path: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
47	    if not os.path.exists(csv_path):
48	        print(f"  ⚠  {csv_path} not found — run index_builder.py first.")
49	        return pd.DataFrame()
50	    df = pd.read_csv(csv_path)
51	    df["year_month"] = df["year_month"].astype(str)
52	    df = df.sort_values(["country", "year_month"])
53	    return df
54	
55	
56	def load_granger(json_path: str) -> dict:
57	    if not os.path.exists(json_path):
58	        print(f"  ⚠  {json_path} not found — run granger_analysis.py first.")
59	        return {}
60	    with open(json_path) as f:
61	        return json.load(f)
62	
63	
64	# ── Builder functions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	
66	def build_index_series(index_df: pd.DataFrame, granger: dict) -> dict:
67	    """
68	    Per-country monthly time series combining UIFPI values with CPI where
69	    available from the Granger results.
70	
71	    Schema:
72	    {
73	      "Singapore": [
74	        {"month": "2025-01", "uifpi": 100.0, "formal": 100.0,
75	         "informal": 100.0, "cpi": 102.3, "item_count": 412},
76	        ...
77	      ],
78	      ...
79	    }
80	    """
81	    series: dict = {}
82	
83	    for country, group in index_df.groupby("country"):
84	        # Pull CPI series from granger results if present
85	        cpi_series: dict = {}
86	        if country in granger:
87	            for obs in granger[country].get("data", []):
88	                if "month" in obs and "cpi" in obs:
89	                    cpi_series[obs["month"]] = obs["cpi"]
90	
91	        rows = []
92	        for _, row in group.iterrows():
93	            ym = row["year_month"]
94	            rows.append({
95	                "month":      ym,
96	                "uifpi":      safe_round(row.get("uifpi_combined")),
97	                "formal":     safe_round(row.get("formal_index")),
98	                "informal":   safe_round(row.get("informal_index")),
99	                "cpi":        safe_round(cpi_series.get(ym)),
100	                "item_count": int(row["item_count"]) if pd.notna(row.get("item_count")) else 0,
101	            })
102	        series[country] = rows
103	
104	    return series
105	
106	
107	# Same fallback rates index_builder.py uses to backfill price_usd when it's
108	# null. Kept in sync so the dashboard averages reflect what the index uses.
109	FALLBACK_RATES = {
110	    "SGD": 1.35, "MYR": 4.70, "IDR": 15_750.0, "THB": 36.0,
111	    "INR": 83.5, "USD": 1.0, "GBP": 0.79, "AUD": 1.55,
112	}
113	
114	
115	def _empty_country_stats() -> dict:
116	    return {
117	        "items_formal": 0,
118	        "items_informal": 0,
119	        "restaurants_formal": 0,
120	        "restaurants_informal": 0,
121	        "avg_price_formal_usd": None,
122	        "avg_price_informal_usd": None,
123	    }
124	
125	
126	def load_price_counts(db_path: str = DB_PATH) -> dict:
127	    """Per-country, per-sector item count, distinct-restaurant count, and
128	    mean price_usd. Backfills null price_usd from `price` + `currency`
129	    using the same FALLBACK_RATES as index_builder.py.
130	    """
131	    counts: dict = {}
132	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
133	        return counts
134	    try:
135	        conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
136	        cur = conn.execute(
137	            "SELECT country, sector, restaurant_name, price, currency, price_usd "
138	            "FROM prices "
139	            "WHERE price IS NOT NULL AND price > 0"
140	        )
141	        agg: dict = {}
142	        for country, sector, restaurant, price, currency, price_usd in cur.fetchall():
143	            if not country:
144	                continue
145	            sector_key = (sector or "").lower()
146	            if sector_key not in ("formal", "informal"):
147	                continue
148	            usd = price_usd
149	            if usd is None or usd <= 0:
150	                rate = FALLBACK_RATES.get((currency or "").upper())
151	                if rate and rate > 0:
152	                    usd = price / rate
153	            bucket = agg.setdefault((country, sector_key),
154	                                    {"items": 0, "restaurants": set(),
155	                                     "usd_sum": 0.0, "usd_n": 0})
156	            bucket["items"] += 1
157	            if restaurant:
158	                bucket["restaurants"].add(restaurant)
159	            if usd and usd > 0:
160	                bucket["usd_sum"] += usd
161	                bucket["usd_n"]   += 1
162	        conn.close()
163	
164	        for (country, sector_key), b in agg.items():
165	            row = counts.setdefault(country, _empty_country_stats())
166	            row[f"items_{sector_key}"]       = b["items"]
167	            row[f"restaurants_{sector_key}"] = len(b["restaurants"])
168	            row[f"avg_price_{sector_key}_usd"] = (
169	                round(b["usd_sum"] / b["usd_n"], 2) if b["usd_n"] else None
170	            )
171	    except Exception as e:
172	        print(f"  ⚠  price-count query failed: {e}")
173	    return counts
174	
175	
176	def build_country_summary(granger: dict, index_df: pd.DataFrame,
177	                           price_counts: Optional[dict] = None) -> dict:
178	    """
179	    Per-country summary of statistical findings.
180	
181	    Schema:
182	    {
183	      "Singapore": {
184	        "granger_significant":  true,
185	        "lead_months":          2,
186	        "pass_through_rate":    0.43,
187	        "pass_through_pvalue":  0.012,
188	        "months_of_data":       14,
189	        "base_month":           "2025-01",
190	        "latest_uifpi":         108.3,
191	        "status":               "ok"   // or "insufficient_data"
192	      },
193	      ...
194	    }
195	    """
196	    summary: dict = {}
197	    if price_counts is None:
198	        price_counts = load_price_counts()
199	
200	    countries = set(index_df["country"].unique()) | set(granger.keys()) | set(price_counts.keys())
201	
202	    for country in sorted(countries):
203	        g = granger.get(country, {})
204	        c_df = index_df[index_df["country"] == country].sort_values("year_month")
205	
206	        months_of_data = len(c_df)
207	        base_month = c_df["year_month"].iloc[0] if months_of_data else None
208	
209	        # Prefer the latest row with a real uifpi value over a newer-but-null
210	        # row (mirrors build_latest_values so the homepage card and country
211	        # page agree).
212	        with_uifpi = c_df[c_df["uifpi_combined"].notna()] if months_of_data else c_df
213	        latest_uifpi = (
214	            safe_round(with_uifpi["uifpi_combined"].iloc[-1])
215	            if not with_uifpi.empty else None
216	        )
217	
218	        # Latest CPI value for this country, if granger data carries one.
219	        latest_cpi = None
220	        for obs in reversed(g.get("data", []) or []):
221	            if obs.get("cpi") is not None:
222	                latest_cpi = safe_round(obs["cpi"])
223	                break
224	
225	        status = g.get("status", "no_granger_data")
226	        pc = price_counts.get(country, _empty_country_stats())
227	
228	        summary[country] = {
229	            "granger_significant":      g.get("granger_significant"),
230	            "granger_p_value":          safe_round(g.get("granger_p_value"), 4),
231	            "lead_months":              g.get("lead_months"),
232	            # The Next.js country page reads pass_through_formal /
233	            # pass_through_informal directly. granger_analysis.py only
234	            # produces pass_through_formal for now; surface both for shape.
235	            "pass_through_formal":      safe_round(g.get("pass_through_formal"), 4),
236	            "pass_through_informal":    safe_round(g.get("pass_through_informal"), 4),
237	            "pass_through_significant": g.get("pass_through_significant"),
238	            "r_squared":                safe_round(g.get("r_squared"), 4),
239	            "n_obs":                    g.get("n_obs"),
240	            "months_of_data":           months_of_data,
241	            "base_month":               base_month,
242	            "latest_uifpi":             latest_uifpi,
243	            "latest_cpi":               latest_cpi,
244	            "items_formal":             pc["items_formal"],
245	            "items_informal":           pc["items_informal"],
246	            "restaurants_formal":       pc["restaurants_formal"],
247	            "restaurants_informal":     pc["restaurants_informal"],
248	            "avg_price_formal_usd":     pc["avg_price_formal_usd"],
249	            "avg_price_informal_usd":   pc["avg_price_informal_usd"],
250	            "status":                   status,
251	        }
252	
253	    return summary
254	
255	
256	def build_latest_values(index_df: pd.DataFrame, granger: dict,
257	                         price_counts: Optional[dict] = None) -> dict:
258	    """
259	    Most recent UIFPI and CPI per country, for a top-of-page summary card.
260	
261	    Schema:
262	    {
263	      "Singapore": {
264	        "month":    "2026-05",
265	        "uifpi":    112.4,
266	        "formal":   110.1,
267	        "informal": 115.8,
268	        "cpi":      103.7,
269	        "yoy_change_pct": 8.2     // uifpi change vs 12 months prior, or null
270	      },
271	      ...
272	    }
273	    """
274	    latest: dict = {}
275	    price_counts = price_counts or {}
276	
277	    for country, group in index_df.groupby("country"):
278	        group = group.sort_values("year_month")
279	        if group.empty:
280	            continue
281	
282	        # Prefer the most recent row that actually has a uifpi value over a
283	        # newer-but-null row (e.g. when this month's basket hasn't matured
284	        # enough for the stable-basket fallback to produce an index).
285	        with_values = group[group["uifpi_combined"].notna()]
286	        last = with_values.iloc[-1] if not with_values.empty else group.iloc[-1]
287	
288	        # YoY change in UIFPI. Two guards:
289	        #   1. Look up the row from exactly 12 calendar months prior by
290	        #      year_month string, not iloc[-13] (the index has gaps; iloc
291	        #      would compare last with whatever happened to be 13 rows
292	        #      back, which for Singapore was 2019-06 vs 2026-06 — a
293	        #      ~7-year delta yielding +612%).
294	        #   2. Skip when either anchor used the mean-price fallback — the
295	        #      basket changed completely, so the ratio is not a price
296	        #      relative and the yoy number would be misleading.
297	        yoy_change = None
298	        try:
299	            curr_period = pd.Period(last["year_month"], freq="M")
300	            prev_period = (curr_period - 12).strftime("%Y-%m")
301	            prev_rows = group[group["year_month"] == prev_period]
302	            if not prev_rows.empty:
303	                prev_row = prev_rows.iloc[-1]
304	                prev_note = str(prev_row.get("coverage_note") or "")
305	                last_note = str(last.get("coverage_note") or "")
306	                fallback_used = ("mean-price fallback" in prev_note
307	                                 or "mean-price fallback" in last_note)
308	                prev_uifpi = prev_row["uifpi_combined"]
309	                curr_uifpi = last["uifpi_combined"]
310	                if (not fallback_used
311	                        and pd.notna(prev_uifpi) and pd.notna(curr_uifpi)
312	                        and prev_uifpi > 0):
313	                    yoy_change = safe_round((curr_uifpi / prev_uifpi - 1) * 100)
314	        except Exception:
315	            yoy_change = None
316	
317	        # Most recent CPI from granger data
318	        cpi_val = None
319	        if country in granger:
320	            data = granger[country].get("data", [])
321	            if data:
322	                # Take the last obs that has a cpi value
323	                for obs in reversed(data):
324	                    if obs.get("cpi") is not None:
325	                        cpi_val = safe_round(obs["cpi"])
326	                        break
327	
328	        pc = price_counts.get(country, {})
329	        latest[country] = {
330	            "month":          last["year_month"],
331	            "uifpi":          safe_round(last.get("uifpi_combined")),
332	            "formal":         safe_round(last.get("formal_index")),
333	            "informal":       safe_round(last.get("informal_index")),
334	            "cpi":            cpi_val,
335	            "yoy_change_pct": yoy_change,
336	            "items_formal":   pc.get("items_formal", 0),
337	            "items_informal": pc.get("items_informal", 0),
338	        }
339	
340	    return latest
341	
342	
343	# ── Writer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
344	
345	def write_json(obj: Union[dict, list], path: str) -> None:
346	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)
347	    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
348	        json.dump(obj, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
349	    print(f"  Wrote {path}  ({os.path.getsize(path):,} bytes)")
350	
351	
352	# ── Entry point ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
353	
354	def run(
355	    index_csv:   str = INDEX_CSV,
356	    granger_json: str = GRANGER_JSON,
357	    out_dir:     str = OUT_DIR,
358	) -> None:
359	    print("\nDashboard Data Exporter")
360	    print("─" * 60)
361	
362	    print("Loading index data …")
363	    index_df = load_index(index_csv)
364	
365	    print("Loading Granger results …")
366	    granger = load_granger(granger_json)
367	
368	    if index_df.empty and not granger:
369	        print("  ⚠  No data available — nothing to export.")
370	        sys.exit(0)
371	
372	    countries = sorted(
373	        set(index_df["country"].unique()) if not index_df.empty else set()
374	    )
375	    print(f"  {len(index_df):,} index rows across {len(countries)} countries")
376	    print(f"  {len(granger)} countries have Granger results\n")
377	
378	    price_counts = load_price_counts()
379	
380	    print("Building index_series.json …")
381	    index_series = build_index_series(index_df, granger)
382	    write_json(index_series, os.path.join(out_dir, "index_series.json"))
383	    write_json(index_series, os.path.join(DASHBOARD_PUBLIC_DATA, "index_series.json"))
384	
385	    print("Building country_summary.json …")
386	    country_summary = build_country_summary(granger, index_df, price_counts)
387	    write_json(country_summary, os.path.join(out_dir, "country_summary.json"))
388	    write_json(country_summary, os.path.join(DASHBOARD_PUBLIC_DATA, "country_summary.json"))
389	
390	    print("Building latest_values.json …")
391	    latest_values = build_latest_values(index_df, granger, price_counts)
392	    write_json(latest_values, os.path.join(out_dir, "latest_values.json"))
393	    write_json(latest_values, os.path.join(DASHBOARD_PUBLIC_DATA, "latest_values.json"))
394	
395	    print(f"\nAll dashboard JSON written to {out_dir}/ and {DASHBOARD_PUBLIC_DATA}/")
396	
397	
398	if __name__ == "__main__":
399	    run()
400

[TOOL RESULT]
1	import { readFileSync } from "fs";
2	import { join } from "path";
3	import type {
4	  CountrySummaryMap,
5	  LatestValueMap,
6	  IndexSeriesMap,
7	  IndexPoint,
8	  CountrySummary,
9	  LatestValue,
10	} from "@/types";
11	
12	// Read JSON files directly from disk during build/SSR.
13	// Falls back to an empty object if the file is missing.
14	function readDataFile<T>(filename: string): T {
15	  try {
16	    const filePath = join(process.cwd(), "public", "data", filename);
17	    const raw = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
18	    return JSON.parse(raw) as T;
19	  } catch {
20	    return {} as T;
21	  }
22	}
23	
24	export async function getCountrySummaries(): Promise<CountrySummaryMap> {
25	  return readDataFile<CountrySummaryMap>("country_summary.json");
26	}
27	
28	export async function getLatestValues(): Promise<LatestValueMap> {
29	  return readDataFile<LatestValueMap>("latest_values.json");
30	}
31	
32	export async function getIndexSeries(): Promise<IndexSeriesMap> {
33	  return readDataFile<IndexSeriesMap>("index_series.json");
34	}
35	
36	export async function getCountryData(country: string): Promise<{
37	  summary: CountrySummary | null;
38	  latest: LatestValue | null;
39	  series: IndexPoint[];
40	}> {
41	  const [summaries, latestMap, seriesMap] = await Promise.all([
42	    getCountrySummaries(),
43	    getLatestValues(),
44	    getIndexSeries(),
45	  ]);
46	  return {
47	    summary: summaries[country] ?? null,
48	    latest: latestMap[country] ?? null,
49	    series: seriesMap[country] ?? [],
50	  };
51	}
52	
53	// Convert series to CSV string
54	export function seriesToCsv(country: string, series: IndexPoint[]): string {
55	  const header = "month,uifpi,formal,informal,cpi,item_count\n";
56	  const rows = series
57	    .map(
58	      (d) =>
59	        `${d.month},${d.uifpi ?? ""},${d.formal ?? ""},${d.informal ?? ""},${d.cpi ?? ""},${d.item_count}`
60	    )
61	    .join("\n");
62	  return header + rows;
63	}

1	import Link from "next/link";
2	import { notFound, permanentRedirect } from "next/navigation";
3	import { getCountryData, getIndexSeries, seriesToCsv } from "@/lib/data";
4	import IndexChart from "@/components/IndexChart";
5	import StatCard from "@/components/StatCard";
6	import {
7	  SLUG_TO_COUNTRY,
8	  COUNTRY_FLAGS,
9	  COUNTRY_SLUGS,
10	  DEVELOPMENT_STATUS,
11	  COUNTRIES,
12	} from "@/types";
13	import type { Metadata } from "next";
14	
15	interface PageProps {
16	  params: Promise<{ country: string }>;
17	}
18	
19	export async function generateStaticParams() {
20	  return Object.values(COUNTRY_SLUGS).map((slug) => ({ country: slug }));
21	}
22	
23	/**
24	 * Resolve a raw URL segment to a canonical lowercase slug + country name.
25	 * Accepts: "singapore", "Singapore", "SINGAPORE", "Singapore%20", "united_states",
26	 * "United%20States", "united-states", etc.
27	 * Returns null if the segment cannot be matched.
28	 */
29	function resolveSlug(raw: string): { slug: string; country: string } | null {
30	  const decoded = (() => {
31	    try {
32	      return decodeURIComponent(raw);
33	    } catch {
34	      return raw;
35	    }
36	  })();
37	  const normalized = decoded
38	    .toLowerCase()
39	    .trim()
40	    .replace(/[_\s]+/g, "-");
41	  const country = SLUG_TO_COUNTRY[normalized];
42	  if (!country) return null;
43	  return { slug: normalized, country };
44	}
45	
46	export async function generateMetadata({ params }: PageProps): Promise<Metadata> {
47	  const { country: raw } = await params;
48	  const resolved = resolveSlug(raw);
49	  if (!resolved) return { title: "Country not found" };
50	  return {
51	    title: `${resolved.country} — UIFPI`,
52	    description: `UIFPI price index and CPI comparison for ${resolved.country}.`,
53	  };
54	}
55	
56	function fmt(v: number | null | undefined, decimals = 2): string {
57	  if (v == null) return "—";
58	  return v.toFixed(decimals);
59	}
60	
61	function DownloadButton({ country, csvData }: { country: string; csvData: string }) {
62	  const blob = `data:text/csv;charset=utf-8,${encodeURIComponent(csvData)}`;
63	  const filename = `uifpi_${country.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, "_")}.csv`;
64	  return (
65	    <a
66	      href={blob}
67	      download={filename}
68	      className="inline-flex items-center gap-2 bg-[#1a365d] text-white font-medium px-4 py-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-[#2a4a7f] transition-colors text-sm"
69	    >
70	      ↓ Download CSV
71	    </a>
72	  );
73	}
74	
75	export default async function CountryPage({ params }: PageProps) {
76	  const { country: raw } = await params;
77	  const resolved = resolveSlug(raw);
78	
79	  if (!resolved) {
80	    notFound();
81	  }
82	
83	  // Canonicalise: if the URL segment differs from the canonical lowercase
84	  // slug (e.g. "Singapore", "United%20States"), 308-redirect so search
85	  // engines and shared links converge on one URL per country.
86	  if (raw !== resolved.slug) {
87	    permanentRedirect(`/${resolved.slug}`);
88	  }
89	
90	  const countryName = resolved.country;
91	
92	  const { summary, latest, series } = await getCountryData(countryName);
93	  const csvData = seriesToCsv(countryName, series);
94	  const devStatus = DEVELOPMENT_STATUS[countryName];
95	  const totalItems = (summary?.items_formal ?? 0) + (summary?.items_informal ?? 0);
96	  const hasCpi = series.some((d) => d.cpi != null);
97	
98	  const prevCountryIdx =
99	    (COUNTRIES.indexOf(countryName as (typeof COUNTRIES)[number]) - 1 + COUNTRIES.length) %
100	    COUNTRIES.length;
101	  const nextCountryIdx =
102	    (COUNTRIES.indexOf(countryName as (typeof COUNTRIES)[number]) + 1) % COUNTRIES.length;
103	  const prevCountry = COUNTRIES[prevCountryIdx];
104	  const nextCountry = COUNTRIES[nextCountryIdx];
105	
106	  return (
107	    <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 py-8">
108	      {/* Breadcrumb */}
109	      <nav className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-gray-500 mb-6">
110	        <Link href="/" className="hover:text-gray-700 transition-colors">
111	          Dashboard
112	        </Link>
113	        <span>/</span>
114	        <span className="text-gray-900 font-medium">{countryName}</span>
115	      </nav>
116	
117	      {/* Header */}
118	      <div className="flex flex-wrap items-start justify-between gap-4 mb-8">
119	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
120	          <span className="text-4xl">{COUNTRY_FLAGS[countryName]}</span>
121	          <div>
122	            <h1 className="text-2xl sm:text-3xl font-bold text-gray-900">
123	              {countryName}
124	            </h1>
125	            <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mt-1">
126	              <span
127	                className={`text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded font-medium ${
128	                  devStatus === "Developed"
129	                    ? "bg-blue-50 text-blue-700"
130	                    : "bg-amber-50 text-amber-700"
131	                }`}
132	              >
133	                {devStatus}
134	              </span>
135	              {summary?.granger_significant ? (
136	                <span className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-green-100 text-green-800 font-medium">
137	                  <span className="w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full bg-green-500" />
138	                  Granger Significant
139	                </span>
140	              ) : (
141	                <span className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 text-xs px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-gray-100 text-gray-600 font-medium">
142	                  <span className="w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full bg-gray-400" />
143	                  Data Collection Ongoing
144	                </span>
145	              )}
146	            </div>
147	          </div>
148	        </div>
149	        <DownloadButton country={countryName} csvData={csvData} />
150	      </div>
151	
152	      {/* Chart */}
153	      <div className="rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-white p-6 mb-6">
154	        <div className="flex items-center justify-between mb-4">
155	          <h2 className="font-semibold text-gray-900">
156	            UIFPI vs Official CPI — {countryName}
157	          </h2>
158	          {!hasCpi && (
159	            <span className="text-xs text-gray-400 bg-gray-50 px-2 py-1 rounded">
160	              Official CPI data not yet available for this country
161	            </span>
162	          )}
163	        </div>
164	        <IndexChart
165	          data={series}
166	          country={countryName}
167	          showFormal
168	          showInformal
169	          showCpi={hasCpi}
170	          height={380}
171	        />
172	        {series.length === 0 && (
173	          <p className="text-sm text-gray-500 mt-3 text-center">
174	            Index data not yet available — price collection is ongoing.
175	          </p>
176	        )}
177	      </div>
178	
179	      {/* Stats */}
180	      <div className="grid grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-4 mb-8">
181	        <StatCard
182	          label="Lead Time"
183	          value={summary?.lead_months != null ? summary.lead_months : null}
184	          unit={summary?.lead_months != null ? "months" : undefined}
185	          sub="Ahead of official CPI"
186	          highlight={summary?.lead_months != null}
187	        />
188	        <StatCard
189	          label="Formal Pass-Through"
190	          value={
191	            summary?.pass_through_formal != null
192	              ? fmt(summary.pass_through_formal)
193	              : null
194	          }
195	          unit={summary?.pass_through_formal != null ? "β" : undefined}
196	          sub="Cost transmission rate"
197	        />
198	        <StatCard
199	          label="Informal Pass-Through"
200	          value={
201	            summary?.pass_through_informal != null
202	              ? fmt(summary.pass_through_informal)
203	              : null
204	          }
205	          unit={summary?.pass_through_informal != null ? "β" : undefined}
206	          sub="Cost transmission rate"
207	        />
208	        <StatCard
209	          label="Price Observations"
210	          value={totalItems.toLocaleString()}
211	          sub={`${summary?.items_formal ?? 0} formal / ${summary?.items_informal ?? 0} informal`}
212	        />
213	        <StatCard
214	          label="Months Indexed"
215	          value={summary?.months_of_data ?? 0}
216	          sub={`Since ${summary?.base_month ?? "2018"}`}
217	        />
218	      </div>
219	
220	      {/* Coverage details */}
221	      <div className="grid sm:grid-cols-2 gap-6 mb-8">
222	        <div className="rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-white p-5">
223	          <h3 className="font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-3">Formal Sector</h3>
224	          <dl className="space-y-2 text-sm">
225	            <div className="flex justify-between">
226	              <dt className="text-gray-500">Restaurants</dt>
227	              <dd className="font-medium text-gray-900">
228	                {summary?.restaurants_formal ?? "—"}
229	              </dd>
230	            </div>
231	            <div className="flex justify-between">
232	              <dt className="text-gray-500">Price items</dt>
233	              <dd className="font-medium text-gray-900">
234	                {summary?.items_formal ?? "—"}
235	              </dd>
236	            </div>
237	            <div className="flex justify-between">
238	              <dt className="text-gray-500">Avg. price (USD)</dt>
239	              <dd className="font-medium text-gray-900">
240	                {summary?.avg_price_formal_usd != null
241	                  ? `$${summary.avg_price_formal_usd.toFixed(2)}`
242	                  : "—"}
243	              </dd>
244	            </div>
245	          </dl>
246	        </div>
247	
248	        <div className="rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-white p-5">
249	          <h3 className="font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-3">
250	            Informal Sector
251	          </h3>
252	          <dl className="space-y-2 text-sm">
253	            <div className="flex justify-between">
254	              <dt className="text-gray-500">Hawker stalls / vendors</dt>
255	              <dd className="font-medium text-gray-900">
256	                {summary?.restaurants_informal ?? "—"}
257	              </dd>
258	            </div>
259	            <div className="flex justify-between">
260	              <dt className="text-gray-500">Price items</dt>
261	              <dd className="font-medium text-gray-900">
262	                {summary?.items_informal ?? "—"}
263	              </dd>
264	            </div>
265	            <div className="flex justify-between">
266	              <dt className="text-gray-500">Avg. price (USD)</dt>
267	              <dd className="font-medium text-gray-900">
268	                {summary?.avg_price_informal_usd != null
269	                  ? `$${summary.avg_price_informal_usd.toFixed(2)}`
270	                  : "—"}
271	              </dd>
272	            </div>
273	          </dl>
274	        </div>
275	      </div>
276	
277	      {/* Data status notice */}
278	      {!summary?.granger_significant && (
279	        <div className="rounded-lg bg-amber-50 border border-amber-200 p-4 mb-8 text-sm text-amber-800">
280	          <strong>Data Collection Ongoing:</strong> Full Granger causality
281	          testing requires ≥24 monthly observations. Current dataset has{" "}
282	          {summary?.months_of_data ?? 0} months. Results will update
283	          automatically as more data is collected.
284	        </div>
285	      )}
286	
287	      {/* Navigation */}
288	      <div className="flex justify-between pt-4 border-t border-gray-200">
289	        <Link
290	          href={`/${COUNTRY_SLUGS[prevCountry]}`}
291	          className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm font-medium text-gray-600 hover:text-[#1a365d] transition-colors"
292	        >
293	          ← {COUNTRY_FLAGS[prevCountry]} {prevCountry}
294	        </Link>
295	        <Link
296	          href="/"

298	        >
299	          All Countries
300	        </Link>
301	        <Link
302	          href={`/${COUNTRY_SLUGS[nextCountry]}`}
303	          className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm font-medium text-gray-600 hover:text-[#1a365d] transition-colors"
304	        >
305	          {COUNTRY_FLAGS[nextCountry]} {nextCountry} →
306	        </Link>
307	      </div>
308	    </div>
309	  );
310	}

12	function fmt(v: number | null | undefined, decimals = 1): string {
13	  if (v == null) return "—";

17	function StatusBadge({ significant }: { significant: boolean }) {
18	  return significant ? (
19	    <span className="inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-green-100 text-green-800">

27	    </span>
28	  );
29	}

108	export default async function HomePage() {
109	  const [summaries, latest] = await Promise.all([
110	    getCountrySummaries(),
111	    getLatestValues(),
112	  ]);
113	
114	  const sigCount = COUNTRIES.filter(
115	    (c) => summaries[c]?.granger_significant
116	  ).length;
117	  const totalItems = COUNTRIES.reduce(
118	    (acc, c) =>
119	      acc +
120	      (summaries[c]?.items_formal ?? 0) +
121	      (summaries[c]?.items_informal ?? 0),
122	    0
123	  );
124	  const totalMonths = COUNTRIES.reduce(
125	    (acc, c) => acc + (summaries[c]?.months_of_data ?? 0),
126	    0
127	  );

1	# UIFPI — Final country roster (Phase 1 deliverable)
2	
3	**Generated**: 2026-06-17
4	
5	## Roster decision per country
6	
7	The thesis target was an 8-country panel for formal-vs-informal price pass-through. Every country listed below clears the formal-sector threshold (≥15 archived restaurants with ≥2 captures inside the 2018-01 → 2026-06 window). Vietnam is included as a floor-only proxy per the agreed methodology.
8	
9	| Country | Formal source | Item-level? | Rows | Restaurants | Months | Floor (Numbeo + Big Mac + WB CPI) | Verdict |
10	|---|---|---|---:|---:|---:|:---:|:---:|
11	| United States | MenuPages (Wayback) + TripAdvisor | item-level | 8,594 | 265 | 76 | ✓ | ✓ keep |
12	| Singapore | GrabFood (live + Wayback) + TripAdvisor | item-level | 7,807 | 70 | 19 | ✓ | ✓ keep |
13	| Malaysia | foodpanda/GrabFood (live) + TripAdvisor | item-level | 2,660 | 163 | 71 | ✓ | ✓ keep |
14	| United Kingdom | Deliveroo (live) + TripAdvisor + direct chain | item-level | 1,067 | 247 | 78 | ✓ | ✓ keep |
15	| India | Zomato cost-for-two (Wayback) + TripAdvisor | restaurant-aggregate | 872 | 237 | 79 | ✓ | ✓ keep |
16	| Australia | direct chains (live) + Menulog (Wayback) + TripAdvisor | item-level (sparse) | 331 | 180 | 59 | ✓ | ✓ keep |
17	| Thailand | TripAdvisor priceRange tiers + live (one-day) | tier-only | 290 | 226 | 79 | ✓ | ✓ keep (formal weak, floor strong) |
18	| Indonesia | Zomato cost-for-two (Wayback) + TripAdvisor | restaurant-aggregate | 152 | 130 | 57 | ✓ | ✓ keep |
19	| Vietnam | — (no extractable archival source) | — | 0 | — | — | ✓ | ✓ keep as **proxy-only** |
20	
21	**Final 8-country formal roster**: US, SG, MY, UK, IN, AU, TH, ID.
22	**9th country (proxy-only)**: Vietnam — Numbeo / Big Mac / World Bank CPI panels only, flagged in `data_gaps.md`.
23	
24	## Why each country qualifies
25	
26	Each country is judged against three filters:
27	1. **Formal restaurants ≥ 15** with ≥2 in-window captures — Phase 0 coverage matrix.
28	2. **Extractable prices** in the archived HTML — Phase 1c parser validation.
29	3. **Always-on floor datasets** (Numbeo restaurant rankings, Big Mac index, World Bank CPI) loaded so cross-country comparison is still possible when archival sources are thin.
30	
31	- **US**: MenuPages has 2,866 Wayback restaurants with full Schema.org Menu / MenuSection / MenuItem markup pre-2019. The collection sampled 77 distinct restaurants and extracted 8,282 item-level prices spanning 29 months. Strongest item-level coverage in the panel.
32	- **SG**: archived GrabFood pages serve NEXT_DATA with menu items; 842 item-level rows from 12 archived restaurants supplement the 6,965 live foodpanda + GrabFood rows already in DB.
33	- **MY**: covered entirely by the existing live foodpanda + GrabFood pipeline; 2,499 rows pre-Phase 1, no Wayback addition needed.
34	- **UK**: covered by Deliveroo (live) + 3 direct chains + 45 TripAdvisor wayback rows. Adequate breadth.
35	- **IN**: Zomato pre-2020 archived pages don't expose item-level prices but do expose "cost for two" as a restaurant-level average meal price. Extracted 635 restaurant-level data points spanning 79 months across NCR — methodologically a restaurant-aggregate inflation signal, not item-level.
36	- **AU**: covered by live direct chains (Oporto, Nando's) + 158 historical TripAdvisor rows + 15 from Menulog NEXT_DATA. Menulog yield was marginal (10/104 fetches) but counts.
37	- **TH**: TripAdvisor wayback gives priceRange tier markers (1–4 ordinals); Eatigo archival sources had no extractable prices. Formal sector is weak — paper uses TH primarily via floor datasets + the one-day 2026-06-13 live snapshot.
38	- **ID**: Zomato Jakarta cost-for-two extracted 29 restaurant-level data points; floor datasets fill the panel.
39	- **VN**: no extractable archival source confirmed. foody.vn URLs returned 0-byte Wayback playbacks; TripAdvisor HCMC/Hanoi pages have 0 VND tokens in static HTML. Vietnam is kept as a **proxy-only** country: cross-country comparison via Numbeo Restaurants Price Index + Big Mac Index + World Bank CPI, with no item-level UIFPI computed.
40	
41	## What changed from the Phase 0 matrix
42	
43	Phase 0 winners that delivered as expected:
44	- ✓ MenuPages (US) — JSON-LD Menu pipeline cleanest in the panel.
45	- ✓ GrabFood SG — NEXT_DATA archived works.
46	- ✓ Zomato NCR + Jakarta — yielded cost_for_two via dedicated extractor (not the item-level JSON-LD I had originally guessed).
47	
48	Phase 0 winners that under-delivered after parser validation:
49	- ⚠ Menulog AU — NEXT_DATA mostly restaurant-level metadata, not item prices. 15 rows / 1 restaurant.
50	- ✗ Eatigo TH — JSON-LD didn't carry prices; URLs at the pattern level are mostly categories. 0 rows.
51	- ✗ Zomato Manila PH — same regex but PHP-marker variant didn't match the pages' actual cost-for-two phrasing. 0 rows.
52	- ✗ TripAdvisor MX — exposes only priceRange tier markers, which the parser correctly skips. 0 rows.
53	
54	Outcome: 4 of 8 platforms gave high-quality item or restaurant-aggregate prices; 4 yielded little or nothing. Coverage gaps are documented in `data_gaps.md`.
55	
56	## Cron / forward collection
57	
58	The existing live scraper (`live_scraper.py`) continues running:
59	- Daily at 21:00 (`0 21 * * *`)
60	- Monthly on the 1st at 10:00 (`0 10 1 * *`)
61	
62	That will keep adding rows for SG, MY, UK, AU each day, providing the going-forward monthly series for the index and Granger analysis. The Wayback pipeline is the historical backfill; live is the going-forward.
63

[2026-06-17T12:56:16.202Z] USER

[TOOL RESULT]
1	# UIFPI — Data limitations (paper-ready section)
2	
3	This document is written to be lifted into the limitations section of the ISEF paper. Each gap is stated with the reason it exists, what was attempted, and what proxy is in place when item-level data is unavailable.
4	
5	## Roster overview
6	
7	The Universal Informal–Formal Price Index covers nine countries: eight with item-level or restaurant-aggregate price data (US, SG, MY, UK, IN, AU, TH, ID) and one with macroeconomic proxy only (VN). This split reflects two empirical realities documented during data collection (Phase 0 coverage probe and Phase 1c parser validation):
8	
9	1. **Wayback Machine archives are not uniformly useful across platforms.** Even when a platform has thousands of archived snapshots, those archives are only useful if the prices are present in the static HTML that Wayback captures. JavaScript-rendered prices loaded after page render are not archived.
10	2. **Per-country platform availability is unequal.** Several markets (Vietnam, Philippines, Thailand) have no surviving archival source that exposes either item-level menu prices or a clean restaurant-level price proxy in static HTML.
11	
12	## Country-by-country gaps
13	
14	### Vietnam
15	- **No item-level or restaurant-aggregate price source.** Foody.vn archived URLs return 0-byte Wayback playbacks (the snapshots exist in CDX but the replay layer fails). TripAdvisor Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi pages return 0 Vietnamese-đồng (`₫`) tokens in static HTML; they expose only TripAdvisor's `priceRange` `$$/$$$/$$$$` tier markers, which are categorical, not currency.
16	- **Proxy used**: Numbeo Restaurants Price Index (annual snapshots 2018→2025), Big Mac Index (2014→2026), World Bank CPI (annual 2015→2025). All three live in the `numbeo_index`, `bigmac_index`, and `monthly_cpi` tables and form the cross-country comparison for VN.
17	- **Limitation**: VN is excluded from the formal-vs-informal pass-through regression (no informal vendor data, no item-level formal data). It is included only in the cross-country price-level comparison plot.
18	
19	### Philippines
20	- **Zomato Manila returned 0 cost-for-two extractions across 67 sampled snapshots.** The cost-for-two phrasing differs from India/Indonesia or the PHP currency token (`₱`) is missing from the static HTML. Investigation deferred.
21	- **Proxy used**: same triple (Numbeo + Big Mac + World Bank).
22	- **Limitation**: same as VN — proxy-only in the cross-country comparison; not in the pass-through regression.
23	
24	### Thailand
25	- **Eatigo Bangkok archived URLs returned 0 baht-token extractions across 69 sampled snapshots.** Eatigo's URL pattern at the CDX prefix level catches `/about-us`, `/privacy-policy`, and category index pages (`/c/{cuisine}-{id}`) rather than individual restaurant pages; even when restaurant pages were sampled, they contained no THB tokens in static HTML.
26	- **Limited fallback**: TripAdvisor Bangkok pages give `priceRange` tier markers ($, $$, $$$, $$$$) for 290 restaurant-page snapshots over 79 months. These are categorical levels, not currency amounts, and are deliberately excluded from index construction (see `index_builder.py` tier-marker exclusion).
27	- **Proxy used**: Numbeo + Big Mac + World Bank.
28	- **Limitation**: TH formal-sector index is computed from a one-day 2026-06-13 live snapshot only. Going-forward live scraping will accumulate Thai data; archival depth is not available.
29	
30	### Mexico
31	- **TripAdvisor Mexico is the only confirmed-large source (2,873 ≥2-cap restaurants).** Pages contain only restaurant-level `FoodEstablishment` metadata with `priceRange` tier markers — the JSON-LD walker correctly skips these to avoid tier-as-price contamination. 0 menu items extracted.
32	- **Proxy used**: Numbeo + Big Mac + World Bank.
33	- **Limitation**: MX is proxy-only; same status as VN, PH, TH.
34	
35	### India
36	- **Zomato India archives lack item-level prices but expose restaurant-level "cost for two".** This is a restaurant-aggregate signal: one INR data point per restaurant per archived date, representing the typical price of a meal for two people at that restaurant. It is methodologically valid as an inflation series (restaurant-level mean meal price tracked over time) but is at lower granularity than the US item-level data from MenuPages.
37	- 635 cost-for-two data points across 57 distinct Zomato NCR restaurants spanning 79 months.
38	- **Implication for analysis**: the matched-model index method in `index_builder.py` requires (restaurant, item, month) triples for cross-month item matching. With India's restaurant-aggregate data, we use `item_name = 'cost_for_two'` as a synthetic item; the matched-model thus matches *restaurants* across months rather than individual menu items. This is a deliberate methodological choice forced by source granularity, not a bug.
39	
40	### Indonesia
41	- Same data shape as India (Zomato cost-for-two, restaurant-aggregate). 29 data points across Jakarta. Smaller because Wayback's per-quarter snapshot density on `zomato.com/jakarta/*` is lower than NCR.
42	
43	### Australia
44	- **Menulog NEXT_DATA contains menu structure but mostly restaurant-level metadata, not item prices.** Validation showed 10/104 fetches yielded items; the 15 rows extracted came from a small number of restaurants whose archived NEXT_DATA happened to include offer pricing.
45	- AU also has 158 historical TripAdvisor rows (mix of tier markers and review-quoted prices) and 2 live direct chain restaurants (Oporto, Nando's Australia) collected via the going-forward live pipeline.
46	
47	### Singapore
48	- Strong item-level coverage from both **archived GrabFood** (842 rows / 12 restaurants / 8 months from Wayback NEXT_DATA) and the existing **live foodpanda + GrabFood pipeline** (~6,000 rows / 29 active restaurants). Item-level data is plentiful but the *number of distinct months* (19) is lower than IN/TH/UK/MY because the live pipeline only started in mid-2026 and Wayback's GrabFood SG coverage clusters in late 2019.
49	
50	### United Kingdom
51	- Covered by the live Deliveroo pipeline + a handful of direct chain sites. 247 distinct restaurants across 78 months — adequate for the panel.
52	
53	### United States
54	- Strongest single-platform yield: **MenuPages on Wayback**, with full Schema.org Menu / MenuSection / MenuItem markup pre-2019. 8,282 rows / 55 archived restaurants / 29 months at item granularity. US is the reference case for what archival item-level data can look like when a platform was server-rendered.
55	
56	## Floor datasets (cross-country common denominator)
57	
58	Three datasets are loaded for *every* roster country regardless of per-country archival coverage:
59	
60	- **Numbeo Restaurants Price Index** — `numbeo_index` table; 4 indicator × 8 year × 9 country grid = 288 rows. Indicators: Inexpensive Restaurant Meal, Mid-range Meal for Two, McMeal at McDonald's, Domestic Beer.
61	- **Big Mac Index** — `bigmac_index` table; 352 obs back to 2000–2026 for all 9 countries.
62	- **World Bank `FP.CPI.TOTL`** — `monthly_cpi` table; 1,052 monthly obs across 9 countries 2015–2026.
63	
64	These give the cross-country comparison its skeleton when item-level coverage is thin.
65	
66	## Known unresolved issues
67	
68	1. **Singapore tier-marker contamination** (already mitigated). The existing TripAdvisor historical pipeline stored `priceRange` tier markers as raw prices in `prices` rows with item_name beginning `'Price tier'`. The index builder filters these out in `load_price_data()` so they never contaminate the index, but they remain in raw `prices` for transparency.
69	2. **Australian Menulog `restaurant_name` collapse**: 10 yielding fetches collapsed to 1 distinct `restaurant_name` because the `_restaurant_from_url()` helper extracts the URL's last path segment, which for Menulog is often `restaurants` rather than the slug. A targeted fix would re-extract from the URL's structured slug. Not blocking the panel.
70	3. **Indonesia historical `prices` rows**: only 1 row dated 2019-09. The 152 ID total in the per-country roll-up combines that 1 row + the 29 Zomato Jakarta wayback rows + 121 from earlier TripAdvisor historical scraping (mostly tier markers, excluded from the index).
71	4. **Vietnam exclusion from item-level analysis**: stated in roster.md; reiterated here so the limitations section captures it.
72	
73	## What I would do with more time
74	
75	- Re-probe Foody.vn with `id_` raw-replay against specific timestamps to diagnose why playbacks return 0 bytes — may unlock VN item-level.
76	- Targeted Playwright fetches of dynamically-rendered chain pages (DoorDash, Swiggy, modern Zomato) to capture API responses Wayback misses.
77	- Common Crawl WARC reads as an independent archive — Phase 0 deferred this; CC may have static-rendered snapshots of pages where Wayback only captured JS shells.
78	- Manual hand-curation of ~10 high-signal historical PDF menus per country (independent restaurants, hotel restaurants, government inflation surveys with itemised price tables) — slow but high-quality.
79

1	# Historical menu-price data: what's recoverable from Wayback
2	
3	**Date**: 2026-06-16
4	**Goal**: Build enough historical UIFPI observations across 8 countries to run Granger causality against CPI. Baseline: Singapore p=0.092 (the only marginal pre-existing result).
5	
6	## TL;DR
7	
8	After exhaustive probing of Wayback Machine archives across multiple source types, **no viable archival route exists** for the price data we actually need. The only path to Granger-testable monthly menu prices is **sustained going-forward live scraping** of foodpanda / GrabFood / direct sites.
9	
10	## What was tried, what was found
11	
12	### 1. TripAdvisor restaurant reviews (Wayback)
13	Already in pipeline via `historical_scraper.py`. Today's rescan added **+1,403 rows** distributed across 32 quarter-windows × 7 countries (Singapore excluded per instruction).
14	
15	**Verdict**: data exists but is mostly unusable for index purposes. **92% of the new rows are TripAdvisor `priceRange` tier markers (`$`, `$$`, `$$$`, `$$$$`) stored as the literal integers 1–4 in the `price` column**, with `item_name = "Price tier (TripAdvisor: $$$$)"`. Only 5–34 real menu prices per country are present.
16	
17	Including tier-as-price in the index produced wildly inflated values (Indonesia's `uifpi_combined` hit **26,021,657**), and the apparent significant Granger results (MY p=0.021, ID p=0.005 under restaurant-median method) were entirely tier-bug artifacts. After excluding tier rows, every country drops below the 8-month minimum for Granger.
18	
19	Fix landed: `index_builder.py` now filters `item_name LIKE "Price tier%"` rows out of index construction. Raw rows remain in `uifpi.db` for transparency.
20	
21	### 2. Chain restaurant PDF menus (Wayback)
22	Hypothesis was that major chains publish menu PDFs on their websites with prices. Probed `*.mcdonalds.com/*.pdf`, `*.kfc.com/*.pdf`, `*.pizzahut.com/*.pdf`, plus per-country variants for 8 countries.
23	
24	**Verdict**: chains don't publish menus as PDFs. The PDFs that do exist are forms (job applications, children's-party consent forms), allergen/nutrition charts, press releases, and recipes. Wide-glob queries also frequently time out on Wayback's CDX index, but the non-timeout queries returned 0 menu PDFs.
25	
26	### 3. Chain restaurant HTML menu pages (Wayback)
27	Pivoted to archived HTML at canonical menu URLs: `mcdonalds.co.uk/menu`, `kfc.co.uk/menu`, `wagamama.com/menu`, etc. CDX confirmed 200+ snapshots for several (KFC UK, KFC AU, KFC MY, McD Indonesia, Wagamama UK).
28	
29	**Verdict**: snapshots exist but contain no price data.
30

The `index_builder.py` filter was removed as redundant. The pre-purge DB is preserved as `uifpi.db.backup_pre_tier_purge_20260617_204040`."

[2026-06-17T12:58:01.431Z] USER
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[TOOL RESULT]
1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to the UIFPI project. Dates in YYYY-MM-DD.
4	
5	## 2026-06-15 — Scraper hardening, URL audit, parallelism
6	
7	### Scraper engine (`live_scraper.py`)
8	
9	- **Parallel mode** via `UIFPI_CONCURRENCY=N`. Targets are sharded across N
10	  worker threads, each holding its own Playwright browser and SQLite
11	  connection (WAL handles concurrent writes). On the workstation: a full
12	  47-target sweep dropped from ~5h sequential to ~21 min with 4 workers.
13	- **XHR-intercept scraper** in `scrape_direct`. Captures JSON responses
14	  fetched after page load and walks them for `name`/`price` pairs. Recovers
15	  data from React-shell sites (Nando AU GraphQL → 41 items, Hoppers → 14).
16	- **Resource blocking** via `page.route` — images, fonts, CSS, and known
17	  analytics hosts are aborted. Cuts foodpanda/grabfood page loads ~40-60%.
18	- **Bot-block fast-fail**: dropped the 45s inner retry on `ACCESS_DENIED`;
19	  IP-level blocks don't lift on that timescale, so failed targets defer
20	  straight to the end-of-run retry queue.
21	- **`networkidle` → `domcontentloaded`** in `scrape_swiggy`, `scrape_js`,
22	  `scrape_direct`. `networkidle` never fires on pages with polling, so the
23	  old code timed out at 45s instead of proceeding.
24	- **Tighter waits**: foodpanda selector wait 15s × 4 → 5s × 4; GrabFood
25	  menu-render poll 30 × 1s → 12 × 500ms; inter-target sleep in parallel
26	  mode 8-18s → 3-7s.
27	- **Foodpanda 404/500 detection** in `_looks_like_block` — page returns
28	  with "404 Ooops!" / "500" title now fail fast instead of retrying.
29	- **Warm-up navigation** added: hit the home page first, set cookies, then
30	  go to the chain page (less obvious to anti-bot fingerprint).

  "new_string": "# Changelog\n\nAll notable changes to the UIFPI project. Dates in YYYY-MM-DD.\n\n## 2026-06-17 \u2014 TripAdvisor tier-marker purge, dashboard honesty pass\n\n### TripAdvisor `priceRange` tier markers removed from `prices` table\n\nThe TripAdvisor scraper historically stored `priceRange` tier strings (`$`,\n`$$`, `$$$`, `$$$$`) as integer ordinals 1\u20134 in the `price` column, with\n`item_name` beginning `\"Price tier (TripAdvisor: \u2026)\"`. They are categorical\nlevels, not currency, and were already excluded from index construction \u2014\nbut they inflated raw row counts on the dashboard tile and the country\npages, which was misleading.\n\n- **Raw table purge**: 1,648 tier-marker rows deleted across 8 countries.\n  Per-country breakdown:\n\n  | Country | Rows removed |\n  |---|---|\n  | Thailand | 279 |\n  | United Kingdom | 293 |\n  | United States | 277 |\n  | India | 237 |\n  | Australia | 221 |\n  | Malaysia | 188 |\n  | Indonesia | 118 |\n  | Singapore | 35 |\n\n  Pre-purge snapshot preserved as\n  `uifpi.db.backup_pre_tier_purge_20260617_204040`.\n- **Scraper updated** (`historical_scraper.py`): the\n  `FoodEstablishment` JSON-LD branch no longer emits tier rows (restaurant\n  name harvesting is preserved). A defensive `startswith('Price tier')`\n  guard sits in the insert loop. The `PRICE_RANGE_TIERS` /\n  `price_range_to_tier` helpers are gone.\n- **Index builder** (`index_builder.py`): the post-load `tier_mask`\n  filter has been removed as redundant; a one-line comment points to\n  this changelog entry.\n- **Audit cleanup**: removed stale `(\"price tier\", \"OTHER\")` rule from\n  `nlp_pipeline.py` and the `\"price tier\"` noise keyword from\n  `fill_manual_labels.py`. Updated `final_roster.md`, `data_gaps.md`,\n  and `docs/archival_data_findings_2026-06-16.md` to reflect that the\n  tier rows are gone, not just filtered.\n\n### Dashboard count honesty\n\n- Re-ran `index_builder.py` + `dashboard_data.py` against the post-purge\n  database. The homepage tile counts and per-country sector counts now\n  reflect **price-bearing rows only**, not raw rows.\n- **The visible drop in homepage item counts is from tier-marker\n  removal, not data loss.** Thailand: 290 \u2192 11 (the 279 tier markers\n  are gone; the 11 real currency rows from the 2026-06 Wayback\n  TripAdvisor/Wongnai THB-regex sweep remain). Indonesia: 152 \u2192 34.\n  Equivalent reductions across every country in the table above.\n- Added per-country `COVERAGE_NOTES` (Thailand, Indonesia) on the\n  country page, rendered as an amber caveat banner under the chart so\n  the visitor understands what the single TH point and the\n  restaurant-aggregate ID series actually represent.\n\n## 2026-06-15 \u2014 Scraper hardening, URL audit, parallelism"
}

## Summary

**TripAdvisor cleanup (a–e):**
- Purge was already executed in a prior session today — 1,648 rows removed across 8 countries (TH 279, UK 293, US 277, IN 237, AU 221, MY 188, ID 118, SG 35). Backup at `uifpi.db.backup_pre_tier_purge_20260617_204040`.

- `index_builder.py:162` exclusion logic removed, replaced by one-line pointer comment.
- Audit cleanup: removed dead `"price tier"` rules in `nlp_pipeline.py` and `fill_manual_labels.py`. Updated `final_roster.md`, `data_gaps.md`, `docs/archival_data_findings_2026-06-16.md` to reflect that tiers are gone, not just filtered.
- Re-ran `index_builder.py` + `dashboard_data.py`.

**Track A:**
- Dashboard count tile is now honest: TH 290 → **11**, ID 152 → **34**. (Per-country dump above.)
- Country page now renders an amber "Coverage note" banner under the chart for TH ("Single 2026-06 snapshot … live collection going forward only") and ID ("Restaurant-aggregate Zomato cost-for-two series … treat each point as a typical-meal-for-two price"). Wired via a new `COVERAGE_NOTES` map in `dashboard/types/index.ts`.
- TH single-point rendering: Recharts already plots the lone 2026-06 dot — no chart code change needed.

**CHANGELOG:** Appended `2026-06-17` section noting the homepage count drop is from tier-marker removal, not data loss. (No separate `CHANGES.md` created — the repo uses `CHANGELOG.md`.)

**Not started (per your "start with Track A + TripAdvisor" gate):** Track B (AU Menulog → ID GoFood → TH GrabFood scrapes), Track C (Phase 0 probes for BR/DE/ZA + Mexico floor-only page). Awaiting your go-ahead after you review the dashboard.

Nothing is committed yet — `historical_scraper.py`, `index_builder.py`, the dashboard TSX, the docs, `CHANGELOG.md`, `uifpi.db`, `uifpi_index.csv`, and the regenerated JSON in `dashboard_data/` + `dashboard/public/data/` are all in your working tree. 60	| Vietnam | foody HCMC | 5,387 | 0 | ✗ |
61	
62	## Reading the matrix
63	
64	- `≥2-cap restaurants` = distinct archived URLs with two or more captures inside the window. This is the gating metric for the formal-sector roster (≥15 required).
65	- `Sample $-hits` = currency-token count in ONE randomly-chosen archived page. Low or zero hits mean even though Wayback has captures, the captured HTML doesn't contain prices — usually because the site is/was a JS shell that loaded prices via API.
66	- `LD` / `ND` = whether the sample HTML has JSON-LD or `__NEXT_DATA__`; presence of either gives a clean structured data path that's usually more reliable than DOM regex.
67	- A country clears the formal threshold only when BOTH ≥2-cap ≥ 15 **and** sample $-hits ≥ 5.
68	- Common Crawl was not probed in this pass. It is a secondary archive available for Phase 1 if Wayback gaps are narrow.
69

[2026-06-17T13:05:56.750Z] USER
