[TOOL RESULT]
1	"""
2	Always-on floor datasets: load the unconditional macro / cross-country
3	benchmarks for every roster country (and Vietnam as proxy-only).
4	
5	Sources:
6	  1. World Bank API     - FP.CPI.TOTL (annual CPI, 2018-present)
7	  2. Big Mac Index      - The Economist's monthly CSV on GitHub
8	  3. Numbeo             - 'Restaurants Price Index' yearly snapshots (2018+)
9	                          and 'Inexpensive Restaurant Meal' price rankings
10	
11	Tables created/used:
12	  monthly_cpi      (existing) — World Bank annual CPI back-filled where sparse
13	  bigmac_index     (new)      — country, date, dollar_price, ...
14	  numbeo_index     (new)      — country, year, indicator, value, source_url
15	
16	Idempotent: each run upserts on its natural key. Country roster:
17	  US, IN, ID, TH, AU, PH, SG, MX  (formal roster)
18	  VN                              (proxy-only)
19	"""
20	import csv
21	import io
22	import json
23	import os
24	import re
25	import sqlite3
26	import time
27	from datetime import datetime
28	
29	import requests
30	from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
31	
32	BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
33	DB   = os.path.join(BASE, 'uifpi.db')
34	HDR  = {'User-Agent': 'UIFPI-research-pipeline (academic; contact via repo issues)'}
35	
36	ROSTER = [
37	    # (country, iso2, iso3, currency)
38	    ('United States', 'US', 'USA', 'USD'),
39	    ('India',         'IN', 'IND', 'INR'),
40	    ('Indonesia',     'ID', 'IDN', 'IDR'),
41	    ('Thailand',      'TH', 'THA', 'THB'),
42	    ('Australia',     'AU', 'AUS', 'AUD'),
43	    ('Philippines',   'PH', 'PHL', 'PHP'),
44	    ('Singapore',     'SG', 'SGP', 'SGD'),
45	    ('Mexico',        'MX', 'MEX', 'MXN'),
46	    ('Vietnam',       'VN', 'VNM', 'VND'),  # proxy-only
47	]
48	
49	# Numbeo "items" we want (their item-id integers from the URL):
50	# 1 = inexpensive restaurant meal (informal proxy)
51	# 2 = mid-range meal for two (formal proxy)
52	# Combined indices live on the rankings page via displayColumn= param;
53	# 4 = Restaurants Price Index, 8 = Local Purchasing Power Index
54	NUMBEO_ITEMS = [
55	    (1, 'Meal, Inexpensive Restaurant'),
56	    (2, 'Meal for 2 People, Mid-range Restaurant, Three-course'),
57	    (4, 'McMeal at McDonalds (or Equivalent Combo Meal)'),
58	    (5, 'Domestic Beer (0.5 liter draught)'),
59	]
60	NUMBEO_RANKING_COLS = [
61	    (4, 'Restaurants Price Index'),
62	    (5, 'Restaurant Price Index + Groceries Price Index'),
63	]
64	
65	
66	# ── Schema ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67	
68	def init_schema(conn):
69	    c = conn.cursor()
70	    c.execute("""
71	        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bigmac_index (
72	            id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
73	            iso3          TEXT NOT NULL,
74	            country       TEXT,
75	            date          TEXT NOT NULL,
76	            local_price   REAL,
77	            dollar_ex     REAL,
78	            dollar_price  REAL,
79	            source        TEXT DEFAULT 'economist',
80	            UNIQUE(iso3, date)
81	        )
82	    """)
83	    c.execute("""
84	        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS numbeo_index (
85	            id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
86	            country      TEXT NOT NULL,
87	            iso2         TEXT,
88	            year         INTEGER NOT NULL,
89	            indicator    TEXT NOT NULL,
90	            indicator_id INTEGER,
91	            value        REAL,
92	            currency     TEXT,
93	            source_url   TEXT,
94	            UNIQUE(country, year, indicator)
95	        )
96	    """)
97	    conn.commit()
98	
99	
100	# ── World Bank ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
101	
102	def load_worldbank_cpi(conn):
103	    """Backfill monthly_cpi with annual CPI from World Bank for any country
104	    whose existing series is < 8 years long."""
105	    print("\n=== World Bank CPI (FP.CPI.TOTL) ===")
106	    c = conn.cursor()
107	    loaded = 0
108	    for country, iso2, iso3, _cur in ROSTER:
109	        # How many years of monthly data do we already have?
110	        existing = c.execute(
111	            "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT substr(year_month,1,4)) FROM monthly_cpi "
112	            "WHERE country_code = ?", (iso2,)
113	        ).fetchone()[0]
114	        if existing >= 8:
115	            print(f"  {country:<14} {iso2}  already has {existing} years — skip")
116	            continue
117	        # Fetch annual series 2015-2025
118	        url = (f'https://api.worldbank.org/v2/country/{iso3}/indicator/'
119	               f'FP.CPI.TOTL?format=json&date=2015:2025&per_page=200')
120	        try:
121	            r = requests.get(url, headers=HDR, timeout=30)
122	            r.raise_for_status()
123	            data = r.json()
124	            if not isinstance(data, list) or len(data) < 2:
125	                print(f"  {country:<14} {iso2}  unexpected payload — skip")
126	                continue
127	            rows = [d for d in data[1] if d.get('value') is not None]
128	            if not rows:
129	                print(f"  {country:<14} {iso2}  no values — skip")
130	                continue
131	            # Each annual value: expand to 12 monthly rows tagged as annual_interp
132	            n_before = c.execute(
133	                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM monthly_cpi WHERE country_code = ?",
134	                (iso2,)
135	            ).fetchone()[0]
136	            for d in rows:
137	                year = int(d['date'])
138	                val = float(d['value'])
139	                for m in range(1, 13):
140	                    ym = f'{year:04d}-{m:02d}'
141	                    c.execute(
142	                        "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO monthly_cpi "
143	                        "(country_code, year, month, year_month, cpi_value, "
144	                        " source, date_downloaded) VALUES "
145	                        "(?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
146	                        (iso2, year, m, ym, val, 'worldbank_annual',
147	                         datetime.now().isoformat()[:19])
148	                    )
149	            conn.commit()
150	            n_after = c.execute(
151	                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM monthly_cpi WHERE country_code = ?",
152	                (iso2,)
153	            ).fetchone()[0]
154	            added = n_after - n_before
155	            loaded += added
156	            print(f"  {country:<14} {iso2}  +{added} rows ({len(rows)} years)")
157	        except Exception as e:
158	            print(f"  {country:<14} {iso2}  ERR {str(e)[:60]}")
159	        time.sleep(1.0)
160	    print(f"  World Bank: {loaded} new rows inserted")
161	
162	
163	# ── Big Mac Index ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
164	
165	BIGMAC_URL = ('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TheEconomist/big-mac-data/'
166	              'master/output-data/big-mac-full-index.csv')
167	
168	def load_bigmac(conn):
169	    print("\n=== Big Mac index (Economist) ===")
170	    iso3s = {r[2] for r in ROSTER}
171	    try:
172	        r = requests.get(BIGMAC_URL, headers=HDR, timeout=60)
173	        r.raise_for_status()
174	    except Exception as e:
175	        print(f"  fetch failed: {e}")
176	        return
177	    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(r.text))
178	    c = conn.cursor()
179	    n = 0
180	    for row in reader:
181	        iso3 = (row.get('iso_a3') or '').upper()
182	        if iso3 not in iso3s:
183	            continue
184	        try:
185	            date = row.get('date', '')[:10]
186	            local = float(row['local_price']) if row.get('local_price') else None
187	            dx    = float(row['dollar_ex']) if row.get('dollar_ex') else None
188	            dp    = float(row['dollar_price']) if row.get('dollar_price') else None
189	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
190	            continue
191	        country = row.get('name') or row.get('country', '')
192	        c.execute(
193	            "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO bigmac_index "
194	            "(iso3, country, date, local_price, dollar_ex, dollar_price, source) "
195	            "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'economist')",
196	            (iso3, country, date, local, dx, dp)
197	        )
198	        n += 1
199	    conn.commit()
200	    print(f"  Big Mac: {n} rows inserted/refreshed across {len(iso3s)} countries")
201	    # Show per-country coverage
202	    for country, iso2, iso3, _cur in ROSTER:
203	        rng = c.execute(
204	            "SELECT MIN(date), MAX(date), COUNT(*) FROM bigmac_index "
205	            "WHERE iso3 = ?", (iso3,)
206	        ).fetchone()
207	        print(f"    {country:<14} {iso3}  {rng[2]:>3} obs  {rng[0]} → {rng[1]}")
208	
209	
210	# ── Numbeo ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
211	
212	NUMBEO_BASE = 'https://www.numbeo.com'
213	
214	def load_numbeo(conn):
215	    """Load Numbeo per-item country rankings for each year 2018-2025 and
216	    each item in NUMBEO_ITEMS. Numbeo's rankings page is country × item ×
217	    year — perfect for cross-country panel data."""
218	    print("\n=== Numbeo item rankings ===")
219	    c = conn.cursor()
220	    target_countries = {r[0]: (r[1], r[3]) for r in ROSTER}
221	    n_total = 0
222	    for year in range(2018, 2026):
223	        for item_id, item_name in NUMBEO_ITEMS:
224	            url = (f'{NUMBEO_BASE}/cost-of-living/country_price_rankings'
225	                   f'?itemId={item_id}&title={year}')
226	            try:
227	                r = requests.get(url, headers=HDR, timeout=30)
228	                if r.status_code != 200:
229	                    print(f"  {year} item {item_id}  HTTP {r.status_code}")
230	                    time.sleep(1.5); continue
231	            except Exception as e:
232	                print(f"  {year} item {item_id}  ERR {str(e)[:50]}")
233	                time.sleep(2.0); continue
234	            soup = BeautifulSoup(r.text, 'html.parser')
235	            # Numbeo rankings page has no table headers — the data table is
236	            # just the one with the most <tr> rows (~99 countries).
237	            tables = soup.find_all('table')
238	            if not tables:
239	                print(f"  {year} item {item_id}  no tables on page")
240	                time.sleep(1.0); continue
241	            table = max(tables, key=lambda t: len(t.find_all('tr')))
242	            n_year = 0
243	            for tr in table.find_all('tr'):
244	                tds = tr.find_all('td')
245	                if len(tds) < 4:
246	                    continue
247	                # Layout: [rank, country, flag/empty, price]
248	                country_cell = tds[1].get_text(strip=True)
249	                price_cell   = tds[3].get_text(strip=True)
250	                if country_cell not in target_countries:
251	                    continue
252	                iso2, cur = target_countries[country_cell]
253	                # Numbeo writes "12.50 $" / "1,234.56 ₹" — pull the number
254	                m = re.search(r'([\d.,]+)', price_cell.replace(',', ''))
255	                if not m:
256	                    continue
257	                try:
258	                    val = float(m.group(1))
259	                except ValueError:
260	                    continue
261	                c.execute(
262	                    "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO numbeo_index "
263	                    "(country, iso2, year, indicator, indicator_id, value, "
264	                    " currency, source_url) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
265	                    (country_cell, iso2, year, item_name, item_id,
266	                     val, 'USD', url)
267	                )
268	                n_year += 1
269	                n_total += 1
270	            conn.commit()
271	            print(f"  {year} item {item_id:<2} '{item_name[:40]:<40}'  +{n_year}")
272	            time.sleep(1.5)
273	    print(f"  Numbeo total rows: {n_total}")
274	
275	
276	def main():
277	    print("Floor datasets loader")
278	    print(f"Roster: {', '.join(r[0] for r in ROSTER)}")
279	    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB)
280	    init_schema(conn)
281	    load_worldbank_cpi(conn)
282	    load_bigmac(conn)
283	    load_numbeo(conn)
284	    conn.close()
285	    print("\nDone.")
286	
287	
288	if __name__ == '__main__':
289	    main()
290

1	"""
2	Phase 0 — Wayback CDX coverage probe across (country, platform) pairs.
3	
4	For each pair, queries Wayback CDX with a URL pattern that matches the
5	platform's actual URL convention, then samples one archived page to check
6	whether prices are present in the static HTML. Output: coverage_report.csv
7	and coverage_report.md.
8	
9	This is the decision gate. We do NOT bulk-scrape pages here — only enough
10	sampling to confirm prices are extractable. Stop and report after Phase 0.
11	
12	Run:  python3 phase0_coverage_probe.py
13	"""
14	import csv
15	import json
16	import os
17	import random
18	import re
19	import sys
20	import time
21	from collections import defaultdict
22	from datetime import datetime
23	
24	import requests
25	
26	BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
27	CDX  = 'http://web.archive.org/cdx/search/cdx'
28	WBM  = 'https://web.archive.org/web'
29	HDR  = {
30	    'User-Agent': 'UIFPI-research-probe (academic; contact via repo issues)'
31	}
32	WINDOW_FROM = '20180101'
33	WINDOW_TO   = '20260601'
34	
35	# Per-probe knobs. Wayback rate-limits aggressive callers; be polite.
36	CDX_TIMEOUT       = 90
37	CDX_LIMIT         = 30000
38	CDX_DELAY         = 4.0
39	CDX_RETRIES       = 2
40	CDX_RETRY_BACKOFF = 15
41	FETCH_TIMEOUT     = 45
42	FETCH_DELAY       = 2.5
43	
44	# (country, sector_hint, platform_label, cdx_url_pattern, currency_re_label)
45	# Patterns use the trailing-wildcard convention (no matchType param);
46	# this matches the working style from historical_scraper.py.
47	PROBES = [
48	    # US — formal-sector listings & menu indexes
49	    ('United States', 'formal', 'allmenus.com',     'allmenus.com/*',          'USD'),
50	    ('United States', 'formal', 'menupages.com',    'menupages.com/*',         'USD'),
51	    ('United States', 'formal', 'yelp.com/biz NYC', 'yelp.com/biz/*new-york*', 'USD'),
52	    ('United States', 'formal', 'yelp.com/menu',    'yelp.com/menu/*',         'USD'),
53	    # India
54	    ('India',         'formal', 'zomato Mumbai',    'zomato.com/mumbai/*',     'INR'),
55	    ('India',         'formal', 'zomato NCR',       'zomato.com/ncr/*',        'INR'),
56	    ('India',         'formal', 'swiggy Mumbai',    'swiggy.com/mumbai/*',     'INR'),
57	    ('India',         'formal', 'swiggy Bangalore', 'swiggy.com/bangalore/*',  'INR'),
58	    ('India',         'formal', 'burrp Mumbai',     'burrp.com/mumbai/*',      'INR'),
59	    ('India',         'formal', 'dineout.co.in',    'dineout.co.in/*',         'INR'),
60	    # Indonesia
61	    ('Indonesia',     'formal', 'zomato Jakarta',   'zomato.com/jakarta/*',    'IDR'),
62	    ('Indonesia',     'formal', 'qraved Jakarta',   'qraved.com/jakarta/*',    'IDR'),
63	    ('Indonesia',     'formal', 'pergikuliner',     'pergikuliner.com/restaurants/*', 'IDR'),
64	    # Thailand
65	    ('Thailand',      'formal', 'wongnai BKK',      'wongnai.com/bangkok/*',   'THB'),
66	    ('Thailand',      'formal', 'wongnai restaurants','wongnai.com/restaurants/*', 'THB'),
67	    ('Thailand',      'formal', 'eatigo BKK',       'eatigo.com/th/bangkok/*', 'THB'),
68	    # Australia
69	    ('Australia',     'formal', 'zomato Sydney',    'zomato.com/sydney/*',     'AUD'),
70	    ('Australia',     'formal', 'zomato Melbourne', 'zomato.com/melbourne/*',  'AUD'),
71	    ('Australia',     'formal', 'urbanspoon',       'urbanspoon.com/n/*',      'AUD'),
72	    ('Australia',     'formal', 'menulog',          'menulog.com.au/restaurants/*', 'AUD'),
73	    # Vietnam
74	    ('Vietnam',       'formal', 'foody HCMC',       'foody.vn/ho-chi-minh/*',  'VND'),
75	    # Philippines
76	    ('Philippines',   'formal', 'zomato Manila',    'zomato.com/manila/*',     'PHP'),
77	    # Malaysia
78	    ('Malaysia',      'formal', 'zomato KL',        'zomato.com/kuala-lumpur/*', 'MYR'),
79	    # Singapore
80	    ('Singapore',     'formal', 'hungrygowhere',    'hungrygowhere.com/dining-guide/restaurants/*', 'SGD'),
81	    ('Singapore',     'formal', 'foodpanda SG',     'foodpanda.sg/restaurant/*', 'SGD'),
82	    ('Singapore',     'formal', 'food.grab SG',     'food.grab.com/sg/en/restaurant/*', 'SGD'),
83	    # Mexico
84	    ('Mexico',        'formal', 'tripadvisor MX',   'tripadvisor.com.mx/Restaurant_Review*', 'MXN'),
85	]
86	
87	# Currency-detection regex (used on sampled archived HTML)
88	CURRENCY_REGEXES = {
89	    'USD': re.compile(r'\$\s?\d+(?:\.\d{2})?'),
90	    'GBP': re.compile(r'£\s?\d+(?:\.\d{2})?'),
91	    'INR': re.compile(r'(?:₹|Rs\.?)\s?\d+(?:[.,]\d{2})?'),
92	    'IDR': re.compile(r'Rp\.?\s?\d+(?:[.,]\d{3})*'),
93	    'THB': re.compile(r'(?:฿|THB)\s?\d+(?:\.\d{2})?'),
94	    'AUD': re.compile(r'A?\$\s?\d+(?:\.\d{2})?'),
95	    'SGD': re.compile(r'S\$\s?\d+(?:\.\d{2})?'),
96	    'MYR': re.compile(r'RM\s?\d+(?:\.\d{2})?'),
97	    'PHP': re.compile(r'(?:₱|PHP)\s?\d+(?:\.\d{2})?'),
98	    'VND': re.compile(r'\d+(?:\.\d{3})*\s?₫|\d+(?:\.\d{3})*\s?VND'),
99	    'MXN': re.compile(r'\$\s?\d+(?:\.\d{2})?'),
100	}
101	
102	
103	def query_cdx(pattern):
104	    """One CDX query with retries. Returns (rows, error_or_None)."""
105	    params = {
106	        'url':    pattern,
107	        'from':   WINDOW_FROM,
108	        'to':     WINDOW_TO,
109	        'output': 'json',
110	        'fl':     'timestamp,original',
111	        'filter': ['statuscode:200', 'mimetype:text/html'],
112	        'limit':  CDX_LIMIT,
113	    }
114	    last_err = None
115	    for attempt in range(CDX_RETRIES + 1):
116	        try:
117	            r = requests.get(CDX, params=params, headers=HDR, timeout=CDX_TIMEOUT)
118	            if r.status_code != 200:
119	                last_err = f'HTTP {r.status_code}'
120	                if r.status_code in (429, 503, 504) and attempt < CDX_RETRIES:
121	                    time.sleep(CDX_RETRY_BACKOFF)
122	                    continue
123	                return None, last_err
124	            data = r.json()
125	            if not data or len(data) < 2:
126	                return [], None
127	            return [(row[0], row[1]) for row in data[1:]], None
128	        except Exception as e:
129	            last_err = str(e)[:80]
130	            if attempt < CDX_RETRIES:
131	                time.sleep(CDX_RETRY_BACKOFF)
132	    return None, last_err
133	
134	
135	def sample_page_for_prices(timestamp, orig_url, currency_re):
136	    """Fetch one archived page with id_ suffix and count price matches."""
137	    url = f"{WBM}/{timestamp}id_/{orig_url}"
138	    try:
139	        r = requests.get(url, headers=HDR, timeout=FETCH_TIMEOUT)
140	        if r.status_code != 200:
141	            return None, f'HTTP {r.status_code}'
142	        text = r.text
143	        n_price = len(currency_re.findall(text))
144	        has_ld  = 'application/ld+json' in text
145	        has_nd  = '__NEXT_DATA__' in text
146	        bytes_kb = len(r.content) // 1024
147	        return {
148	            'kb': bytes_kb, 'price_hits': n_price,
149	            'json_ld': has_ld, 'next_data': has_nd,
150	        }, None
151	    except Exception as e:
152	        return None, str(e)[:60]
153	
154	
155	def main():
156	    print(f"Phase 0 coverage probe — {len(PROBES)} (country, platform) pairs")
157	    print(f"Window: {WINDOW_FROM} → {WINDOW_TO}")
158	    print(f"CDX limit {CDX_LIMIT}, timeout {CDX_TIMEOUT}s, delay {CDX_DELAY}s, "
159	          f"retries {CDX_RETRIES}\n")
160	
161	    results = []
162	    rnd = random.Random(20260616)
163	
164	    for i, (country, sector, platform, pat, cur) in enumerate(PROBES, 1):
165	        print(f"[{i:>2}/{len(PROBES)}] {country:<14} {platform:<22} {pat}")
166	        rows, err = query_cdx(pat)
167	
168	        rec = {
169	            'country': country, 'sector': sector, 'platform': platform,
170	            'pattern': pat, 'currency': cur,
171	            'n_restaurants_ge2': 0, 'n_snapshots': 0,
172	            'distinct_urls': 0, 'earliest': '', 'latest': '',
173	            'cdx_status': '',
174	            'sample_url': '', 'sample_ts': '',
175	            'sample_kb': '', 'sample_price_hits': '',
176	            'sample_json_ld': '', 'sample_next_data': '',
177	            'sample_status': '',
178	        }
179	
180	        if err:
181	            print(f"          CDX ERROR: {err}")
182	            rec['cdx_status'] = f'error:{err}'
183	            results.append(rec)
184	            time.sleep(CDX_DELAY)
185	            continue
186	
187	        if not rows:
188	            print(f"          0 rows")
189	            rec['cdx_status'] = 'empty'
190	            results.append(rec)
191	            time.sleep(CDX_DELAY)
192	            continue
193	
194	        per_url = defaultdict(list)
195	        for ts, orig in rows:
196	            per_url[orig].append(ts)
197	        ge2 = [u for u, ts_list in per_url.items() if len(ts_list) >= 2]
198	        all_ts = sorted(ts for ts_list in per_url.values() for ts in ts_list)
199	        rec.update({
200	            'n_restaurants_ge2': len(ge2),
201	            'n_snapshots': len(rows),
202	            'distinct_urls': len(per_url),
203	            'earliest': all_ts[0][:8] if all_ts else '',
204	            'latest':   all_ts[-1][:8] if all_ts else '',
205	            'cdx_status': 'truncated' if len(rows) >= CDX_LIMIT else 'ok',
206	        })
207	        trunc = ' (TRUNCATED)' if len(rows) >= CDX_LIMIT else ''
208	        print(f"          {len(per_url):>6} distinct URLs, {len(ge2):>5} ≥2-cap, "
209	              f"{len(rows):>6} snapshots, {rec['earliest']}-{rec['latest']}{trunc}")
210	
211	        # Sample ONE archived page from a ≥2-capture URL (or any URL if none)
212	        # to confirm prices are present in the static HTML.
213	        candidates = ge2 if ge2 else list(per_url.keys())
214	        sample_url = rnd.choice(candidates)
215	        sample_ts  = per_url[sample_url][len(per_url[sample_url]) // 2]
216	        cre = CURRENCY_REGEXES.get(cur, CURRENCY_REGEXES['USD'])
217	        time.sleep(FETCH_DELAY)
218	        print(f"          sampling {sample_ts} {sample_url[:70]}")
219	        s, ferr = sample_page_for_prices(sample_ts, sample_url, cre)
220	        rec['sample_url'] = sample_url
221	        rec['sample_ts']  = sample_ts
222	        if ferr:
223	            rec['sample_status'] = f'error:{ferr}'
224	            print(f"          sample fetch failed: {ferr}")
225	        else:
226	            rec.update({
227	                'sample_kb': s['kb'],
228	                'sample_price_hits': s['price_hits'],
229	                'sample_json_ld': 'Y' if s['json_ld'] else 'N',
230	                'sample_next_data': 'Y' if s['next_data'] else 'N',
231	                'sample_status': 'ok',
232	            })
233	            print(f"          sample: {s['kb']} KB, {s['price_hits']} {cur} hits, "
234	                  f"LD={s['json_ld']} ND={s['next_data']}")
235	
236	        results.append(rec)
237	        time.sleep(CDX_DELAY)
238	
239	    # CSV
240	    csv_path = os.path.join(BASE, 'coverage_report.csv')
241	    fieldnames = ['country', 'sector', 'platform', 'pattern', 'currency',
242	                  'n_restaurants_ge2', 'n_snapshots', 'distinct_urls',
243	                  'earliest', 'latest', 'cdx_status',
244	                  'sample_url', 'sample_ts', 'sample_kb',
245	                  'sample_price_hits', 'sample_json_ld', 'sample_next_data',
246	                  'sample_status']
247	    with open(csv_path, 'w', newline='') as fh:
248	        w = csv.DictWriter(fh, fieldnames=fieldnames)
249	        w.writeheader()
250	        w.writerows(results)
251	    print(f"\nCSV → {csv_path}")
252	
253	    # Markdown
254	    md_path = os.path.join(BASE, 'coverage_report.md')
255	    with open(md_path, 'w') as fh:
256	        fh.write(f"# Phase 0 — Wayback CDX coverage matrix\n\n")
257	        fh.write(f"Generated: {datetime.now().isoformat()}\n  \n")
258	        fh.write(f"Window: {WINDOW_FROM} → {WINDOW_TO}\n  \n")
259	        fh.write(f"CDX limit per query: {CDX_LIMIT:,} (rows ≥ limit = truncated).\n\n")
260	        fh.write("Each row probes one (country, platform) pair: counts distinct\n"
261	                 "archived URLs and snapshots in-window, then fetches one sample\n"
262	                 "archived page (id_ raw, no Wayback toolbar) and counts currency-\n"
263	                 "shaped price tokens in the static HTML — i.e., the data we'd\n"
264	                 "actually be able to extract without re-running JavaScript.\n\n")
265	        fh.write("## Matrix\n\n")
266	        fh.write("| Country | Platform | URL pattern | ≥2-cap restaurants | "
267	                 "Snapshots | Range | Sample KB | Sample $-hits | LD | ND | Notes |\n")
268	        fh.write("|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|---:|---:|:---:|:---:|---|\n")
269	        for r in results:
270	            rng = f"{r['earliest']}–{r['latest']}" if r['earliest'] else '—'
271	            notes = r['cdx_status']
272	            if r['sample_status'] and r['sample_status'] != 'ok':
273	                notes += '; sample ' + r['sample_status']
274	            fh.write(
275	                f"| {r['country']} | {r['platform']} | `{r['pattern']}` | "
276	                f"{r['n_restaurants_ge2']:,} | {r['n_snapshots']:,} | {rng} | "
277	                f"{r['sample_kb']} | {r['sample_price_hits']} | "
278	                f"{r['sample_json_ld']} | {r['sample_next_data']} | {notes} |\n"
279	            )
280	
281	        fh.write("\n## Country roll-up (best platform per country)\n\n")
282	        roll = {}
283	        for r in results:
284	            c = r['country']
285	            best = roll.get(c)
286	            if (best is None or
287	                (r['n_restaurants_ge2'], r['sample_price_hits'] or 0) >
288	                (best['n_restaurants_ge2'], best['sample_price_hits'] or 0)):
289	                roll[c] = r
290	        fh.write("| Country | Best platform | ≥2-cap restaurants | "
291	                 "Sample $-hits | Clears formal threshold (≥15 + prices visible)? |\n")
292	        fh.write("|---|---|---:|---:|---|\n")
293	        for c in sorted(roll):
294	            r = roll[c]
295	            ok = ('✓' if r['n_restaurants_ge2'] >= 15 and
296	                  (r['sample_price_hits'] or 0) >= 5 else '✗')
297	            fh.write(f"| {c} | {r['platform']} | {r['n_restaurants_ge2']:,} | "
298	                     f"{r['sample_price_hits']} | {ok} |\n")
299	
300	        fh.write("\n## Reading the matrix\n\n")
301	        fh.write("- `≥2-cap restaurants` = distinct archived URLs with two or "
302	                 "more captures inside the window. This is the gating metric "
303	                 "for the formal-sector roster (≥15 required).\n")
304	        fh.write("- `Sample $-hits` = currency-token count in ONE randomly-chosen "
305	                 "archived page. Low or zero hits mean even though Wayback has "
306	                 "captures, the captured HTML doesn't contain prices — usually "
307	                 "because the site is/was a JS shell that loaded prices via API.\n")
308	        fh.write("- `LD` / `ND` = whether the sample HTML has JSON-LD or "
309	                 "`__NEXT_DATA__`; presence of either gives a clean structured "
310	                 "data path that's usually more reliable than DOM regex.\n")
311	        fh.write("- A country clears the formal threshold only when BOTH "
312	                 "≥2-cap ≥ 15 **and** sample $-hits ≥ 5.\n")
313	        fh.write("- Common Crawl was not probed in this pass. It is a secondary "
314	                 "archive available for Phase 1 if Wayback gaps are narrow.\n")
315	    print(f"MD  → {md_path}")
316	    print(f"\nDone. Inspect coverage_report.md before any collection.")
317	
318	
319	if __name__ == '__main__':
320	    main()
321

'USD': re.compile(r'\\$\\s?\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})?'),\n}\n\n\ndef query_cdx(pattern):\n    params = {\n        'url':    pattern,\n        'from':   WINDOW_FROM,\n        'to':     WINDOW_TO,\n        'output': 'json',\n        'fl':     'timestamp,original',\n        'filter': ['statuscode:200', 'mimetype:text/html'],\n        'limit':  CDX_LIMIT,\n    }\n    last_err = None\n    for attempt in range(CDX_RETRIES + 1):\n        try:\n            r = requests.get(CDX, params=params, headers=HDR, timeout=CDX_TIMEOUT)\n            if r.status_code != 200:\n                last_err = f'HTTP {r.status_code}'\n                if r.status_code in (429, 503, 504) and attempt < CDX_RETRIES:\n                    time.sleep(CDX_RETRY_BACKOFF)\n                    continue\n                return None, last_err\n            data = r.json()\n            if not data or len(data) < 2:\n                return [], None\n            return [(row[0], row[1]) for row in data[1:]], None\n        except Exception as e:\n            last_err = str(e)[:80]\n            if attempt < CDX_RETRIES:\n                time.sleep(CDX_RETRY_BACKOFF)\n    return None, last_err\n\n\ndef sample_page_for_prices(timestamp, orig_url, currency_re):\n    url = f\"{WBM}/{timestamp}id_/{orig_url}\"\n    try:\n        r = requests.get(url, headers=HDR, timeout=FETCH_TIMEOUT)\n        if r.status_code != 200:\n            return None, f'HTTP {r.status_code}'\n        text = r.text\n        n_price = len(currency_re.findall(text))\n        has_ld  = 'application/ld+json' in text\n        has_nd  = '__NEXT_DATA__' in text\n        bytes_kb = len(r.content) // 1024\n        return {'kb': bytes_kb, 'price_hits': n_price,\n                'json_ld': has_ld, 'next_data': has_nd}, None\n {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "~/Inflation-menu/dashboard_data.py",
  "old_string": "def write_json(obj: Union[dict, list], path: str) -> None:",
  "new_string": "def build_floor_data(db_path: str = DB_PATH) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Per-proxy-country export of Numbeo, Big Mac, and World Bank CPI\n    series. Consumed by the dashboard's proxy-country page (Mexico).\n\n    Schema:\n    {\n      \"Mexico\": {\n        \"iso2\":     \"MX\",\n        \"currency\": \"MXN\",\n        \"numbeo_inexpensive\": [{\"year\": 2018, \"value\": 8.42}, ...],\n        \"numbeo_midrange\":    [{\"year\": 2018, \"value\": 30.10}, ...],\n        \"bigmac_usd\":         [{\"year\": \"2018-01-01\", \"value\": 2.50}, ...],\n        \"wb_cpi\":             [{\"year\": \"2018-01\", \"value\": 102.1}, ...]\n      }\n    }\n    \"\"\"\n    out: dict = {}\n    if not os.path.exists(db_path):\n        return out\n    try:\n        conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)\n        cur = conn.cursor()\n        for country, iso2, iso3, currency in PROXY_COUNTRIES:\n            # Numbeo: 'Meal, Inexpensive Restaurant' & 'Meal for 2 People...'\n            # Values are USD-normalised in the table; the dashboard renders\n            # them as USD-equivalent local-currency proxies.\n            inexp = cur.execute(\n                \"SELECT year, value FROM numbeo_index \"\n                \"WHERE iso2 = ? AND indicator LIKE 'Meal, Inexpensive%' \"\n                \"ORDER BY year\",\n                (iso2,)\n            ).fetchall()\n            midrange = cur.execute(\n                \"SELECT year, value FROM numbeo_index \"\n                \"WHERE iso2 = ? AND indicator LIKE 'Meal for 2 People%' \"\n                \"ORDER BY year\",\n                (iso2,)\n            ).fetchall()\n            bigmac = cur.execute(\n                \"SELECT date, dollar_price FROM bigmac_index \"\n                \"WHERE iso3 = ? AND dollar_price IS NOT NULL \"\n                \"ORDER BY date\",\n                (iso3,)\n            ).fetchall()\n            cpi = cur.execute(\n                \"SELECT year_month, cpi_value FROM monthly_cpi \"\n                \"WHERE country_code = ? AND cpi_value IS NOT NULL \"\n                \"ORDER BY year_month\",\n                (iso2,)\n            ).fetchall()\n            out[country] = {\n                \"iso2\":     iso2,\n                \"currency\": currency,\n                \"numbeo_inexpensive\": [\n                    {\"year\": y, \"value\": safe_round(v, 2)} for y, v in inexp\n                ],\n                \"numbeo_midrange\": [\n                    {\"year\": y, \"value\": safe_round(v, 2)} for y, v in midrange\n                ],\n                \"bigmac_usd\": [\n                    {\"year\": d, \"value\": safe_round(v, 2)} for d, v in bigmac\n                ],\n                \"wb_cpi\": [\n                    {\"year\": ym, \"value\": safe_round(v, 1)} for ym, v in cpi\n                ],\n            }\n        conn.close()\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"  \u26a0  floor-data query failed: {e}\")\n    return out\n\n\ndef write_json(obj: Union[dict, list], path: str) -> None:"
}

1	import Link from "next/link";
2	import { notFound, permanentRedirect } from "next/navigation";
3	import { getCountryData, getIndexSeries, seriesToCsv } from "@/lib/data";
4	import IndexChart from "@/components/IndexChart";
5	import StatCard from "@/components/StatCard";
6	import {
7	  SLUG_TO_COUNTRY,
8	  COUNTRY_FLAGS,
9	  COUNTRY_SLUGS,
10	  COVERAGE_NOTES,
11	  DEVELOPMENT_STATUS,
12	  COUNTRIES,
13	} from "@/types";
14	import type { Metadata } from "next";
15	
16	interface PageProps {
17	  params: Promise<{ country: string }>;
18	}
19	
20	export async function generateStaticParams() {
21	  return Object.values(COUNTRY_SLUGS).map((slug) => ({ country: slug }));
22	}
23	
24	/**
25	 * Resolve a raw URL segment to a canonical lowercase slug + country name.
26	 * Accepts: "singapore", "Singapore", "SINGAPORE", "Singapore%20", "united_states",
27	 * "United%20States", "united-states", etc.
28	 * Returns null if the segment cannot be matched.
29	 */
30	function resolveSlug(raw: string): { slug: string; country: string } | null {
31	  const decoded = (() => {
32	    try {
33	      return decodeURIComponent(raw);
34	    } catch {
35	      return raw;
36	    }
37	  })();
38	  const normalized = decoded
39	    .toLowerCase()
40	    .trim()
41	    .replace(/[_\s]+/g, "-");
42	  const country = SLUG_TO_COUNTRY[normalized];
43	  if (!country) return null;
44	  return { slug: normalized, country };
45	}
46	
47	export async function generateMetadata({ params }: PageProps): Promise<Metadata> {
48	  const { country: raw } = await params;
49	  const resolved = resolveSlug(raw);
50	  if (!resolved) return { title: "Country not found" };
51	  return {
52	    title: `${resolved.country} — UIFPI`,
53	    description: `UIFPI price index and CPI comparison for ${resolved.country}.`,
54	  };
55	}
56	
57	function fmt(v: number | null | undefined, decimals = 2): string {
58	  if (v == null) return "—";
59	  return v.toFixed(decimals);
60	}
61	
62	function DownloadButton({ country, csvData }: { country: string; csvData: string }) {
63	  const blob = `data:text/csv;charset=utf-8,${encodeURIComponent(csvData)}`;
64	  const filename = `uifpi_${country.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, "_")}.csv`;
65	  return (
66	    <a
67	      href={blob}
68	      download={filename}
69	      className="inline-flex items-center gap-2 bg-[#1a365d] text-white font-medium px-4 py-2 rounded-lg hover:bg-[#2a4a7f] transition-colors text-sm"
70	    >
71	      ↓ Download CSV
72	    </a>
73	  );
74	}
75	
76	export default async function CountryPage({ params }: PageProps) {
77	  const { country: raw } = await params;
78	  const resolved = resolveSlug(raw);
79	
80	  if (!resolved) {
81	    notFound();
82	  }
83	
84	  // Canonicalise: if the URL segment differs from the canonical lowercase
85	  // slug (e.g. "Singapore", "United%20States"), 308-redirect so search
86	  // engines and shared links converge on one URL per country.
87	  if (raw !== resolved.slug) {
88	    permanentRedirect(`/${resolved.slug}`);
89	  }
90	
91	  const countryName = resolved.country;
92	
93	  const { summary, latest, series } = await getCountryData(countryName);
94	  const csvData = seriesToCsv(countryName, series);
95	  const devStatus = DEVELOPMENT_STATUS[countryName];
96	  const totalItems = (summary?.items_formal ?? 0) + (summary?.items_informal ?? 0);
97	  const hasCpi = series.some((d) => d.cpi != null);
98	
99	  const prevCountryIdx =
100	    (COUNTRIES.indexOf(countryName as (typeof COUNTRIES)[number]) - 1 + COUNTRIES.length) %
101	    COUNTRIES.length;
102	  const nextCountryIdx =
103	    (COUNTRIES.indexOf(countryName as (typeof COUNTRIES)[number]) + 1) % COUNTRIES.length;
104	  const prevCountry = COUNTRIES[prevCountryIdx];
105	  const nextCountry = COUNTRIES[nextCountryIdx];
106	
107	  return (
108	    <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8 py-8">
109	      {/* Breadcrumb */}
110	      <nav className="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-gray-500 mb-6">
111	        <Link href="/" className="hover:text-gray-700 transition-colors">
112	          Dashboard
113	        </Link>
114	        <span>/</span>
115	        <span className="text-gray-900 font-medium">{countryName}</span>
116	      </nav>
117	
118	      {/* Header */}
119	      <div className="flex flex-wrap items-start justify-between gap-4 mb-8">
120	        <div className="flex items-center gap-3">

[2026-06-17T13:13:14.171Z] USER

[TOOL RESULT]
1	"""
2	Phase 1 — historical HTML scraper.
3	
4	For each (country, platform) winner from the Phase 0 matrix, do a
5	time-distributed Wayback CDX walk, fetch each archived snapshot via
6	the id_ raw-bytes path, and extract menu prices using a per-platform
7	parser. Insert item-level rows into uifpi.db with source = wayback-<platform>.
8	
9	This complements the existing TripAdvisor pipeline in historical_scraper.py;
10	it does NOT modify that file's targets. The two scrapers coexist:
11	  - historical_scraper.py    — TripAdvisor Restaurant_Review pages
12	                               (review-quoted prices + $-tier markers)
13	  - historical_html_scraper  — Real menu pages on Zomato / MenuPages /
14	                               Eatigo / Menulog / GrabFood / TripAdvisor MX
15	
16	Resumable: any (snapshot_url) already in `prices` is skipped at fetch time.
17	Schema: extends `prices` (already has all columns we need).
18	
19	Usage:
20	  python3 historical_html_scraper.py                    # all winners
21	  python3 historical_html_scraper.py 'India: zomato NCR'
22	  python3 historical_html_scraper.py --max-per-target 200
23	"""
24	import argparse
25	import json
26	import os
27	import random
28	import re
29	import sqlite3
30	import sys
31	import time
32	from collections import defaultdict
33	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
34	from datetime import datetime
35	
36	import requests
37	from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
38	
39	BASE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
40	DB   = os.path.join(BASE, 'uifpi.db')
41	HDR  = {'User-Agent': 'UIFPI-research-pipeline (academic; contact via repo issues)'}
42	CDX  = 'http://web.archive.org/cdx/search/cdx'
43	WBM  = 'https://web.archive.org/web'
44	
45	CDX_TIMEOUT       = 90
46	CDX_LIMIT         = 30000
47	CDX_DELAY         = 3.0
48	CDX_RETRIES       = 2
49	CDX_BACKOFF       = 15
50	FETCH_TIMEOUT     = 45
51	FETCH_RETRIES     = 2
52	FETCH_BACKOFF     = 8
53	FETCH_DELAY       = 1.5      # per worker
54	DEFAULT_PER_PERIOD = 5       # snapshots per quarter window per platform
55	DEFAULT_MAX_PER_TARGET = 500
56	PROGRESS_FILE = os.path.join(BASE, 'historical_html_progress.json')
57	
58	# ── Currency regexes for DOM fallback ────────────────────────────────────────
59	
60	CURRENCY_REGEXES = {
61	    'USD': re.compile(r'\$\s?(\d+(?:\.\d{2})?)'),
62	    'INR': re.compile(r'(?:₹|Rs\.?\s)\s?(\d+(?:[.,]\d{2})?)'),
63	    'IDR': re.compile(r'Rp\.?\s?([\d.,]+)'),
64	    'THB': re.compile(r'(?:฿|THB)\s?(\d+(?:\.\d{2})?)'),
65	    'AUD': re.compile(r'A?\$\s?(\d+(?:\.\d{2})?)'),
66	    'SGD': re.compile(r'S\$\s?(\d+(?:\.\d{2})?)'),
67	    'PHP': re.compile(r'(?:₱|PHP)\s?(\d+(?:\.\d{2})?)'),
68	    'MXN': re.compile(r'\$\s?(\d+(?:\.\d{2})?)'),
69	}
70	
71	# ── Generic JSON-LD price walker ─────────────────────────────────────────────
72	
73	def _walk_ld(node, items, name_ctx=None):
74	    """Walk a JSON-LD tree collecting (name, price, currency) where MenuItem
75	    or Offer or Product carries a price."""
76	    if isinstance(node, dict):
77	        nm = (node.get('name') or name_ctx)
78	        cur_node = node.get('priceCurrency')
79	        price_node = node.get('price')
80	        if price_node is None:
81	            offers = node.get('offers')
82	            if isinstance(offers, dict):
83	                price_node = offers.get('price')
84	                cur_node = cur_node or offers.get('priceCurrency')
85	            elif isinstance(offers, list) and offers:
86	                if isinstance(offers[0], dict):
87	                    price_node = offers[0].get('price')
88	                    cur_node = cur_node or offers[0].get('priceCurrency')
89	        if price_node is None:
90	            ps = node.get('priceSpecification')
91	            if isinstance(ps, dict):
92	                price_node = ps.get('price')
93	                cur_node = cur_node or ps.get('priceCurrency')
94	        # Skip non-menu @types (addresses, coordinates, restaurant-level
95	        # entities). TripAdvisor encodes FoodEstablishment with a priceRange
96	        # tier; restaurants have averagePrice fields that are restaurant-
97	        # level not item-level. Only emit prices from menu-shaped nodes.
98	        bad_types = {'PostalAddress', 'GeoCoordinates', 'Restaurant',
99	                     'FoodEstablishment', 'Place', 'LocalBusiness',
100	                     'BreadcrumbList', 'AggregateRating', 'Review'}
101	        if nm and price_node is not None and node.get('@type') not in bad_types:
102	            try:
103	                p = float(re.sub(r'[^\d.]', '', str(price_node)))
104	            except Exception:
105	                p = None
106	            if p and 0 < p < 100_000:
107	                items.append((str(nm)[:120], p, cur_node))
108	        for v in node.values():
109	            _walk_ld(v, items, nm)
110	    elif isinstance(node, list):
111	        for v in node:
112	            _walk_ld(v, items, name_ctx)
113	
114	
115	def extract_jsonld(html):
116	    """Return list of (name, price, currency_or_None) from all JSON-LD blocks."""
117	    if not html:
118	        return []
119	    out = []
120	    for m in re.finditer(
121	        r'<script[^>]+type=["\']application/ld\+json["\'][^>]*>(.*?)</script>',
122	        html, re.S | re.I,
123	    ):
124	        try:
125	            obj = json.loads(m.group(1))
126	        except Exception:
127	            continue
128	        _walk_ld(obj, out)
129	    # Dedup
130	    seen = set(); uniq = []
131	    for n, p, c in out:
132	        k = (n, round(p, 2), c)
133	        if k in seen:
134	            continue
135	        seen.add(k); uniq.append((n, p, c))
136	    return uniq
137	
138	
139	def extract_nextdata(html):
140	    """Pull __NEXT_DATA__ JSON blob and walk for prices the same way."""
141	    if not html or '__NEXT_DATA__' not in html:
142	        return []
143	    m = re.search(
144	        r'<script[^>]+id=["\']__NEXT_DATA__["\'][^>]*>(.*?)</script>',
145	        html, re.S | re.I,
146	    )
147	    if not m:
148	        return []
149	    try:
150	        obj = json.loads(m.group(1))
151	    except Exception:
152	        return []
153	    out = []
154	    _walk_ld(obj, out)
155	    seen = set(); uniq = []
156	    for n, p, c in out:
157	        k = (n, round(p, 2), c)
158	        if k in seen:
159	            continue
160	        seen.add(k); uniq.append((n, p, c))
161	    return uniq
162	
163	
164	def extract_zomato_costfortwo(html, currency):
165	    """Zomato pre-2020 restaurant pages publish 'cost for two people'
166	    as a restaurant-level average meal price rather than item-level prices.
167	    Extract that single signal per page.
168	
169	    Returns [('cost_for_two', price, currency)] or [].
170	    """
171	    if not html:
172	        return []
173	    text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', html)
174	    text = re.sub(r'&[a-zA-Z]+;', ' ', text)
175	    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text)
176	    # Patterns like "₹1,600 for two people" or "Rs. 1600 for two"
177	    patterns = [
178	        r'(?:₹|Rs\.?\s)\s?([\d,]+)\s*for\s*two',
179	        r'Rp\.?\s?([\d.,]+)\s*for\s*two',
180	        r'₱\s?([\d,]+)\s*for\s*two',
181	    ]
182	    for pat in patterns:
183	        m = re.search(pat, text, re.I)
184	        if m:
185	            raw = m.group(1)
186	            # IDR uses dots as thousand separators with no decimals
187	            if currency == 'IDR':
188	                raw = raw.replace('.', '').replace(',', '')
189	            else:
190	                raw = raw.replace(',', '')
191	            try:
192	                p = float(raw)
193	            except ValueError:
194	                continue
195	            if p > 0:
196	                return [('cost_for_two', p, currency)]
197	    return []
198	
199	
200	# ── Per-platform parsers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
201	
202	def parse_zomato(html, currency):
203	    """Zomato pre-2020 archived pages don't expose item-level prices in DOM
204	    or JSON-LD — they show 'cost for two people' as a restaurant-level
205	    average meal price. Extract that single signal per page; treat it as
206	    item_name='cost_for_two' so the index can roll it up per restaurant.
207	    """
208	    items = extract_jsonld(html)
209	    if items:
210	        return _coerce(items, currency)
211	    return _coerce(extract_zomato_costfortwo(html, currency), currency)
212	
213	
214	def parse_menupages(html, currency):
215	    """MenuPages: Schema.org Menu → MenuSection → MenuItem. The JSON-LD
216	    walker hits these cleanly (200+ items/page in validation). No DOM
217	    fallback — better an empty parse than junk."""
218	    return _coerce(extract_jsonld(html), currency)
219	
220	
221	def parse_eatigo(html, currency):
222	    """Eatigo BKK: validation found 0 LD prices and the sampled pages were
223	    mostly category/listing URLs. Returns empty; Thailand will need a
224	    different source or fall back to the Numbeo proxy."""
225	    return _coerce(extract_jsonld(html), currency)
226	
227	
228	def parse_menulog(html, currency):
229	    """Menulog: NEXT_DATA has the menu structure, JSON-LD as backup."""
230	    items = extract_nextdata(html) or extract_jsonld(html)
231	    return _coerce(items, currency)
232	
233	
234	def parse_grabfood(html, currency):
235	    """GrabFood SG archived: NEXT_DATA + JSON-LD."""
236	    items = extract_nextdata(html) or extract_jsonld(html)
237	    return _coerce(items, currency)
238	
239	
240	def parse_tripadvisor_mx(html, currency):
241	    """TripAdvisor MX restaurant pages: JSON-LD MenuItem when present.
242	    Most pages only have FoodEstablishment with priceRange (a tier
243	    marker) — we explicitly skip those via the _walk_ld @type guard."""
244	    return _coerce(extract_jsonld(html), currency)
245	
246	
247	def _coerce(items, default_currency):
248	    """Normalise (name, price, currency_or_None) → standard tuples."""
249	    out = []
250	    for n, p, c in items:

278	
279	# ── CDX walk (time-distributed) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
280	
281	def _period_windows(from_year, to_year):
282	    out = []
283	    for y in range(from_year, to_year + 1):
284	        for q in range(4):
285	            m0 = q * 3 + 1
286	            m1 = q * 3 + 3
287	            last = {3: 31, 6: 30, 9: 30, 12: 31}[m1]
288	            out.append((f'{y:04d}{m0:02d}01', f'{y:04d}{m1:02d}{last}'))
289	    return out
290	
291	
292	def get_distributed_snapshots(pattern, per_period, max_snapshots,
293	                              from_year=2018, to_year=2026):
294	    out = []
295	    seen = set()
296	    for start, end in _period_windows(from_year, to_year):
297	        params = {
298	            'url':    pattern, 'from': start, 'to': end,
299	            'output': 'json',  'fl':   'timestamp,original',
300	            'filter': ['statuscode:200', 'mimetype:text/html'],
301	            'collapse': 'urlkey',
302	            'limit':  per_period * 4,
303	        }
304	        rows = None
305	        for attempt in range(CDX_RETRIES + 1):
306	            try:
307	                r = requests.get(CDX, params=params, headers=HDR,
308	                                 timeout=CDX_TIMEOUT)
309	                if r.status_code == 200:
310	                    data = r.json()
311	                    rows = data[1:] if len(data) > 1 else []
312	                    break
313	                if attempt < CDX_RETRIES:
314	                    time.sleep(CDX_BACKOFF)
315	            except Exception:
316	                if attempt < CDX_RETRIES:
317	                    time.sleep(CDX_BACKOFF)
318	        if rows is None:
319	            rows = []
320	        taken = 0
321	        for row in rows:
322	            ts, orig = row[0], row[1]
323	            if orig in seen:
324	                continue
325	            seen.add(orig)
326	            out.append({'timestamp': ts, 'url': orig})
327	            taken += 1
328	            if taken >= per_period:
329	                break
330	        if max_snapshots and len(out) >= max_snapshots:
331	            break
332	        time.sleep(CDX_DELAY)
333	    return out
334	
335	
336	def fetch_snapshot(ts, url):
337	    raw_url = f'{WBM}/{ts}id_/{url}'
338	    for attempt in range(FETCH_RETRIES + 1):
339	        try:
340	            r = requests.get(raw_url, headers=HDR, timeout=FETCH_TIMEOUT)
341	            if r.status_code != 200:
342	                if attempt < FETCH_RETRIES:
343	                    time.sleep(FETCH_BACKOFF)
344	                    continue
345	                return None
346	            return r.text
347	        except Exception:
348	            if attempt < FETCH_RETRIES:
349	                time.sleep(FETCH_BACKOFF)
350	    return None
351	
352	
353	# ── DB helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
354	
355	def already_have(conn, url):
356	    return conn.execute(
357	        "SELECT 1 FROM prices WHERE url = ? LIMIT 1", (url,)
358	    ).fetchone() is not None
359	
360	
361	def insert_items(conn, country, sector, source_key, url, ts, items):
362	    if not items:
363	        return 0
364	    try:
365	        collection_date = datetime.strptime(ts[:8], '%Y%m%d').strftime('%Y-%m-%d')
366	    except Exception:
367	        collection_date = ts[:10]
368	    n = 0
369	    for name, price, currency in items:
370	        conn.execute(
371	            "INSERT INTO prices "
372	            "(restaurant_name, item_name, price, currency, country, sector, "
373	            " source, collection_date, url) "
374	            "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
375	            (name[:100], name[:200], price, currency, country, sector,
376	             source_key, collection_date, url)
377	        )
378	        n += 1
379	    conn.commit()
380	    return n
381	
382	
383	# ── Runner ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
384	
385	def load_progress():
386	    if os.path.exists(PROGRESS_FILE):
387	        with open(PROGRESS_FILE) as fh:
388	            return json.load(fh)
389	    return {}
390	
391	
392	def save_progress(p):
393	    with open(PROGRESS_FILE, 'w') as fh:
394	        json.dump(p, fh, indent=2)
395	
396	
397	def run_target(target, per_period, max_per_target):
398	    country, sector, label, src_key, pat, currency, parser = target
399	    key = f'{country}:{label}'
400	    print(f"\n{'='*70}\n  {key}  ({pat})\n{'='*70}")
401	    progress = load_progress()
402	    info = progress.get(key, {})
403	    snaps = info.get('snapshots')
404	    done  = set(info.get('done_urls', []))
405	    if not snaps:
406	        print(f"  Querying CDX (distributed, per_period={per_period}, "
407	              f"max={max_per_target}) …")
408	        snaps = get_distributed_snapshots(pat, per_period, max_per_target)
409	        print(f"  Found {len(snaps)} candidate snapshots")
410	        progress[key] = {'snapshots': snaps, 'done_urls': list(done),
411	                         'status': 'in_progress'}
412	        save_progress(progress)
413	
414	    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB, timeout=30)
415	    conn.execute('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL')
416	    conn.execute('PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL')
417	
418	    parse_attempts = 0
419	    parse_hits     = 0
420	    rows_inserted  = 0
421	
422	    for i, snap in enumerate(snaps):
423	        ts, url = snap['timestamp'], snap['url']
424	        if url in done:
425	            continue
426	        if already_have(conn, url):
427	            done.add(url); continue
428	        print(f"  [{i+1}/{len(snaps)}] {ts[:8]} {url[:70]} … ",
429	              end='', flush=True)
430	        time.sleep(FETCH_DELAY)
431	        html = fetch_snapshot(ts, url)
432	        if html is None:
433	            print("fetch fail")
434	            done.add(url)
435	            progress[key]['done_urls'] = list(done)
436	            save_progress(progress)
437	            continue
438	        parse_attempts += 1
439	        try:
440	            items = parser(html, currency)
441	        except Exception as e:
442	            print(f"parse err {str(e)[:30]}")
443	            done.add(url)
444	            progress[key]['done_urls'] = list(done)
445	            save_progress(progress)
446	            continue
447	        # Use the URL slug as a stable restaurant_name proxy when parser
448	        # doesn't return useful names — but here items already carry name.
449	        # Strip restaurant name from the URL for the row's restaurant_name col.

486	          f"{rows_inserted} rows inserted")
487	    return {
488	        'target': key, 'attempts': parse_attempts,
489	        'hits': parse_hits, 'rows': rows_inserted,
490	    }

492	
493	def _restaurant_from_url(url, platform_label):
494	    """Extract a restaurant slug for restaurant_name col."""
495	    # Strip query / fragment
496	    u = url.split('?', 1)[0].split('#', 1)[0]
497	    parts = [p for p in u.rstrip('/').split('/') if p]
498	    # Last meaningful path component is usually the restaurant slug
499	    slug = parts[-1] if parts else url
500	    slug = slug.replace('-', ' ').replace('_', ' ')[:80]
501	    return f'{slug} ({platform_label})'
502	
503	
504	def main():
505	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
506	    ap.add_argument('targets', nargs='*',
507	                    help='Optional target keys (e.g., "India: zomato NCR"). '
508	                         'Default = all winners.')
509	    ap.add_argument('--per-period', type=int, default=DEFAULT_PER_PERIOD)
510	    ap.add_argument('--max-per-target', type=int, default=DEFAULT_MAX_PER_TARGET)
511	    ap.add_argument('--list', action='store_true', help='List targets and exit')
512	    args = ap.parse_args()
513	
514	    if args.list:
515	        for t in TARGETS:
516	            print(f"  {t[0]:<14} {t[2]:<20} pattern={t[4]}")
517	        return
518	
519	    selected = TARGETS
520	    if args.targets:
521	        keys = {a.strip().lower() for a in args.targets}
522	        selected = [t for t in TARGETS
523	                    if f'{t[0]}: {t[2]}'.lower() in keys or
524	                       t[2].lower() in keys]
525	        if not selected:
526	            print("No targets matched."); return
527	
528	    print(f"Historical HTML scraper — {len(selected)} target(s)")
529	    print(f"per_period={args.per_period}, max_per_target={args.max_per_target}\n")
530	
531	    summary = []
532	    for t in selected:
533	        summary.append(run_target(t, args.per_period, args.max_per_target))
534	
535	    print(f"\n{'='*70}\nDone.")
536	    print(f"{'target':<40} {'attempts':>9} {'hits':>5} {'rows':>7}")
537	    for s in summary:
538	        print(f"  {s['target']:<38} {s['attempts']:>9} {s['hits']:>5} {s['rows']:>7}")
539	
540	
541	if __name__ == '__main__':
542	    main()

      'cdx_status': '',\n            'sample_url': '', 'sample_ts': '',\n            'sample_kb': '', 'sample_price_hits': '',\n            'sample_json_ld': '', 'sample_next_data': '',\n         results.append(rec)\n            time.sleep(CDX_DELAY)\n            continue\n\n        if not rows:\n            print(f\"          0 rows\", flush=True)\n            rec['cdx_status'] = 'empty'\n            results.append(rec)\n            time.sleep(CDX_DELAY)\n            continue\n\n        per_url = defaultdict(list)\n        for ts, orig in rows:\n            per_url[orig].append(ts)\n        ge2 = [u for u, ts_list in per_url.items() if len(ts_list) >= 2]\n        all_ts = sorted(ts for ts_list in per_url.values() for ts in ts_list)\n        rec.update({\n            'n_restaurants_ge2': len(ge2),\n            'n_snapshots': len(rows),\n            'distinct_urls': len(per_url),\n            'earliest': all_ts[0][:8] if all_ts else '',\n            'latest':   all_ts[-1][:8] if all_ts else '',\n            'cdx_status': 'truncated' if len(rows) >= CDX_LIMIT else 'ok',\n        })\n        trunc = ' (TRUNCATED)' if len(rows) >= CDX_LIMIT else ''\n        print(f\"          {len(per_url):>6} distinct URLs, {len(ge2):>5} \u22652-cap, \"\n              f\"{len(rows):>6} snapshots, {rec['earliest']}-{rec['latest']}{trunc}\",\n              flush=True)\n\n        candidates = ge2 if ge2 else list(per_url.keys())\n        sample_url = rnd.choice(candidates)\n        sample_ts  = per_url[sample_url][len(per_url[sample_url]) // 2]\n        cre = CURRENCY_REGEXES.get(cur, CURRENCY_REGEXES['USD'])\n        time.sleep(FETCH_DELAY)\n        print(f\"          sampling {sample_ts} {sample_url[:70]}\", flush=True)\n        s, ferr = sample_page_for_prices(sample_ts, sample_url, cre)\n        rec['sample_url'] = sample_url\n        rec['sample_ts']  = sample_ts\n        if ferr:\n            rec['sample_status'] = f'error:{ferr}'\n            print(f\"          sample fetch failed: {ferr}\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            rec.update({\n                'sample_kb': s['kb'],\n                'sample_price_hits': s['price_hits'],\n                'sample_json_ld': 'Y' if s['json_ld'] else 'N',\n                'sample_next_data': 'Y' if s['next_data'] else 'N',\n                'sample_block': s['block'],\n                'sample_status': 'ok' if not s['block'] else f'block:{s[\"block\"]}',\n            })\n            block_str = f' BLOCK={s[\"block\"]}' if s['block'] else ''\n            print(f\"          sample: {s['kb']} KB, {s['price_hits']} {cur} hits, \"\n                  f\"LD={s['json_ld']} ND={s['next_data']}{block_str}\",\n                  flush=True)\n\n        results.append(rec)\n        time.sleep(CDX_DELAY)\n\n    csv_path = os.path.join(BASE, 'coverage_report_track_b.csv')\n    fieldnames = ['country', 'sector', 'platform', 'pattern', 'currency',\n                  'n_restaurants_ge2', 'n_snapshots', 'distinct_urls',\n                  'earliest', 'latest', 'cdx_status',\n                  'sample_url', 'sample_ts', 'sample_kb',\n                  'sample_price_hits', 'sample_json_ld', 'sample_next_data',\n                  'sample_block', 'sample_status']\n    with open(csv_path, 'w', newline='') as fh:\n        w = csv.DictWriter(fh, fieldnames=fieldnames)\n        w.writeheader()\n        w.writerows(results)\n    print(f\"\\nCSV \u2192 {csv_path}\", flush=True)\n\n    md_path = os.path.join(BASE, 'coverage_report_track_b.md')\n    with open(md_path, 'w') as fh:\n        fh.write(f\"# Phase 0 \u2014 Track B candidate-source probe\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(f\"Generated: {datetime.now().isoformat()}\\n  \\n\")\n        fh.write(f\"Window: {WINDOW_FROM} \u2192 {WINDOW_TO}\\n  \\n\")\n        fh.write(f\"CDX limit per query: {CDX_LIMIT:,}.\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"Same yields-table format as `coverage_report.md`. Adds a \"\n                 \"`Block` column flagging Cloudflare/captcha/tiny-page \"\n                 \"responses so we can bail on bot-blocked sources fast.\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"## Matrix\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"| Country | Platform | URL pattern | \u22652-cap restaurants | \"\n                 \"Snapshots | Range | Sample KB | Sample $-hits | LD | ND | Block | Notes |\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|---:|---:|:---:|:---:|---|---|\\n\")\n        for r in results:\n            rng = f\"{r['earliest']}\u2013{r['latest']}\" if r['earliest'] else '\u2014'\n            notes = r['cdx_status']\n            if r['sample_status'] and r['sample_status'] not in ('ok', ''):\n                notes += '; sample ' + r['sample_status']\n            block = r.get('sample_block') or '\u2014'\n            fh.write(\n                f\"| {r['country']} | {r['platform']} | `{r['pattern']}` | \"\n                f\"{r['n_restaurants_ge2']:,} | {r['n_snapshots']:,} | {rng} | \"\n                f\"{r['sample_kb']} | {r['sample_price_hits']} | \"\n                f\"{r['sample_json_ld']} | {r['sample_next_data']} | \"\n                f\"{block} | {notes} |\\n\"\n            )\n\n        fh.write(\"\\n## Decision: which targets to add to \"\n                 \"`historical_html_scraper.py`?\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"| Country | Best platform | \u22652-cap | $-hits | Block | Verdict |\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"|---|---|---:|---:|---|---|\\n\")\n        roll = {}\n        for r in results:\n            c = r['country']\n            best = roll.get(c)\n            if (best is None or\n                (r['n_restaurants_ge2'], r['sample_price_hits'] or 0) >\n                (best['n_restaurants_ge2'], best['sample_price_hits'] or 0)):\n                roll[c] = r\n        for c in sorted(roll):\n            r = roll[c]\n            blocked = (r.get('sample_block') or '') != ''\n            ok = (r['n_restaurants_ge2'] >= 15 and\n                  (r['sample_price_hits'] or 0) >= 5 and not blocked)\n            verdict = ('\u2713 queue' if ok else\n                       'bail (bot-blocked)' if blocked else\n                       'bail (no prices visible)')\n            fh.write(f\"| {c} | {r['platform']} | {r['n_restaurants_ge2']:,} | \"\n                     f\"{r['sample_price_hits']} | \"\n                     f\"{r.get('sample_block') or '\u2014'} | {verdict} |\\n\")\n    print(f\"MD  \u2192 {md_path}\", flush=True)\n    print(f\"\\nDone.\", flush=True)\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    main()\n"
}

37	
38	## Reading the matrix
39	
40	- `≥2-cap restaurants` = distinct archived URLs with two or more captures inside the window. Gating metric for the formal-sector roster (≥15 required).
41	- `Sample $-hits` = currency-token count in ONE randomly-chosen archived page. Low or zero hits ⇒ HTML doesn't contain prices (JS shell that loaded prices via API after render).
42	- `LD` / `ND` = JSON-LD or `__NEXT_DATA__` present; either gives a clean structured-data path.
43	- A country clears the formal threshold only when BOTH ≥2-cap ≥ 15 **and** sample $-hits ≥ 5.
44

ID and TH formal coverage stays where it was \u2014 Zomato cost-for-two for ID, the single-snapshot Wayback TripAdvisor/Wongnai sweep for TH.\n\n## Track C \u2014 BR / DE / ZA detail\n\n### Brazil\n\n- **Uber Eats BR** (`ubereats.com/br/*`) \u2014 4,590 \u22652-cap restaurants, sample 218 BRL hits in 776 KB, JSON-LD present, no NEXT_DATA. Decisive winner.\n- **iFood RJ** (`ifood.com.br/delivery/rio-de-janeiro-rj/*`) \u2014 3,760 \u22652-cap, 5 BRL hits, LD + ND. Cleanest archived sample of any iFood URL; SP and the global `rappi.com.br/restaurantes/*` both returned HTTP 503 on Wayback playback.\n- TripAdvisor BR has 2,860 \u22652-cap restaurants but the sampled page had **0 BRL hits** in 1,320 KB \u2014 same JS-shell pattern as the original Mexico TripAdvisor MX probe.\n\n### Germany\n\n- **Lieferando** (`lieferando.de/speisekarte/*`) \u2014 6,464 \u22652-cap, 102 EUR hits in a 617 KB page, LD + ND. Strongest single signal in this whole probe round.\n- **Wolt DE** (`wolt.com/de/deu/*`) \u2014 4,358 \u22652-cap, 50 EUR hits, LD only. Solid fallback; Wolt entered DE around late 2020, so it's a shorter window.\n- Yelp DE: 5,536 \u22652-cap but 0 EUR hits \u2014 JS shell.\n- TripAdvisor DE + Uber Eats DE: HTTP 503 on Wayback playback.\n\n### South Africa\n\n- **TripAdvisor ZA** (`tripadvisor.co.za/Restaurant_Review*`) \u2014 2,744 \u22652-cap, 16 ZAR hits in 1,229 KB, JSON-LD. Note: \"ZAR hits\" here uses `\\bR\\s?\\d+(?:[.,]\\d{2})?` which has potential overlap with random capital-R tokens; need to validate in the actual parser.\n- **Uber Eats ZA** (`ubereats.com/za/*`) \u2014 1,845 \u22652-cap, 6 ZAR hits, LD only.\n- Mr D Food (`mrdfood.com/*`) \u2014 5,966 \u22652-cap but HTTP 503 on sample fetch. Probably blocks Wayback by user-agent or referrer.\n- EatOut ZA returned 0 rows.\n\n## Track B detail\n\n### Australia \u2014 Menulog revisit\n\nThe existing `wayback-menulog` source has 15 rows in `prices` from 128 fetched snapshots \u2014 a 12% parser yield that prior docs (`data_gaps.md`) noted as the source's apparent ceiling. The revisit probe sample shows **48 AUD hits in 851 KB** with both JSON-LD and NEXT_DATA present, which means the parser is leaving the majority of menu items on the table, not that the source is thin.\n\nRecommended next pass:\n1. Inspect 5 actual Menulog NEXT_DATA blobs to confirm where the prices sit (the existing `extract_nextdata` walks generically but probably hits a non-MenuItem path).\n2. Re-run `historical_html_scraper.py 'Australia: menulog' --per-period 15` after clearing the AU Menulog entry from `historical_html_progress.json` so the sweep widens beyond the 128-snapshot cache.\n\nNot run in this session because a full sweep with `per_period=15` is on the order of 30+ minutes of Wayback calls.\n\n### Indonesia \u2014 GoFood\n\n- `gofood.co.id/*` \u2014 4,901 \u22652-cap, 1,024 KB sample, **0 IDR hits**. LD + ND both present, but the JSON blobs contain restaurant metadata only (the actual menu items hydrate via XHR after page render). Both `gofood.co.id/jakarta/*` (more targeted) and `gojek.com/*/food/*` (alternative URL convention) returned the same shape \u2014 JS shell, prices not in static HTML.\n- ID formal coverage stays at the Zomato cost-for-two restaurant-aggregate signal (29 obs across 21 months).\n\n### Thailand \u2014 GrabFood\n\n- Both `food.grab.com/th/en/*` and `food.grab.com/th/th/*` returned **captcha pages** on Wayback playback \u2014 the 4 THB hits found by regex were inside the captcha challenge HTML, not menu data. Grab actively blocks archive crawlers.\n- LineMan TH (`lineman.co.th/*`) had **0 archived URLs** matching this pattern at all.\n- TH formal coverage stays at the single 2026-06-13 Wayback TripAdvisor/Wongnai THB-regex sweep (11 items, 9 restaurants).\n\n## Implications\n\n1. **Roster expands from 8 \u2192 11 countries**: US, SG, MY, UK, IN, AU, TH, ID, plus **BR, DE, ZA**. Mexico stays proxy-only (no archival source clears threshold; nothing in this probe round changed that).\n2. **The dashboard's `COUNTRIES` constant should expand to 11** after the first BR / DE / ZA scrape sweeps actually produce rows. Until then, BR / DE / ZA could optionally be added as proxy-only tiles (Numbeo + Big Mac + WB CPI), same template as Mexico.\n3. **`historical_html_scraper.py` gains three targets**: BR Uber Eats, DE Lieferando, ZA TripAdvisor (with the existing TripAdvisor parser). Each needs a smoke-test of 10\u201320 snapshots before a full sweep.\n4. **`floor_datasets.py` ROSTER must add BR / DE / ZA** so Numbeo + Big Mac + WB CPI data is loaded \u2014 currently the ROSTER stops at the 8 + VN panel.\n\n## Known issue: probe roll-up logic\n\nBoth probe scripts pick the \"best platform\" via `(n_restaurants_ge2, sample_price_hits or 0)` tuple comparison. When the sample fetch fails (HTTP 503), `sample_price_hits` is `''`, which `or 0`-coalesces to 0 and the largest-but-blocked source wins the tuple. The BR table in `coverage_report_br_de_za.md` therefore shows \"Rappi BR \u2014 best platform\" even though Rappi's sample fetch failed and a working Uber Eats BR sample returned 218 hits. The matrix itself is correct; only the roll-up section is misleading. Fix queued: skip rows where `sample_status` starts with `error:` before ranking.\n"
}

  "new_string": "CURRENCY_REGEXES = {\n    'USD': re.compile(r'\\$\\s?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})?)'),\n    'INR': re.compile(r'(?:\u20b9|Rs\\.?\\s)\\s?(\\d+(?:[.,]\\d{2})?)'),\n    'IDR': re.compile(r'Rp\\.?\\s?([\\d.,]+)'),\n    'THB': re.compile(r'(?:\u0e3f|THB)\\s?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})?)'),\n    'AUD': re.compile(r'A?\\$\\s?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})?)'),\n    'SGD': re.compile(r'S\\$\\s?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})?)'),\n    'PHP': re.compile(r'(?:\u20b1|PHP)\\s?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})?)'),\n    'MXN': re.compile(r'\\$\\s?(\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})?)'),\n    # Added 2026-06-17 (Track C). BRL uses 'R$' with comma decimal; EUR\n    # in DE locale also uses comma decimal and frequently writes '\u20ac' at\n    # either end of the number. ZAR uses bare 'R' which collides with\n    # English words \u2014 validate downstream that the regex match is on a\n    # price field, not body text.\n    'BRL': re.compile(r'R\\$\\s?(\\d+(?:[.,]\\d{2})?)'),\n    'EUR': re.compile(r'(?:\u20ac\\s?(\\d+(?:[.,]\\d{2})?)|(\\d+(?:[.,]\\d{2})?)\\s?\u20ac)'),\n    'ZAR': re.compile(r'\\bR\\s?(\\d+(?:[.,]\\d{2})?)'),\n}"
}

Diagnosis: the existing rows have `item_name` = restaurant name and `price` = 4.99 / 5.0 / 10.0 / 20.0 — those are **delivery fees and minimum order amounts**, not menu prices. Let me fetch a real archived page and inspect.

  "command": "python3 -u <<'PY' 2>&1 | tail -80\nimport requests, re, json, time\n\n# Strategy: use Wayback's \"closest\" availability API to find good snapshots\ndef closest(url, ts='20230601'):\n    api = f\"https://archive.org/wayback/available?url={url}&timestamp={ts}\"\n    try:\n        r = requests.get(api, timeout=20)\n        d = r.json()\n        c = d.get('archived_snapshots', {}).get('closest')\n        if c and c.get('available'):\n            return c['timestamp'], c['url']\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"  {url[:60]}: err {e}\")\n    return None, None\n\ncandidates = [\n    \"menulog.com.au/restaurants-pizza-hut-australia\",\n    \"menulog.com.au/restaurants-mcdonalds\",\n    \"menulog.com.au/restaurants-kfc\",\n    \"menulog.com.au/restaurants-grill-d\",\n    \"menulog.com.au/restaurants-domino-pizza-bondi-junction\",\n    \"menulog.com.au/restaurants-subway-bondi-junction\",\n    \"menulog.com.au/restaurants-nandos\",\n]\n\nfor u in candidates:\n    ts, archived = closest(u, '20230101')\n    if not ts:\n        print(f\"  {u[:60]} -- no archive\")\n        continue\n    raw = archived.replace('/web/', '/web/').replace(f'/{ts}/', f'/{ts}id_/', 1)\n    print(f\"\\n{ts} {u[:60]}\")\n    r = requests.get(raw, timeout=30)\n    n_aud = len(re.findall(r'A?\\$\\s?\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})', r.text))\n    has_nd = '__NEXT_DATA__' in r.text\n    has_ld = 'application/ld+json' in r.text\n    print(f\"  status={r.status_code} bytes={len(r.text)>>10}KB AUD(with decimals)={n_aud} ND={has_nd} LD={has_ld}\")\n    if n_aud >= 10 and (has_nd or has_ld):\n        print(f\"  *** GOOD SAMPLE ***\")\n        with open('/tmp/menulog_good.html', 'w') as f:\n            f.write(r.text)\n        print(f\"  saved to /tmp/menulog_good.html ({len(r.text)>>10}KB)\")\n        break\n    time.sleep(1)\nPY",

  "old_string": "def _walk_ld(node, items, name_ctx=None):\n    \"\"\"Walk a JSON-LD tree collecting (name, price, currency) where MenuItem\n    or Offer or Product carries a price.\"\"\"\n    if isinstance(node, dict):\n        nm = (node.get('name') or name_ctx)\n        cur_node = node.get('priceCurrency')\n        price_node = node.get('price')\n        if price_node is None:\n            offers = node.get('offers')\n            if isinstance(offers, dict):\n                price_node = offers.get('price')\n                cur_node = cur_node or offers.get('priceCurrency')\n            elif isinstance(offers, list) and offers:\n                if isinstance(offers[0], dict):\n                    price_node = offers[0].get('price')\n                    cur_node = cur_node or offers[0].get('priceCurrency')\n        if price_node is None:\n            ps = node.get('priceSpecification')\n            if isinstance(ps, dict):\n                price_node = ps.get('price')\n                cur_node = cur_node or ps.get('priceCurrency')\n        # Skip non-menu @types (addresses, coordinates, restaurant-level\n        # entities). TripAdvisor encodes FoodEstablishment with a priceRange\n        # tier; restaurants have averagePrice fields that are restaurant-\n        # level not item-level. Only emit prices from menu-shaped nodes.\n        bad_types = {'PostalAddress', 'GeoCoordinates', 'Restaurant',\n                     'FoodEstablishment', 'Place', 'LocalBusiness',\n                     'BreadcrumbList', 'AggregateRating', 'Review'}\n        if nm and price_node is not None and node.get('@type') not in bad_types:\n            try:\n                p = float(re.sub(r'[^\\d.]', '', str(price_node)))\n            except Exception:\n                p = None\n            if p and 0 < p < 100_000:\n                items.append((str(nm)[:120], p, cur_node))\n        for v in node.values():\n            _walk_ld(v, items, nm)\n    elif isinstance(node, list):\n        for v in node:\n            _walk_ld(v, items, name_ctx)",
  "new_string": "def _walk_ld(node, items, name_ctx=None):\n    \"\"\"Walk a JSON-LD tree collecting (name, price, currency) where MenuItem\n    or Offer or Product carries a price.\n\n    Emission requires the local node to have its OWN `name` field \u2014 see\n    the 2026-06-18 fix below.\n    \"\"\"\n    if isinstance(node, dict):\n        local_name = node.get('name')\n        nm = local_name or name_ctx\n        cur_node = node.get('priceCurrency')\n        price_node = node.get('price')\n        if price_node is None:\n            offers = node.get('offers')\n            if isinstance(offers, dict):\n                price_node = offers.get('price')\n                cur_node = cur_node or offers.get('priceCurrency')\n            elif isinstance(offers, list) and offers:\n                if isinstance(offers[0], dict):\n                    price_node = offers[0].get('price')\n                    cur_node = cur_node or offers[0].get('priceCurrency')\n        if price_node is None:\n            ps = node.get('priceSpecification')\n            if isinstance(ps, dict):\n                price_node = ps.get('price')\n                cur_node = cur_node or ps.get('priceCurrency')\n        # Skip non-menu @types (addresses, coordinates, restaurant-level\n        # entities). TripAdvisor encodes FoodEstablishment with a priceRange\n        # tier; restaurants have averagePrice fields that are restaurant-\n        # level not item-level. Only emit prices from menu-shaped nodes.\n        bad_types = {'PostalAddress', 'GeoCoordinates', 'Restaurant',\n                     'FoodEstablishment', 'Place', 'LocalBusiness',\n                     'BreadcrumbList', 'AggregateRating', 'Review'}\n        # Fix 2026-06-18: emit only when this node has its OWN name field.\n        # Inheriting name_ctx from a Restaurant/FoodEstablishment parent\n        # and pairing it with a child Offer/priceSpecification price\n        # produced the 15 wayback-menulog rows that all had restaurant\n        # names as item_names paired with 4.99 / 5.00 / 10.00 / 20.00\n        # values \u2014 i.e. delivery fees and min-order amounts, not menu\n        # items. MenuItem nodes that carry their own `name` are\n        # unaffected; anonymous Offer leaves under non-menu parents are\n        # correctly dropped.\n        if local_name and price_node is not None and node.get('@type') not in bad_types:\n            try:\n                p = float(re.sub(r'[^\\d.]', '', str(price_node)))\n            except Exception:\n                p = None\n            if p and 0 < p < 100_000:\n                items.append((str(local_name)[:120], p, cur_node))\n        for v in node.values():\n            _walk_ld(v, items, nm)\n    elif isinstance(node, list):\n        for v in node:\n            _walk_ld(v, items, name_ctx)"
}
