       'from':   WINDOW_FROM,\n        'to':     WINDOW_TO,\n        'output': 'json',\n        'fl':     'timestamp,original',\n        'filter': ['statuscode:200', 'mimetype:text/html'],\n        'limit':  CDX_LIMIT,\n    }\n    last_err = None\n    for attempt in range(CDX_RETRIES + 1):\n        try:\n            r = requests.get(CDX, params=params, headers=HDR, timeout=CDX_TIMEOUT)\n            if r.status_code != 200:\n                last_err = f'HTTP {r.status_code}'\n                if r.status_code in (429, 503, 504) and attempt < CDX_RETRIES:\n                    time.sleep(CDX_RETRY_BACKOFF); continue\n                return None, last_err\n            data = r.json()\n            if not data or len(data) < 2:\n                return [], None\n            return [(row[0], row[1]) for row in data[1:]], None\n        except Exception as e:\n            last_err = str(e)[:80]\n            if attempt < CDX_RETRIES:\n                time.sleep(CDX_RETRY_BACKOFF)\n    return None, last_err\n\n\ndef sample_page(timestamp, orig_url, currency_re):\n    url = f\"{WBM}/{timestamp}id_/{orig_url}\"\n    try:\n        r = requests.get(url, headers=HDR, timeout=FETCH_TIMEOUT)\n        if r.status_code != 200:\n            return None, f'HTTP {r.status_code}'\n        text = r.text\n        n_price = len(currency_re.findall(text))\n        has_ld  = 'application/ld+json' in text\n        has_nd  = '__NEXT_DATA__' in text\n        bl = text.lower(); block = ''\n        if len(text) < 400:\n            block = 'tiny-page'\n        elif 'cloudflare' in bl and ('attention required' in bl or 'verify' in bl):\n            block = 'cloudflare-challenge'\n        elif 'captcha' in bl or 'recaptcha' in bl:\n            block = 'captcha'\n        return {\n            'kb': len(r.content) // 1024, 'price_hits': n_price,\n            'json_ld': has_ld, 'next_data': has_nd, 'block': block,\n        }, None\n    except Exception as e:\n        return None, str(e)[:60]\n\n\ndef main():\n    print(f\"Phase 0 ID/TH alts probe \u2014 {len(PROBES)} pairs\", flush=True)\n    results = []\n    rnd = random.Random(20260618)\n\n    for i, (country, sector, platform, pat, cur) in enumerate(PROBES, 1):\n        print(f\"[{i:>2}/{len(PROBES)}] {country:<10} {platform:<28} {pat}\",\n              flush=True)\n        rows, err = query_cdx(pat)\n        rec = {\n            'country': country, 'sector': sector, 'platform': platform,\n            'pattern': pat, 'currency': cur,\n            'n_restaurants_ge2': 0, 'n_snapshots': 0,\n            'distinct_urls': 0, 'earliest': '', 'latest': '',\n            'cdx_status': '', 'sample_url': '', 'sample_ts': '',\n            'sample_kb': '', 'sample_price_hits': '',\n            'sample_json_ld': '', 'sample_next_data': '',\n            'sample_block': '', 'sample_status': '',\n        }\n        if err:\n            print(f\"          CDX ERROR: {err}\", flush=True)\n            rec['cdx_status'] = f'error:{err}'\n            results.append(rec); time.sleep(CDX_DELAY); continue\n        if not rows:\n            print(f\"          0 rows\", flush=True)\n            rec['cdx_status'] = 'empty'\n            results.append(rec); time.sleep(CDX_DELAY); continue\n        per_url = defaultdict(list)\n        for ts, orig in rows:\n            per_url[orig].append(ts)\n        ge2 = [u for u, ts_list in per_url.items() if len(ts_list) >= 2]\n        all_ts = sorted(ts for ts_list in per_url.values() for ts in ts_list)\n        rec.update({\n            'n_restaurants_ge2': len(ge2),\n            'n_snapshots': len(rows),\n            'distinct_urls': len(per_url),\n            'earliest': all_ts[0][:8] if all_ts else '',\n            'latest':   all_ts[-1][:8] if all_ts else '',\n            'cdx_status': 'truncated' if len(rows) >= CDX_LIMIT else 'ok',\n        })\n        trunc = ' (TRUNC)' if len(rows) >= CDX_LIMIT else ''\n        print(f\"          {len(per_url):>6} URLs, {len(ge2):>5} \u22652-cap, \"\n              f\"{len(rows):>6} snaps, {rec['earliest']}-{rec['latest']}{trunc}\",\n              flush=True)\n        cands = ge2 if ge2 else list(per_url.keys())\n        s_url = rnd.choice(cands)\n        s_ts  = per_url[s_url][len(per_url[s_url]) // 2]\n        cre   = CURRENCY_REGEXES.get(cur, re.compile(r'\\d+'))\n        time.sleep(FETCH_DELAY)\n        print(f\"          sampling {s_ts} {s_url[:60]}\", flush=True)\n        s, ferr = sample_page(s_ts, s_url, cre)\n        rec['sample_url'] = s_url; rec['sample_ts'] = s_ts\n        if ferr:\n            rec['sample_status'] = f'error:{ferr}'\n            print(f\"          sample fetch failed: {ferr}\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            rec.update({\n                'sample_kb': s['kb'],\n                'sample_price_hits': s['price_hits'],\n                'sample_json_ld': 'Y' if s['json_ld'] else 'N',\n                'sample_next_data': 'Y' if s['next_data'] else 'N',\n                'sample_block': s['block'],\n                'sample_status': 'ok' if not s['block'] else f'block:{s[\"block\"]}',\n            })\n            bs = f' BLOCK={s[\"block\"]}' if s['block'] else ''\n            print(f\"          {s['kb']}KB, {s['price_hits']} {cur} hits, \"\n                  f\"LD={s['json_ld']} ND={s['next_data']}{bs}\", flush=True)\n        results.append(rec)\n        time.sleep(CDX_DELAY)\n\n    csv_path = os.path.join(BASE, 'coverage_report_id_th_alts.csv')\n    fns = ['country','sector','platform','pattern','currency',\n           'n_restaurants_ge2','n_snapshots','distinct_urls',\n           'earliest','latest','cdx_status',\n           'sample_url','sample_ts','sample_kb',\n           'sample_price_hits','sample_json_ld','sample_next_data',\n           'sample_block','sample_status']\n    with open(csv_path,'w',newline='') as fh:\n        w = csv.DictWriter(fh, fieldnames=fns); w.writeheader(); w.writerows(results)\n    print(f\"\\nCSV \u2192 {csv_path}\", flush=True)\n\n    md_path = os.path.join(BASE, 'coverage_report_id_th_alts.md')\n    with open(md_path,'w') as fh:\n        fh.write(f\"# Phase 0 \u2014 ID/TH alternative-source probe\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(f\"Generated: {datetime.now().isoformat()}\\n  \\n\")\n        fh.write(f\"Window: {WINDOW_FROM} \u2192 {WINDOW_TO}\\n  \\n\")\n        fh.write(\"Probes alternative routes to escape the dead GoFood / \"\n                 \"GrabFood paths identified in `coverage_report_track_b.md`.\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"## Matrix\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"| Country | Platform | URL pattern | \u22652-cap restaurants | \"\n                 \"Snapshots | Range | Sample KB | Sample $-hits | LD | ND | Block | Notes |\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|---:|---:|:---:|:---:|---|---|\\n\")\n        for r in results:\n            rng = f\"{r['earliest']}\u2013{r['latest']}\" if r['earliest'] else '\u2014'\n            notes = r['cdx_status']\n            if r['sample_status'] and r['sample_status'] not in ('ok',''):\n                notes += '; sample ' + r['sample_status']\n            block = r.get('sample_block') or '\u2014'\n            fh.write(\n                f\"| {r['country']} | {r['platform']} | `{r['pattern']}` | \"\n                f\"{r['n_restaurants_ge2']:,} | {r['n_snapshots']:,} | {rng} | \"\n                f\"{r['sample_kb']} | {r['sample_price_hits']} | \"\n                f\"{r['sample_json_ld']} | {r['sample_next_data']} | \"\n                f\"{block} | {notes} |\\n\"\n            )\n        fh.write(\"\\n## Decision\\n\\n\")\n        roll = {}\n        for r in results:\n            c = r['country']\n            if (r.get('sample_status') or '').startswith('error:'):\n                continue\n            best = roll.get(c)\n            if (best is None or\n                (r['n_restaurants_ge2'], r['sample_price_hits'] or 0) >\n                (best['n_restaurants_ge2'], best['sample_price_hits'] or 0)):\n                roll[c] = r\n        fh.write(\"| Country | Best platform | \u22652-cap | $-hits | Block | Verdict |\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"|---|---|---:|---:|---|---|\\n\")\n        for c in sorted(roll):\n            r = roll[c]\n            blocked = (r.get('sample_block') or '') != ''\n            ok = (r['n_restaurants_ge2'] >= 15 and\n                  (r['sample_price_hits'] or 0) >= 5 and not blocked)\n            verdict = ('\u2713 queue' if ok else\n                       'bail (bot-blocked)' if blocked else\n                       'bail (no prices visible)')\n            fh.write(f\"| {c} | {r['platform']} | {r['n_restaurants_ge2']:,} | \"\n                     f\"{r['sample_price_hits']} | \"\n                     f\"{r.get('sample_block') or '\u2014'} | {verdict} |\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"\\nIf both ID and TH bail again, the documented plan is \"\n                 \"to ship them as floor-only countries \u2014 see \"\n                 \"the user direction 2026-06-18.\\n\")\n    print(f\"MD  \u2192 {md_path}\", flush=True)\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    main()\n"
}

For Schema.org\nRestaurant JSON-LD that pattern produced\n`(\"Restaurant Name\", 4.99)` where the price was the delivery fee from\nan anonymous `Offer` under `Restaurant.offers` and the name was the\nrestaurant's name \u2014 the parent's. The 15 wayback-menulog rows that\nsat in `prices` were all of this shape (prices 4.99 / 5.00 / 10.00 /\n20.00 \u2014 Menulog's flat delivery fee and minimum-order tiers).\n\nFix: the walker still recurses with the inherited `name_ctx` for\ndescent, but emission requires the local node to have its own `name`.\nAnonymous Offer / priceSpecification leaves under non-menu parents\nare now correctly dropped. MenuPages-shaped JSON-LD\n(Menu \u2192 MenuSection \u2192 MenuItem with own name) is unaffected \u2014 all\nsix synthetic test shapes pass.\n\nAction taken:\n  - The 15 polluted `wayback-menulog` rows in `prices` were deleted.\n  - The `Australia:menulog` entry in `historical_html_progress.json`\n    was reset so the 128 cached snapshots are re-walked with the\n    fixed parser.\n  - `historical_html_scraper.py --per-period 3 --max-per-target 100`\n    re-launched. Look for menu/products inside\n                def walk_find_keys(node, target_keys, results, path=''):\n                    if isinstance(node, dict):\n                        for k, v in node.items():\n                            if k in target_keys:\n                                if isinstance(v, list):\n                                    results.append((path + '/' + k, type(v).__name__, f'len={len(v)}'))\n                                elif isinstance(v, dict):\n                                    results.append((path + '/' + k, type(v).__name__, f'keys={list(v.keys())[:5]}'))\n                                else:\n                                    results.append((path + '/' + k, type(v).__name__, str(v)[:50]))\n                            walk_find_keys(v, target_keys, results, path + '/' + k)\n                    elif isinstance(node, list):\n                        for i, v in enumerate(node[:5]):\n                            walk_find_keys(v, target_keys, results, f'{path}[{i}]')\n                hits = []\n                walk_find_keys(nd_obj, {'menu', 'menuItems', 'products', 'items', 'categories', 'price', 'priceInCents'}, hits)\n                for path, t, info in hits[:25]:\n                    print(f\"    ND{path[:80]}  {t}  {info}\")\n        # [2026-06-17T23:47:01.073Z] ASSISTANT
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Root cause: archived pages truncate mid-script, so `</script>` is absent. The parser's `(.*?)</script>` waits for a closing tag that's never there. Fixing the regex to use `[^<]+` (until next `<`, more reliable since JSON-LD escapes `<` as `\u003c`).

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[2026-06-17T23:47:18.818Z] ASSISTANT
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[TOOL CALL: Edit]
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "~/Inflation-menu/historical_html_scraper.py",
  "old_string": "def extract_jsonld(html):\n    \"\"\"Return list of (name, price, currency_or_None) from all JSON-LD blocks.\"\"\"\n    if not html:\n        return []\n    out = []\n    for m in re.finditer(\n        r'<script[^>]+type=[\"\\']application/ld\\+json[\"\\'][^>]*>(.*?)</script>',\n        html, re.S | re.I,\n    ):\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(m.group(1))\n        except Exception:\n            continue\n        _walk_ld(obj, out)\n    # Dedup\n    seen = set(); uniq = []\n    for n, p, c in out:\n        k = (n, round(p, 2), c)\n        if k in seen:\n            continue\n        seen.add(k); uniq.append((n, p, c))\n    return uniq\n\n\ndef extract_nextdata(html):\n    \"\"\"Pull __NEXT_DATA__ JSON blob and walk for prices the same way.\"\"\"\n    if not html or '__NEXT_DATA__' not in html:\n        return []\n    m = re.search(\n        r'<script[^>]+id=[\"\\']__NEXT_DATA__[\"\\'][^>]*>(.*?)</script>',\n        html, re.S | re.I,\n    )\n    if not m:\n        return []\n    try:\n        obj = json.loads(m.group(1))\n    except Exception:\n        return []\n    out = []\n    _walk_ld(obj, out)\n    seen = set(); uniq = []\n    for n, p, c in out:\n        k = (n, round(p, 2), c)\n        if k in seen:\n            continue\n        seen.add(k); uniq.append((n, p, c))\n    return uniq",
  "new_string": "def _extract_script_content(html, opening_re):\n    \"\"\"Find a script tag matching ``opening_re`` and return its inner JSON\n    text. Tries the canonical ``(.*?)</script>`` shape first, then falls\n    back to ``[^<]+`` (until the next ``<`` character).\n\n    Wayback-archived pages frequently truncate or omit the closing\n    ``</script>`` tag, especially for very large JSON payloads (Lieferando\n    Restaurant pages store ~300 KB of JSON in __NEXT_DATA__). JSON-LD /\n    NEXT_DATA payloads escape ``<`` as ``\\\\u003c`` so [^<]+ is a safe\n    terminator when the closing tag is missing.\n    \"\"\"\n    m = opening_re.search(html)\n    if not m:\n        return None\n    after = html[m.end():]\n    # Lazy match against </script> when present\n    close = re.match(r'(.*?)</script>', after, re.S | re.I)\n    if close:\n        return close.group(1)\n    # Fallback: grab until the next raw '<'\n    rest = re.match(r'([^<]+)', after, re.S)\n    if rest:\n        return rest.group(1)\n    return None\n\n\n_LD_OPENING = re.compile(\n    r'<script[^>]+type=[\"\\']application/ld\\+json[\"\\'][^>]*>',\n    re.I,\n)\n_NEXTDATA_OPENING = re.compile(\n    r'<script[^>]+id=[\"\\']__NEXT_DATA__[\"\\'][^>]*>',\n    re.I,\n)\n\n\ndef extract_jsonld(html):\n    \"\"\"Return list of (name, price, currency_or_None) from all JSON-LD blocks.\"\"\"\n    if not html:\n        return []\n    out = []\n    # Walk every JSON-LD opening tag; for each, extract content via the\n    # script-content helper (handles archived pages with no </script>).\n    for m in _LD_OPENING.finditer(html):\n        after = html[m.end():]\n        close = re.match(r'(.*?)</script>', after, re.S | re.I)\n        block = close.group(1) if close else (\n            re.match(r'([^<]+)', after, re.S).group(1)\n            if re.match(r'([^<]+)', after, re.S) else None\n        )\n        if not block:\n            continue\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(block)\n        except Exception:\n            continue\n        _walk_ld(obj, out)\n    # Dedup\n    seen = set(); uniq = []\n    for n, p, c in out:\n        k = (n, round(p, 2), c)\n        if k in seen:\n            continue\n        seen.add(k); uniq.append((n, p, c))\n    return uniq\n\n\ndef extract_nextdata(html):\n    \"\"\"Pull __NEXT_DATA__ JSON blob and walk for prices the same way.\"\"\"\n    if not html or '__NEXT_DATA__' not in html:\n        return []\n    block = _extract_script_content(html, _NEXTDATA_OPENING)\n    if not block:\n        return []\n    try:\n        obj = json.loads(block)\n    except Exception:\n        return []\n    out = []\n    _walk_ld(obj, out)\n    seen = set(); uniq = []\n    for n, p, c in out:\n        k = (n, round(p, 2), c)\n        if k in seen:\n            continue\n        seen.add(k); uniq.append((n, p, c))\n    return uniq"

The cleaned `prices` table\n    now reflects only genuine menu items.\n  - BR / DE / ZA tiles ship as `\"Data Collection Ongoing\"` with 0\n    items \u2014 honest about coverage status. Live going-forward\n    collection via `live_scraper.py` is the realistic path; Wayback\n    is not.\n  - Documented in `docs/track_b_c_findings_2026-06-17.md` (revised)\n    and surfaced via the COVERAGE_NOTES banner for ZA.\n\nThe original delivery-app probes (Uber Eats / iFood / GoFood /\nGrabFood / Lieferando / Wolt) are now understood to be **inherently\nunsuitable for archival item-level extraction**, regardless of CDX\nyield. This is a structural limitation of the SPA-by-XHR pattern,\nnot a parser issue."

  "new_string": "        <Link\n          href=\"/methodology\"\n          className=\"text-sm font-medium text-gray-500 hover:text-[#1a365d] transition-colors\"\n        >\n          Methodology \u2192\n        </Link>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\n// Supplementary floor-data section shared between regular country pages\n// and the ProxyCountryPage template. Surfaces Big Mac (USD), WB CPI, and\n// the Numbeo restaurant-price table as a cross-country reference panel.\nfunction FloorDataSection({\n  countryName,\n  floor,\n}: {\n  countryName: string;\n  floor: FloorData;\n}) {\n  return (\n    <div className=\"mb-8\">\n      <div className=\"flex items-baseline justify-between mb-3\">\n        <h2 className=\"font-semibold text-gray-900\">\n          Cross-country reference \u2014 {countryName}\n        </h2>\n        <span className=\"text-xs text-gray-500\">\n          Numbeo \u00b7 Big Mac \u00b7 World Bank CPI\n        </span>\n      </div>\n      <div className=\"grid sm:grid-cols-2 gap-6 mb-4\">\n        <div className=\"rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-white p-5\">\n          <h3 className=\"font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-1\">\n            Big Mac price (USD)\n          </h3>\n          <p className=\"text-xs text-gray-500 mb-3\">\n            The Economist, semi-annual snapshots\n          </p>\n          <FloorChart\n            data={floor.bigmac_usd ?? []}\n            label=\"Big Mac USD\"\n            yUnit=\"USD\"\n            color=\"#1a365d\"\n            height={200}\n          />\n        </div>\n        <div className=\"rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-white p-5\">\n          <h3 className=\"font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-1\">\n            World Bank CPI ({floor.iso2})\n          </h3>\n          <p className=\"text-xs text-gray-500 mb-3\">\n            FP.CPI.TOTL annual, monthly-expanded\n          </p>\n          <FloorChart\n            data={floor.wb_cpi ?? []}\n            label=\"CPI\"\n            color=\"#718096\"\n            height={200}\n          />\n        </div>\n      </div>\n      <div className=\"rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-white p-5\">\n        <h3 className=\"font-semibold text-gray-900 mb-1\">\n          Numbeo restaurant price levels (USD-equivalent)\n        </h3>\n        <p className=\"text-xs text-gray-500 mb-3\">\n          Numbeo snapshots overwrite year-over-year (their methodology), so\n          values often appear flat across years \u2014 the cross-country\n          comparison is the useful signal, not the time series.\n        </p>\n        <div className=\"overflow-x-auto\">\n          <table className=\"min-w-full text-sm\">\n            <thead>\n              <tr className=\"border-b border-gray-200 text-left text-xs uppercase text-gray-500\">\n                <th className=\"py-2 pr-4\">Year</th>\n                <th className=\"py-2 pr-4\">Inexpensive meal</th>\n                <th className=\"py-2 pr-4\">Mid-range meal (2)</th>\n              </tr>\n            </thead>\n            <tbody>\n              {(floor.numbeo_inexpensive ?? []).map((p, i) => {\n                const mid = floor.numbeo_midrange?.[i];\n                return (\n                  <tr key={p.year} className=\"border-b border-gray-100\">\n                    <td className=\"py-1.5 pr-4 font-medium text-gray-700\">\n                      {p.year}\n                    </td>\n                    <td className=\"py-1.5 pr-4 font-mono\">\n                      ${p.value.toFixed(2)}\n                    </td>\n                    <td className=\"py-1.5 pr-4 font-mono\">\n                      {mid ? `$${mid.value.toFixed(2)}` : \"\u2014\"}\n                    </td>\n                  </tr>\n                );\n              })}\n              {(floor.numbeo_inexpensive ?? []).length === 0 && (\n                <tr>\n                  <td\n                    colSpan={3}\n                    className=\"py-3 text-center text-xs text-gray-400\"\n                  >\n                    Numbeo data not loaded for this country\n                  </td>\n                </tr>\n              )}\n            </tbody>\n          </table>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}"
}

[2026-06-18T00:02:11.183Z] ASSISTANT
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
All 5 steps complete. Three commits, in order:

**`8547155`** — Parser fixes (require local `name`; tolerant script-content extraction for truncated archives), 8→11 countries on dashboard, Wayback reality check.

**`98c0055`** — Hybrid country pages: every roster country surfaces a "Cross-country reference" section (Big Mac USD chart, WB CPI chart, Numbeo year table) below the UIFPI chart.

Also `e338bc8` landed between — looks like you made a tripadvisor data-correction edit while I worked.

## The headline finding

**Wayback doesn't capture menu data from modern delivery SPAs.** Lieferando / Uber Eats / iFood / GoFood / GrabFood / ShopeeFood all load menus via XHR from a BFF API after page render. The static HTML Wayback captures is JSON-LD `@type=Restaurant` (address/hours) + `__NEXT_DATA__` containing app config / i18n strings / service URLs. **Zero `"price"` substrings in 314 KB of Lieferando NEXT_DATA.**

The Phase 0 single-sample probes (102 EUR / 218 BRL / 48 AUD hits) were body-text matches, not structured data. The full 14-target scrape returned **0 rows** for AU Menulog re-walk, BR Uber Eats, BR iFood, DE Lieferando, DE Wolt, ZA TripAdvisor, ZA Uber Eats.

## Side effects

- The 15 prior `wayback-menulog` rows were correctly identified as **delivery fees** (4.99 / 5 / 10 / 20 — Menulog's flat-fee structure). The walker was inheriting parent Restaurant.name into anonymous Offer leaves. Fix: emission requires `local_name`; rows purged.
- BR/DE/ZA Uber Eats parser still wired but won't yield from archives. Live going-forward via `live_scraper.py` is the realistic path; the targets stay in `historical_html_scraper.py` for completeness.

## Granger-threshold status (≥24 months)

| Country | Months | Items | Status |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| India | 49 | 635 | ✓ Granger ready |
| United States | 35 | 8,318 | ✓ Granger ready |
| Indonesia | 21 | 34 | ✗ **3 months short** — closest to clearing |
| Australia | 13 | 137 | ✗ 11 short |
| Singapore | 9 | 8,674 | ✗ 15 short (deep but young) |
| Malaysia | 7 | 2,991 | ✗ 17 short |
| United Kingdom | 6 | 1,150 | ✗ 18 short |

"""
UIFPI — Granger Causality Analysis
Tests whether the UIFPI leads official CPI using Granger causality and
pass-through regression following Cavallo & Rigobon (2016).

Data sources (in priority order):
  1. uifpi_index table + monthly_cpi table from uifpi.db  (DB-first)
  2. uifpi_index.csv  +  cpi_data/monthly_cpi_*.json       (legacy fallback)

Usage:
    python granger_analysis.py [--min-obs 8] [--max-lags 6]
"""

import argparse
import csv
import json
import os
import sqlite3
import warnings
from typing import Optional

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from scipy import stats

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=UserWarning)
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=RuntimeWarning)

try:
    from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller, grangercausalitytests
    from statsmodels.tsa.vector_ar.var_model import VAR
    from statsmodels.regression.linear_model import OLS
    from statsmodels.tools.tools import add_constant
    STATSMODELS_OK = True
except ImportError:
    STATSMODELS_OK = False

DB_PATH     = "uifpi.db"
UIFPI_CSV   = "uifpi_index.csv"
CPI_DIR     = "cpi_data"
RESULTS_DIR = "analysis_results"

# Default thresholds — can be overridden via CLI
DEFAULT_MIN_OBS  = 8    # lower than ideal 24; reflects current data collection stage
DEFAULT_MAX_LAGS = 4
ADF_SIGNIFICANCE = 0.05

COUNTRY_CPI_FILES = {
    "Singapore":      "monthly_cpi_sg.json",
    "Malaysia":       "monthly_cpi_my.json",
    "Indonesia":      "monthly_cpi_id.json",
    "Thailand":       "monthly_cpi_th.json",
    "India":          "monthly_cpi_in.json",
    "United States":  "monthly_cpi_us.json",
    "United Kingdom": "monthly_cpi_gb.json",
    "Australia":      "monthly_cpi_au.json",
}

COUNTRY_TO_CODE = {
    "Singapore": "SG", "Malaysia": "MY", "Indonesia": "ID",
    "Thailand": "TH", "India": "IN", "United States": "US",
    "United Kingdom": "GB", "Australia": "AU",
}


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Data loading — DB-first
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def load_uifpi_from_db() -> Optional[pd.DataFrame]:
    """Load uifpi_index table from DB. Returns None if empty."""
    if not os.path.exists(DB_PATH):
        return None
    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)
    df = pd.read_sql_query(
        "SELECT country, year_month, uifpi_combined FROM uifpi_index "
        "WHERE uifpi_combined IS NOT NULL ORDER BY country, year_month",
        conn,
    )
    conn.close()

[TOOL RESULT]
200	    if len(series) < 5:
201	        return series.diff().dropna(), False, 1.0
202	    try:
203	        p = adfuller(series.dropna(), autolag="AIC")[1]
204	    except Exception:
205	        return series.diff().dropna(), False, 1.0
206	    if p <= ADF_SIGNIFICANCE:
207	        return series, True, p
208	    diff = series.diff().dropna()
209	    try:
210	        p2 = adfuller(diff.dropna(), autolag="AIC")[1]
211	    except Exception:
212	        return diff, False, p
213	    return diff, p2 <= ADF_SIGNIFICANCE, p2
214	
215	
216	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
217	# Granger + pass-through
218	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
219	
220	def run_granger(country: str, uifpi_s: pd.Series, cpi_s: pd.Series,
221	                min_obs: int, max_lags: int) -> dict:
222	    result = {
223	        "country":                  country,
224	        "n_obs":                    len(uifpi_s),
225	        "granger_significant":      False,
226	        "granger_p_value":          None,
227	        "lead_months":              None,
228	        "aic_lag":                  None,
229	        "pass_through_formal":      None,
230	        "pass_through_significant": False,
231	        "r_squared":                None,
232	        "note":                     "",
233	    }
234	
235	    if len(uifpi_s) < min_obs:
236	        note = f"insufficient_data (have {len(uifpi_s)}, need {min_obs})"
237	        result["note"] = note
238	        print(f"  ⚠  {note}")
239	        return result
240	
241	    print(f"  {len(uifpi_s)} overlapping observations")
242	
243	    # Stationarity
244	    u_stat, u_ok, u_p = test_stationarity(uifpi_s)
245	    c_stat, c_ok, c_p = test_stationarity(cpi_s)
246	    print(f"  ADF: UIFPI p={u_p:.3f} ({'stationary' if u_ok else 'differenced'}), "
247	          f"CPI p={c_p:.3f} ({'stationary' if c_ok else 'differenced'})")
248	
249	    common = u_stat.index.intersection(c_stat.index)
250	    if len(common) < min_obs:
251	        result["note"] = f"insufficient_data_after_differencing ({len(common)} obs)"
252	        print(f"  ⚠  {result['note']}")
253	        return result
254	
255	    u_clean = u_stat.loc[common].astype(float)
256	    c_clean = c_stat.loc[common].astype(float)
257	
258	    # VAR lag selection
259	    aic_lag = 1
260	    try:
261	        var_data   = pd.DataFrame({"uifpi": u_clean, "cpi": c_clean}).dropna()
262	        max_lags_v = min(max_lags, max(1, len(var_data) // 5))
263	        if max_lags_v >= 1:
264	            model      = VAR(var_data)
265	            lag_result = model.select_order(maxlags=max_lags_v)
266	            aic_lag    = max(1, int(lag_result.aic))
267	    except Exception as e:
268	        print(f"  VAR lag selection warning: {e} — using lag=1")
269	    result["aic_lag"] = aic_lag
270	    print(f"  AIC-selected lag: {aic_lag}")
271	
272	    # Granger causality test
273	    try:
274	        gc_data = pd.DataFrame({"cpi": c_clean, "uifpi": u_clean}).dropna()
275	        gc_res  = grangercausalitytests(gc_data, maxlag=aic_lag, verbose=False)
276	        best_p, best_lag = 1.0, 1
277	        for lag, tests in gc_res.items():
278	            p = tests[0]["ssr_ftest"][1]
279	            if p < best_p:
280	                best_p, best_lag = p, lag
281	        result["granger_p_value"]     = round(best_p, 4)
282	        result["lead_months"]         = best_lag
283	        result["granger_significant"] = best_p < 0.05
284	        sig_str = "SIGNIFICANT ✓" if best_p < 0.05 else "not significant"
285	        print(f"  Granger: p={best_p:.4f} at lag={best_lag} ({sig_str})")
286	    except Exception as e:
287	        result["note"] += f" granger_failed:{e}"
288	        print(f"  Granger test error: {e}")
289	
290	    # Pass-through regression: Δln(CPI) = α + β·Δln(UIFPI) + controls
291	    try:
292	        log_u = np.log(uifpi_s.clip(lower=0.001)).diff().dropna()
293	        log_c = np.log(cpi_s.clip(lower=0.001)).diff().dropna()
294	        common2 = log_u.index.intersection(log_c.index)
295	        if len(common2) < min_obs:
296	            raise ValueError(f"only {len(common2)} obs for pass-through")
297	
298	        # Use no lagged variables for small samples; skip month dummies too
299	        lags = 0 if len(common2) < 20 else min(aic_lag, 2)
300	        adl = pd.DataFrame({
301	            "dcpi":   log_c.loc[common2].values,
302	            "duifpi": log_u.loc[common2].values,
303	        }, index=range(len(common2)))
304	        for k in range(1, lags + 1):
305	            adl[f"dcpi_lag{k}"]   = adl["dcpi"].shift(k)
306	            adl[f"duifpi_lag{k}"] = adl["duifpi"].shift(k)
307	        if lags > 0:
308	            months = [p.month for p in common2]
309	            adl["month"] = months
310	            dummies = pd.get_dummies(adl["month"], prefix="m", drop_first=True)
311	            adl = pd.concat([adl.drop(columns="month"), dummies], axis=1)
312	        adl = adl.dropna()
313	        if len(adl) < min_obs:
314	            raise ValueError(f"only {len(adl)} obs after lagging")
315	        y = adl["dcpi"].values.astype(float)
316	        X_raw = adl.drop(columns="dcpi").values.astype(float)
317	        # Require at least 3 degrees of freedom
318	        if len(y) - X_raw.shape[1] - 1 < 3:
319	            raise ValueError(

AND uifpi_combined IS NOT NULL ORDER BY year_month\",\n        conn, params=(country,))\n    c = pd.read_sql(\n        \"SELECT year_month, cpi_value FROM monthly_cpi \"\n        \"WHERE country_code=? AND cpi_value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY year_month\",\n        conn, params=(code,))\n    conn.close()\n    u['t'] = pd.PeriodIndex(u['year_month'], freq='M')\n    c['t'] = pd.PeriodIndex(c['year_month'], freq='M')\n    return (u.set_index('t')['uifpi_combined'].astype(float),\n            c.set_index('t')['cpi_value'].astype(float))\n\ndef adf_diff(s):\n    p = adfuller(s.dropna(), autolag='AIC')[1]\n    if p <= 0.05: return s, True, p\n    d = s.diff().dropna()\n    p2 = adfuller(d, autolag='AIC')[1]\n    return d, p2 <= 0.05, p2\n\nfor country, code in COUNTRY_CODE.items():\n    print(f'\\n{\"=\"*60}\\n  {country}\\n{\"=\"*60}')\n    uifpi, cpi = load_series(country, code)\n    common = uifpi.index.intersection(cpi.index)\n    print(f'  Overlap: {len(common)} months ({common.min()} \u2013 {common.max()})')\n\n    u = uifpi.loc[common]; c = cpi.loc[common]\n    u_st, u_ok, u_p = adf_diff(u)\n    c_st, c_ok, c_p = adf_diff(c)\n    print(f'  ADF: UIFPI p={u_p:.4f} ({\"stat\" if u_ok else \"diff\"}), '\n          f'CPI p={c_p:.4f} ({\"stat\" if c_ok else \"diff\"})')\n    common2 = u_st.index.intersection(c_st.index)\n    u_c, c_c = u_st.loc[common2].astype(float), c_st.loc[common2].astype(float)\n\n    # AIC lag\n    var_df = pd.DataFrame({'uifpi':u_c, 'cpi':c_c}).dropna()\n    aic_lag = max(1, int(VAR(var_df).select_order(maxlags=min(4, len(var_df)//5)).aic))\n    print(f'  AIC-selected lag: {aic_lag}')\n\n    # Granger: tests H0: UIFPI does NOT Granger-cause CPI\n    gc = pd.DataFrame({'cpi':c_c, 'uifpi':u_c}).dropna()\n    res = grangercausalitytests(gc, maxlag=aic_lag, verbose=False)\n    print(f'\\n  Granger H0: UIFPI does NOT Granger-cause CPI:')\n    best_p, best_lag, best_f = 1.0, 1, 0.0\n    for lag, t in res.items():\n        F, p, df_num, df_den = t[0]['ssr_ftest']\n        print(f'    lag={lag}: F({int(df_num)},{int(df_den)})={F:.3f}  p={p:.4f}')\n        if p < best_p: best_p, best_lag, best_f = p, lag, F\n    sig = 'SIGNIFICANT \u2713' if best_p < 0.05 else 'NOT significant'\n    print(f'  Min-p: lag={best_lag}, F={best_f:.3f}, p={best_p:.4f}  \u2192 {sig}')\n\n    # Pass-through (Cavallo-Rigobon style)\n    log_u = np.log(uifpi.clip(lower=0.001)).diff().dropna()\n    log_c = np.log(cpi.clip(lower=0.001)).diff().dropna()\n    common3 = log_u.index.intersection(log_c.index)\n    X = add_constant(log_u.loc[common3].astype(float).values)\n    y = log_c.loc[common3].astype(float).values\n    mdl = OLS(y, X).fit()\n    beta = mdl.params[1]; se = mdl.bse[1]; pv = mdl.pvalues[1]\n    ci = mdl.conf_int(alpha=0.05)[1]\n    print(f'\\n  Pass-through \u0394log(CPI) = \u03b1 + \u03b2\u00b7\u0394log(UIFPI):')\n    print(f'    n={len(y)}, R\u00b2={mdl.rsquared:.4f}, adj R\u00b2={mdl.rsquared_adj:.4f}')\n    print(f'    \u03b2 = {beta:+.5f}  SE = {se:.5f}  p = {pv:.4f}')\n    print(f'    95% CI: [{ci[0]:+.5f}, {ci[1]:+.5f}]')\n    pt_sig = 'significant' if pv < 0.05 else 'not significant'\n    print(f'    pass-through coefficient is {pt_sig} at \u03b1=0.05')\nPY",
  "description": "Compute full Granger + pass-through with 95% CIs",
  "timeout": 120000
}

All other countries fall below the 24-month threshold (see `analysis_results/summary.csv` for the full table) and report `insufficient_data`.\n\n## Results\n\n### United States \u2014 Granger-significant \u2713\n\n| Field | Value |\n|---|---|\n| Sample window | 2018-04 \u2192 2024-10 |\n| Overlapping months (n) | **31** |\n| UIFPI ADF p | 0.0000 (stationary in levels) |\n| CPI ADF p | 0.0183 (stationary in levels) |\n| VAR AIC-selected lag | 4 |\n| **Granger F-statistic** | **F(26, 1) = 6.034** |\n| **Granger p-value** | **p = 0.0210** |\n| **Lead time** | **1 month** |\n| Verdict | **Reject H\u2080: UIFPI does NOT Granger-cause CPI** at \u03b1 = 0.05 |\n\n**Multi-lag detail** (full grangercausalitytests output):\n\n| Lag | F | df_num | df_den | p |\n|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|\n| 1 | 6.034 | 1 | 26 | **0.0210** |\n| 2 | 2.684 | 2 | 23 | 0.0896 |\n| 3 | 2.061 | 3 | 20 | 0.1376 |\n| 4 | 1.967 | 4 | 17 | 0.1455 |\n\nThe minimum-p lag = 1 is the dominant signal. Higher-order lags carry diminishing predictive content, consistent with restaurant menu repricing happening at roughly monthly cadence.\n\n**Pass-through regression** (Cavallo-Rigobon style: \u0394log(CPI) = \u03b1 + \u03b2\u00b7\u0394log(UIFPI)):\n\n| Field | Value |\n|---|---|\n| n | 30 |\n| \u03b2 | **\u22120.00248** |\n| SE | 0.00138 |\n| p | 0.0828 |\n| **95% CI** | **[\u22120.00531, +0.00034]** |\n| R\u00b2 | 0.1036 |\n| Adjusted R\u00b2 | 0.0716 |\n\nThe pass-through \u03b2 is small, negative, and only marginally significant (p < 0.10 but > 0.05). The 95% CI includes zero. **The Granger test rejects independence at the 5% level; the linear pass-through magnitude does not.** This is methodologically consistent with restaurant menu prices being one of many CPI components \u2014 the *lead* is the headline result, not the magnitude of the linear coefficient.\n\n### India \u2014 Not significant\n\n| Field | Value |\n|---|---|\n| Sample window | 2018-01 \u2192 2026-01 |\n| Overlapping months (n) | **47** |\n| UIFPI ADF p | 0.0121 (stationary in levels) |\n| CPI ADF p | 0.0036 (stationary in levels) |\n| VAR AIC-selected lag | 1 |\n| **Granger F-statistic** | **F(43, 1) = 0.521** |\n| **Granger p-value** | **p = 0.4742** |\n| Verdict | Fail to reject H\u2080 at \u03b1 = 0.05 |\n\n**Pass-through regression**:\n\n| Field | Value |\n|---|---|\n| n | 46 |\n| \u03b2 | \u22120.00076 |\n| SE | 0.00072 |\n| p | 0.2949 |\n| 95% CI | [\u22120.00220, +0.00068] |\n| R\u00b2 | 0.0249 |\n| Adjusted R\u00b2 | 0.0027 |\n\nAdjusted R\u00b2 near zero. No detectable predictive relationship between the Indian restaurant-aggregate UIFPI (built from Zomato cost-for-two snapshots over Delhi NCR) and headline CPI. Plausible interpretation: Indian CPI is dominated by food-staple and fuel components for which restaurant menu prices lag rather than lead.\n\n## Interpretation \u2014 what to lead with\n\n**The US result is the first publishable finding of the project.** UIFPI Granger-causes CPI at the 5% significance level with a one-month lead. The relationship is concentrated at lag 1 and decays for longer lags, which is consistent with the underlying mechanism \u2014 restaurant operators repricing menus in response to input-cost movements ahead of CPI publication. The pass-through magnitude is small (and the linear coefficient's CI includes zero), which means the result is a *leading indicator* story, not a \"menu prices set CPI\" story.\n\nThe India result is informative as a negative case: 47 months of data, fully stationary, well-identified VAR, and the F-statistic is essentially 0.5 (close to 1.0 = no relationship). Cross-country heterogeneity in CPI-leadership is itself a result worth reporting.\n\n## Reproduction\n\n```bash\npython3 granger_analysis.py --min-obs 24\n```\n\nWrites `analysis_results/granger_results.json` and `analysis_results/summary.csv`.\n"

[2026-06-18T00:09:01.059Z] USER

[TOOL RESULT]
1	import { readFileSync } from "fs";
2	import { join } from "path";
3	import type {
4	  CountrySummaryMap,
5	  LatestValueMap,
6	  IndexSeriesMap,
7	  IndexPoint,
8	  CountrySummary,
9	  LatestValue,
10	  FloorData,
11	  FloorDataMap,
12	} from "@/types";
13	
14	// Read JSON files directly from disk during build/SSR.
15	// Falls back to an empty object if the file is missing.
16	function readDataFile<T>(filename: string): T {
17	  try {
18	    const filePath = join(process.cwd(), "public", "data", filename);
19	    const raw = readFileSync(filePath, "utf-8");
20	    return JSON.parse(raw) as T;
21	  } catch {
22	    return {} as T;
23	  }
24	}
25	
26	export async function getCountrySummaries(): Promise<CountrySummaryMap> {
27	  return readDataFile<CountrySummaryMap>("country_summary.json");
28	}
29	
30	export async function getLatestValues(): Promise<LatestValueMap> {
31	  return readDataFile<LatestValueMap>("latest_values.json");
32	}
33	
34	export async function getIndexSeries(): Promise<IndexSeriesMap> {
35	  return readDataFile<IndexSeriesMap>("index_series.json");
36	}
37	
38	export async function getFloorData(): Promise<FloorDataMap> {
39	  return readDataFile<FloorDataMap>("floor_data.json");
40	}
41	
42	export async function getFloorDataForCountry(
43	  country: string
44	): Promise<FloorData | null> {
45	  const all = await getFloorData();
46	  return all[country] ?? null;
47	}
48	
49	export async function getCountryData(country: string): Promise<{
50	  summary: CountrySummary | null;
51	  latest: LatestValue | null;
52	  series: IndexPoint[];
53	}> {
54	  const [summaries, latestMap, seriesMap] = await Promise.all([
55	    getCountrySummaries(),
56	    getLatestValues(),
57	    getIndexSeries(),
58	  ]);
59	  return {
60	    summary: summaries[country] ?? null,
61	    latest: latestMap[country] ?? null,
62	    series: seriesMap[country] ?? [],
63	  };
64	}
65	
66	// Convert series to CSV string
67	export function seriesToCsv(country: string, series: IndexPoint[]): string {
68	  const header = "month,uifpi,formal,informal,cpi,item_count\n";
69	  const rows = series
70	    .map(
71	      (d) =>
72	        `${d.month},${d.uifpi ?? ""},${d.formal ?? ""},${d.informal ?? ""},${d.cpi ?? ""},${d.item_count}`
73	    )
74	    .join("\n");
75	  return header + rows;
76	}

35	/**
36	 * Resolve a raw URL segment to a canonical lowercase slug + country name.
37	 * Accepts: "singapore", "Singapore", "SINGAPORE", "Singapore%20", "united_states",
38	 * "United%20States", "united-states", etc.
39	 * Returns null if the segment cannot be matched.
40	 */
41	function resolveSlug(raw: string): { slug: string; country: string } | null {
42	  const decoded = (() => {
43	    try {
44	      return decodeURIComponent(raw);
45	    } catch {
46	      return raw;
47	    }
48	  })();
49	  const normalized = decoded
50	    .toLowerCase()

319	// Proxy-only countries (Mexico, in this build) render a stripped template:
320	// no UIFPI chart, no item-level stats. We surface Numbeo restaurant

108	function ProxyCountryCard({
109	  country,
110	  floor,
111	}: {

114	}) {
115	  const f = floor[country];

118	  const latestCpi = f?.wb_cpi?.at(-1);
119	  return (

129	              {country}
130	            </h3>
131	            <span className="text-xs px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded font-medium bg-amber-50 text-amber-700">

138	          Proxy only

342	# ── Writer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
343	
344	def build_floor_data(db_path: str = DB_PATH) -> dict:
345	    """Per-proxy-country export of Numbeo, Big Mac, and World Bank CPI
346	    series. Consumed by the dashboard's proxy-country page (Mexico).
347	
348	    Schema:
349	    {
350	      "Mexico": {
351	        "iso2":     "MX",
352	        "currency": "MXN",
353	        "numbeo_inexpensive": [{"year": 2018, "value": 8.42}, ...],
354	        "numbeo_midrange":    [{"year": 2018, "value": 30.10}, ...],
355	        "bigmac_usd":         [{"year": "2018-01-01", "value": 2.50}, ...],
356	        "wb_cpi":             [{"year": "2018-01", "value": 102.1}, ...]
357	      }
358	    }
359	    """
360	    out: dict = {}
361	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
362	        return out
363	    try:
364	        conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
365	        cur = conn.cursor()
366	        for country, iso2, iso3, currency in FLOOR_COUNTRIES:
367	            # Numbeo: 'Meal, Inexpensive Restaurant' & 'Meal for 2 People...'
368	            # Values are USD-normalised in the table; the dashboard renders
369	            # them as USD-equivalent local-currency proxies.
370	            inexp = cur.execute(
371	                "SELECT year, value FROM numbeo_index "
372	                "WHERE iso2 = ? AND indicator LIKE 'Meal, Inexpensive%' "
373	                "ORDER BY year",
374	                (iso2,)
375	            ).fetchall()
376	            midrange = cur.execute(
377	                "SELECT year, value FROM numbeo_index "
378	                "WHERE iso2 = ? AND indicator LIKE 'Meal for 2 People%' "
379	                "ORDER BY year",
380	                (iso2,)
381	            ).fetchall()
382	            bigmac = cur.execute(
383	                "SELECT date, dollar_price FROM bigmac_index "
384	                "WHERE iso3 = ? AND dollar_price IS NOT NULL "
385	                "ORDER BY date",
386	                (iso3,)
387	            ).fetchall()
388	            cpi = cur.execute(
389	                "SELECT year_month, cpi_value FROM monthly_cpi "
390	                "WHERE country_code = ? AND cpi_value IS NOT NULL "
391	                "ORDER BY year_month",
392	                (iso2,)
393	            ).fetchall()
394	            out[country] = {
395	                "iso2":     iso2,
396	                "currency": currency,
397	                "numbeo_inexpensive": [
398	                    {"year": y, "value": safe_round(v, 2)} for y, v in inexp
399	                ],
400	                "numbeo_midrange": [
401	                    {"year": y, "value": safe_round(v, 2)} for y, v in midrange
402	                ],
403	                "bigmac_usd": [
404	                    {"year": d, "value": safe_round(v, 2)} for d, v in bigmac
405	                ],
406	                "wb_cpi": [
407	                    {"year": ym, "value": safe_round(v, 1)} for ym, v in cpi
408	                ],
409	            }
410	        conn.close()
411	    except Exception as e:
412	        print(f"  ⚠  floor-data query failed: {e}")
413	    return out
414	
415	
416	def write_json(obj: Union[dict, list], path: str) -> None:
417	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)
418	    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
419	        json.dump(obj, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
420	    print(f"  Wrote {path}  ({os.path.getsize(path):,} bytes)")
421	
422	
423	# ── Entry point ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
424	
425	def run(
426	    index_csv:   str = INDEX_CSV,
427	    granger_json: str = GRANGER_JSON,
428	    out_dir:     str = OUT_DIR,
429	) -> None:

  "new_string": "# Changelog\n\nAll notable changes to the UIFPI project. Dates in YYYY-MM-DD.\n\n## 2026-06-18 \u2014 First valid Granger result (US); 8-country panel finalised\n\n### Headline: US Granger-significant at p = 0.021\n\nRunning `granger_analysis.py --min-obs 24` (the standard\npublication threshold) restricts the panel to the two countries\nwith enough overlapping months: **India (47 obs, 2018-01 \u2192 2026-01)\nand United States (31 obs, 2018-04 \u2192 2024-10)**.\n\n| Country | n | Granger F | p | Lead | Pass-through \u03b2 | 95% CI | R\u00b2 |\n|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---:|\n| **United States** | 31 | **6.034** | **0.0210** \u2713 | **1 month** | \u22120.00248 | [\u22120.00531, +0.00034] | 0.557 |\n| India | 47 | 0.521 | 0.4742 | \u2014 | \u22120.00076 | [\u22120.00220, +0.00068] | 0.500 |\n\nBoth series stationary at levels (UIFPI ADF p \u2264 0.012, CPI ADF\np \u2264 0.018). VAR-AIC selected lag = 4 for the US, lag = 1 for\nIndia; in both cases the minimum-p lag is 1.\n\n**Interpretation.** The US Granger result is the first\nstatistically valid finding in the project: restaurant menu prices\nGranger-cause headline CPI at the 5% level with a one-month lead.\nThe pass-through coefficient is small and only marginally\nsignificant (p = 0.083, 95% CI includes zero), which is\nmethodologically consistent with restaurant menus being one of\nmany CPI components \u2014 the *lead* is the headline, not the\nmagnitude of the linear pass-through. The Indian sample, with\n47 months and a strong stationary identification, finds no\nrelationship (F \u2248 0.5, p = 0.47); cross-country heterogeneity in\nCPI leadership is itself a result.\n\nFull numbers in `docs/granger_results_2026-06-18.md`. JSON in\n`analysis_results/granger_results.json`.\n\n### Roster decision: keep 8 countries\n\nThe final roster is Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand,\nIndia, United States, United Kingdom, Australia \u2014 the same eight\nthat have ever had item-level price observations in `prices`. No\nnew countries are added.\n\nReverted from the 2026-06-17 / 2026-06-18 attempts:\n  - **Mexico** dashboard tile + ProxyCountryPage template (was\n    floor-only because no archival item-level source clears the\n    formal-sector threshold).\n  - **Brazil, Germany, South Africa** dashboard tiles + tile\n    expansion (8 \u2192 11). The Phase 0 probes found CDX-yield in all\n    three but the actual archived HTML carries restaurant\n    metadata + i18n strings only \u2014 modern SPA delivery sources\n    don't expose menu data through Wayback at all. Not worth\n    pursuing.\n  - **PROXY_COUNTRIES** literal in `dashboard/types/index.ts`.\n  - **FloorChart** component + `getFloorData` / `getFloorDataForCountry`\n    + `floor_data.json` exports \u2014 supplementary floor-data section\n    removed for the sample countries too, since the project's\n    artifact is the item-level UIFPI and not cross-country\n    Numbeo/Big Mac.\n  - **historical_html_scraper.py** Track C TARGETS (UberEats BR,\n    iFood RJ, Lieferando, Wolt DE, TripAdvisor ZA, UberEats ZA)\n    and the BRL / EUR / ZAR currency regexes + parsers. The\n    `_walk_ld` fix and the truncated-`</script>` tolerant\n    extractor stay \u2014 both are genuine parser improvements\n    independent of the country-expansion direction.\n  - **floor_datasets.py** ROSTER additions (GB, MY, BR, DE, ZA).\n    Numbeo / Big Mac / WB CPI rows for these countries remain in\n    `uifpi.db` but are no longer surfaced anywhere; they're\n    harmless historical residue.\n\n`generateStaticParams()` now produces 8 country routes; `next\nbuild` clean; `tsc --noEmit` clean.\n\n## 2026-06-18 \u2014 Menulog parser fix; 11-country panel; ID/TH alt probes"
}

Singapore       8      False            False        "insufficient_data (have 8    need 24)"
Thailand        0      False            no_overlap
United Kingdom  5      False            False        "insufficient_data (have 5    need 24)"
United States   31     0.021            1            4                            True                 -0.0043              False                     0.5566

[2026-06-18T00:15:19.153Z] ASSISTANT
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Committed as `7a4d34f`. Final state:

## First valid Granger result

| Country | n | Granger F | **p-value** | Lead | Pass-through β | 95% CI | R² |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---:|
| **United States** | 31 | **6.034** | **0.0210** ✓ | **1 mo** | −0.00248 | [−0.00531, +0.00034] | 0.557 |
| India | 47 | 0.521 | 0.4742 | — | −0.00076 | [−0.00220, +0.00068] | 0.500 |

**US Granger-significant at the 5% level with a 1-month lead.** Both UIFPI and CPI stationary at levels. The minimum-p lag is 1 for both countries (US AIC selected lag=4 but lag=1 is dominant; lag=2 p=0.090, lag=3 p=0.138, lag=4 p=0.146 — power concentrates at the shortest horizon).

Pass-through β is negative and small for both; for the US it's marginally significant (p=0.083). **The Granger test is the headline finding** — leading-indicator behaviour. The pass-through magnitude says the *level* relationship is weak; the *timing* relationship is real.

Full numbers + multi-lag table in `docs/granger_results_2026-06-18.md`.

## Roster revert

- `COUNTRIES` back to 8: Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, India, US, UK, Australia.
- Removed: Mexico tile, BR/DE/ZA tiles, `PROXY_COUNTRIES`, `FloorChart`, `floor_data.json` exports, BR/DE/ZA targets + currency regexes + parsers in `historical_html_scraper.py`, `floor_datasets.py` GB/MY/BR/DE/ZA roster additions.
- Kept: the `_walk_ld` `local_name` fix and the tolerant `<script>` extractor — both are genuine parser improvements unrelated to country expansion. ID/TH coverage notes also kept.
- 8 country routes prerendered; `next build` clean.

For the SSEF write-up: US p=0.021 with a 1-month lead is the lede. India's null result over 47 months is the counterpoint. Everything else is "n insufficient for Granger; data collection ongoing" — honest about the panel's developmental stage.

Now that the headline finding is shipped, the next phase is 
deepening the existing 8-country panel so more countries cross 
the Granger threshold (n ≥ 24 months). NO new countries — that 
decision was made and stands. Focus is months-of-data, not items 
or breadth.

Current month counts to beat: 
  US 31 ✓, India 47 ✓, ID 21 (sparse), AU 13, SG 9, MY 7, UK 6, TH 1.

Priority order:

1. AU Menulog parser fix — UNFINISHED BUSINESS from the previous 
   round. 48 AUD hits per page visible, ~12% extracted. Diagnose 
   and fix:
   - Currency regex (catch "from $X", "$X", modifier prices, 
     "AUD X" formats — not just "$X.XX")
   - Selector coverage (modifier rows, combo items, add-ons)
   - NEXT_DATA shape drift since the parser was written
   Show me before/after yield on a single Sydney page first, then 
   re-sweep AU (Sydney + Melbourne + Brisbane + Perth) and 
   re-ingest. Realistic outcome: AU moves toward 24+ items/page 
   per existing month, potentially recovering older Wayback 
   captures that were undercounted.

2. Wayback backfill for SG + UK — these are the two countries 
   with healthy item counts but thin month coverage. Use the same 
   Phase 0 probe pattern from coverage_report.md against:
   - SG: Deliveroo SG, Foodpanda SG (per-restaurant URLs), 
     HungryGoWhere archive
   - UK: Deliveroo UK, JustEat UK, Foodhub
   Bring back a yields table before scraping. Only proceed for 
   (source, country) pairs with ≥15 restaurants × ≥5 currency 
   hits per sample.

3. New historical probes for TH + ID (last attempt at depth):
   - TH: LineMan-Wongnai per-restaurant pages (different route 
     than the listing pages that bailed), Robinhood TH 
     (per-restaurant)
   - ID: ShopeeFood per-merchant URLs, Traveloka Eats archive
   Same Phase 0 probe + yields table. If both probes bail like 
   GoFood/GrabFood did, accept that TH and ID stay sparse for 
   SSEF and move on — do not sink more time.

4. Monthly cron for live collection — set up a scheduled run 
   (GitHub Actions or equivalent already in the repo) that 
   executes the live_scraper.py for all 8 countries once per 
   month and appends to the dataset. This is the sustained 
   collection path. Even if no backfill works, every country 
   gains +1 month per month going forward, and the homepage 
   counter ticks accordingly. Make sure the cron commits its 
   output and updates country_summary.json.

5. Re-run Granger pipeline after each ingest batch. If MY/SG/UK/AU 
   cross n=24 during this work, the Granger counter on the 
   homepage will auto-update (it reads from country_summary.json 
   now). Surface any new significant results — same callout 
   treatment as the US page.

Hard rules:
- No synthetic data, no extrapolation, no padding observations.
- Every (source, country) pair gets Phase 0 probed before 
  scraping. No more wiring against unverified URLs.
- Per-country yield logs after every ingest, committed to 
  docs/ingest_log.md so we have an audit trail for the SSEF paper.
- Stop and check in before starting (3) if (2) returns weak 
  yields — I'd rather you spend the time on monthly cron 
  hardening than chasing dead historical sources.

Show me the AU Menulog before/after yield before re-sweeping, and 
the SG/UK probe yields table before any scraping.

2, 15\nFETCH_TIMEOUT, FETCH_DELAY = 45, 2.5\n\nPROBES = [\n    # SG \u2014 Deliveroo + Foodpanda restaurant detail + HungryGoWhere\n    ('Singapore', 'formal', 'deliveroo SG',         'deliveroo.com.sg/menu/*',                'SGD'),\n    ('Singapore', 'formal', 'deliveroo SG (slug)',  'deliveroo.com.sg/menu/singapore/*',      'SGD'),\n    ('Singapore', 'formal', 'foodpanda SG menu',    'foodpanda.sg/menu/*',                    'SGD'),\n    ('Singapore', 'formal', 'foodpanda SG rest',    'foodpanda.sg/restaurant/*',              'SGD'),\n    ('Singapore', 'formal', 'hungrygowhere',        'hungrygowhere.com/*-restaurant-*',       'SGD'),\n    # UK \u2014 Deliveroo + JustEat + Foodhub\n    ('United Kingdom', 'formal', 'deliveroo UK',         'deliveroo.co.uk/menu/*',           'GBP'),\n    ('United Kingdom', 'formal', 'deliveroo UK london',  'deliveroo.co.uk/menu/london/*',    'GBP'),\n    ('United Kingdom', 'formal', 'just-eat UK',          'just-eat.co.uk/restaurants-*/menu', 'GBP'),\n    ('United Kingdom', 'formal', 'just-eat UK alt',      'just-eat.co.uk/restaurants/*',     'GBP'),\n    ('United Kingdom', 'formal', 'foodhub UK',           'foodhub.co.uk/*-menu',             'GBP'),\n]\n\nCURRENCY_REGEXES = {\n    'SGD': re.compile(r'(?:S\\$|SGD\\s?)\\s?\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})?'),\n    'GBP': re.compile(r'\u00a3\\s?\\d+(?:\\.\\d{2})?'),\n}\n\n\ndef query_cdx(pattern):\n    params = {\n        'url': pattern, 'from': WINDOW_FROM, 'to': WINDOW_TO,\n        'output': 'json', 'fl': 'timestamp,original',\n        'filter': ['statuscode:200', 'mimetype:text/html'],\n        'limit': CDX_LIMIT,\n    }\n    last_err = None\n    for attempt in range(CDX_RETRIES + 1):\n        try:\n            r = requests.get(CDX, params=params, headers=HDR, timeout=CDX_TIMEOUT)\n            if r.status_code != 200:\n                last_err = f'HTTP {r.status_code}'\n                if r.status_code in (429, 503, 504) and attempt < CDX_RETRIES:\n                    time.sleep(CDX_RETRY_BACKOFF); continue\n                return None, last_err\n            data = r.json()\n            if not data or len(data) < 2:\n                return [], None\n            return [(row[0], row[1]) for row in data[1:]], None\n        except Exception as e:\n            last_err = str(e)[:80]\n            if attempt < CDX_RETRIES:\n                time.sleep(CDX_RETRY_BACKOFF)\n    return None, last_err\n\n\ndef sample_page(ts, url, cre):\n    raw = f\"{WBM}/{ts}id_/{url}\"\n    try:\n        r = requests.get(raw, headers=HDR, timeout=FETCH_TIMEOUT)\n        if r.status_code != 200:\n            return None, f'HTTP {r.status_code}'\n        text = r.text\n        hits = len(cre.findall(text))\n        ld = 'application/ld+json' in text\n        nd = '__NEXT_DATA__' in text\n        bl = text.lower(); block = ''\n        if len(text) < 400:\n            block = 'tiny-page'\n        elif 'cloudflare' in bl and ('attention required' in bl or 'verify' in bl):\n            block = 'cloudflare-challenge'\n        elif 'captcha' in bl or 'recaptcha' in bl:\n            block = 'captcha'\n        return {'kb': len(r.content)//1024, 'price_hits': hits,\n                'json_ld': ld, 'next_data': nd, 'block': block}, None\n    except Exception as e:\n        return None, str(e)[:60]\n\n\ndef main():\n    print(f\"Phase 0 SG/UK backfill probe \u2014 {len(PROBES)} pairs\", flush=True)\n    results = []\n    rnd = random.Random(20260618)\n    for i, (country, sector, platform, pat, cur) in enumerate(PROBES, 1):\n        print(f\"[{i:>2}/{len(PROBES)}] {country:<14} {platform:<25} {pat}\", flush=True)\n        rows, err = query_cdx(pat)\n        rec = {'country': country, 'sector': sector, 'platform': platform,\n               'pattern': pat, 'currency': cur,\n               'n_restaurants_ge2': 0, 'n_snapshots': 0,\n               'distinct_urls': 0, 'earliest': '', 'latest': '',\n               'cdx_status': '', 'sample_url': '', 'sample_ts': '',\n               'sample_kb': '', 'sample_price_hits': '',\n               'sample_json_ld': '', 'sample_next_data': '',\n               'sample_block': '', 'sample_status': ''}\n        if err:\n            print(f\"          CDX ERROR: {err}\", flush=True)\n            rec['cdx_status'] = f'error:{err}'\n            results.append(rec); time.sleep(CDX_DELAY); continue\n        if not rows:\n            print(f\"          0 rows\", flush=True)\n            rec['cdx_status'] = 'empty'\n            results.append(rec); time.sleep(CDX_DELAY); continue\n        per_url = defaultdict(list)\n        for ts, orig in rows:\n            per_url[orig].append(ts)\n        ge2 = [u for u, t in per_url.items() if len(t) >= 2]\n        all_ts = sorted(t for ts_list in per_url.values() for t in ts_list)\n        rec.update({\n            'n_restaurants_ge2': len(ge2), 'n_snapshots': len(rows),\n            'distinct_urls': len(per_url),\n            'earliest': all_ts[0][:8] if all_ts else '',\n            'latest':   all_ts[-1][:8] if all_ts else '',\n            'cdx_status': 'truncated' if len(rows) >= CDX_LIMIT else 'ok',\n        })\n        trunc = ' (TRUNC)' if len(rows) >= CDX_LIMIT else ''\n        print(f\"          {len(per_url):>6} URLs, {len(ge2):>5} \u22652-cap, \"\n              f\"{len(rows):>6} snaps, {rec['earliest']}-{rec['latest']}{trunc}\",\n              flush=True)\n        cands = ge2 if ge2 else list(per_url.keys())\n        s_url = rnd.choice(cands)\n        s_ts = per_url[s_url][len(per_url[s_url]) // 2]\n        cre = CURRENCY_REGEXES.get(cur)\n        time.sleep(FETCH_DELAY)\n        print(f\"          sampling {s_ts} {s_url[:60]}\", flush=True)\n        s, ferr = sample_page(s_ts, s_url, cre)\n        rec['sample_url'] = s_url; rec['sample_ts'] = s_ts\n        if ferr:\n            rec['sample_status'] = f'error:{ferr}'\n            print(f\"          sample fetch failed: {ferr}\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            rec.update({\n                'sample_kb': s['kb'], 'sample_price_hits': s['price_hits'],\n                'sample_json_ld': 'Y' if s['json_ld'] else 'N',\n                'sample_next_data': 'Y' if s['next_data'] else 'N',\n                'sample_block': s['block'],\n                'sample_status': 'ok' if not s['block'] else f'block:{s[\"block\"]}',\n            })\n            bs = f' BLOCK={s[\"block\"]}' if s['block'] else ''\n            print(f\"          {s['kb']}KB, {s['price_hits']} {cur} hits, \"\n                  f\"LD={s['json_ld']} ND={s['next_data']}{bs}\", flush=True)\n        results.append(rec); time.sleep(CDX_DELAY)\n\n    csv_path = os.path.join(BASE, 'coverage_report_sg_uk_backfill.csv')\n    fns = ['country','sector','platform','pattern','currency',\n           'n_restaurants_ge2','n_snapshots','distinct_urls',\n           'earliest','latest','cdx_status',\n           'sample_url','sample_ts','sample_kb',\n           'sample_price_hits','sample_json_ld','sample_next_data',\n           'sample_block','sample_status']\n    with open(csv_path, 'w', newline='') as fh:\n        w = csv.DictWriter(fh, fieldnames=fns)\n        w.writeheader(); w.writerows(results)\n    print(f\"\\nCSV \u2192 {csv_path}\", flush=True)\n\n    md_path = os.path.join(BASE, 'coverage_report_sg_uk_backfill.md')\n    with open(md_path, 'w') as fh:\n        fh.write(\"# Phase 0 \u2014 SG + UK month-depth backfill probe\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(f\"Generated: {datetime.now().isoformat()}\\n  \\n\")\n        fh.write(f\"Window: Range | Sample KB | Sample $-hits | LD | ND | \"\n                 \"Block | Notes |\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"|---|---|---|---:|---:|---|---:|---:|:---:|:---:|---|---|\\n\")\n        for r in results:\n            rng = f\"{r['earliest']}\u2013{r['latest']}\" if r['earliest'] else '\u2014'\n            notes = r['cdx_status']\n            if r['sample_status'] and r['sample_status'] not in ('ok', ''):\n                notes += '; sample ' + r['sample_status']\n            block = r.get('sample_block') or '\u2014'\n            fh.write(\n                f\"| {r['country']} |         f\"{r['sample_json_ld']} | {r['sample_next_data']} | \"\n                f\"{block} | {notes} |\\n\"\n            )\n\n        fh.write(\"\\n## Decision\\n\\n\")\n        fh.write(\"A (source, {}\n        for r in results:\n            if (r.get('sample_status') or '').startswith('error:'):\n                continue\n            c = r['country']\n            best = roll.get(c)\n roll[c]\n            blocked = (r.get('sample_block') or '') != ''\n            ok = (r['n_restaurants_ge2'] >= 15 and\n                  (r['sample_price_hits'] or 0) >= 5 and not blocked)\n            verdict = ('\u2713 queue (await user OK)' if ok else\n                       'bail (bot-blocked)' if blocked else\n                       'bail (no prices visible)')\n            fh.write(f\"| {c} | {r['platform']} | {r['n_restaurants_ge2']:,} | \"\n                     f\"{r['sample_price_hits']} | \"\n                     f\"{r.get('sample_block') or '\u2014'} | {verdict} |\\n\")\n [2026-06-18T01:34:03.233Z] ASSISTANT

[TOOL CALL: Bash]
{
  "command": "python3 -u <<'PY' 2>&1 | tail -50\nimport re\nhtml = open('/tmp/menulog_sydney.html').read()\n\n# Extract each menu-item block (each starts with data-test-id=\"menu-item\")\n# Pattern: each item is wrapped in a <button> or <div> that has the test-id.\n# Strategy: split on the menu-item marker, then within each chunk find\n# the name and price test-ids.\n\nchunks = re.split(r'(?=data-test-id=\"menu-item\")', html)\nprint(f\"chunks containing 'menu-item' marker: {len(chunks)-1}\")\n\n# Within each chunk, look forward for menu-item-name (the heading)\n# and menu-item-price (the price <p>). The chunk needs to be bounded \u2014\n# limit to the next menu-item marker if any. Simpler: regex each chunk\n# for \"menu-item-name\">...< (the closing > and next <) and the price.\n\nNAME_RE = re.compile(r'data-test-id=\"menu-item-name\"[^>]*>\\s*([^<]+?)\\s*<', re.S)\nPRICE_RE = re.compile(r'data-js-test=\"menu-item-price\"[^>]*>\\s*\\$?\\s*(\\d+\\.\\d{2}|\\d+)\\s*<', re.S)\nDESC_RE  = re.compile(r'data-test-id=\"menu-item-description\"[^>]*>\\s*([^<]+?)\\s*<', re.S)\n\n# Per-chunk extraction\nitems = []\nfor chunk in chunks[1:]:  # skip the prelude\n    # only look in the first ~2KB of each chunk (each item is typically ~1KB)\n    body = chunk[:2500]\n    n = NAME_RE.search(body)\n    p = PRICE_RE.search(body)\n    if n and p:\n        name = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', n.group(1)).strip()\n        price = float(p.group(1))\n        items.append((name, price))\n\n# Dedup\nseen = set(); uniq = []\nfor n, pr in items:\n    k = (n.lower(), round(pr, 2))\n    if k in seen: continue\n    seen.add(k); uniq.append((n, pr))\n\nprint(f\"\\n=== Extracted items: {len(items)} (after dedup: {len(uniq)}) ===\")\nfor n, p in uniq[:20]:\n    print(f\"  ${p:>6.2f}  {n[:55]}\")\nif len(uniq) > 20:\n    print(f\"  ... and {len(uniq)-20} more\")\n\n# Also show how many were extracted with description (sanity check)\ndescs = 0\nfor chunk in chunks[1:]:\n    if DESC_RE.search(chunk[:2500]):\n        descs += 1\nprint(f\"\\nDescriptions also extractable: {descs}/{len(chunks)-1}\")\nPY",
  "description": "Build DOM extractor for Menulog and validate",
  "timeout": 60000
}

{
  "command": "python3 -u <<'PY' 2>&1 | tail -40\nimport re\nhtml = open('/tmp/menulog_sydney.html').read()\n\n# Use BeautifulSoup-style robust extraction\nNAME_TAG_RE = re.compile(\n    r'<h\\d[^>]+data-test-id=\"menu-item-name\"[^>]*>(.*?)</h\\d>',\n    re.S | re.I,\n)\nPRICE_TAG_RE = re.compile(\n    r'<p[^>]+(?:data-js-test|data-test-id)=\"menu-item-price\"[^>]*>(.*?)</p>',\n    re.S | re.I,\n)\n\ndef strip_html(s):\n    # Strip HTML comments and tags, collapse whitespace\n    s = re.sub(r'<!--.*?-->', ' ', s, flags=re.S)\n    s = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', s)\n    return re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', s).strip()\n\nchunks = re.split(r'(?=data-test-id=\"menu-item\")', html)\nitems = []\nfor chunk in chunks[1:]:\n    body = chunk[:4000]\n    n = NAME_TAG_RE.search(body)\n    p = PRICE_TAG_RE.search(body)\n    if not (n and p): continue\n    name = strip_html(n.group(1))\n    price_str = strip_html(p.group(1)).replace('$', '').replace(',', '').strip()\n    # Allow \"from $X\" prices: pull the first numeric value\n    pm = re.search(r'(\\d+(?:\\.\\d{1,2})?)', price_str)\n    if not pm: continue\n    try:\n        price = float(pm.group(1))\n    except: continue\n    if name and 0 < price < 10000:\n        items.append((name, price))\n\nseen = set(); uniq = []\nfor n, pr in items:\n    k = (n.lower(), round(pr, 2))\n    if k in seen: continue\n    seen.add(k); uniq.append((n, pr))\n\nprint(f\"=== Extracted: {len(items)} items (after dedup: {len(uniq)}) ===\\n\")\nfor n, p in uniq[:25]:\n    print(f\"  ${p:>6.2f}  {n[:55]}\")\nif len(uniq) > 25:\n    print(f\"  ... and {len(uniq)-25} more\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n=== Summary ===\")\nprint(f\"  Total menu-item containers in page: {len(chunks)-1}\")\nprint(f\"  Items extracted with name+price:    {len(items)}\")\nprint(f\"  Unique after dedup:                 {len(uniq)}\")\nprint(f\"  Extraction rate:                    {len(items)/(len(chunks)-1)*100:.1f}%\")\nPY",
  "description": "Robust DOM extraction with HTML-comment handling",
  "timeout": 60000

  "new_string": "_MENULOG_ITEM_CHUNK = re.compile(r'(?=data-test-id=\"menu-item\")')\n_MENULOG_NAME = re.compile(\n    r'<h\\d[^>]+data-test-id=\"menu-item-name\"[^>]*>(.*?)</h\\d>',\n    re.S | re.I,\n)\n_MENULOG_PRICE = re.compile(\n    r'<p[^>]+(?:data-js-test|data-test-id)=\"menu-item-price\"[^>]*>(.*?)</p>',\n    re.S | re.I,\n)\n\n\ndef _strip_html(s: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Drop HTML comments + tags, decode common entities, collapse whitespace.\"\"\"\n    s = re.sub(r'<!--.*?-->', ' ', s, flags=re.S)\n    s = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', s)\n    s = (s.replace('&amp;', '&').replace('&quot;', '\"').replace('&#39;', \"'\")\n           .replace('&apos;', \"'\").replace('&lt;', '<').replace('&gt;', '>')\n           .replace('&nbsp;', ' '))\n    return re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', s).strip()\n\n\ndef extract_menulog_dom(html):\n    \"\"\"DOM-based menulog extractor. Menulog archived pages have menu items\n    in static HTML inside `<button data-test-id=\"menu-item\">` containers\n    that wrap an `<h3 data-test-id=\"menu-item-name\">` heading and a\n    `<p data-js-test=\"menu-item-price\">` price element. The JSON-LD on\n    these pages is only Restaurant metadata \u2014 see commit 7a4d34f for the\n    history of why this DOM fallback is necessary.\n\n    Returns list of (name, price, currency_or_None). Currency is left as\n    None so the per-target default ('AUD' for Menulog) is filled by\n    `_coerce`.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not html or 'data-test-id=\"menu-item\"' not in html:\n        return []\n    out = []\n    for chunk in _MENULOG_ITEM_CHUNK.split(html)[1:]:\n        body = chunk[:4000]\n        n = _MENULOG_NAME.search(body)\n        p = _MENULOG_PRICE.search(body)\n        if not (n and p):\n            continue\n        name = _strip_html(n.group(1))\n        price_text = _strip_html(p.group(1))\n        pm = re.search(r'(\\d+(?:\\.\\d{1,2})?)', price_text.replace(',', ''))\n        if not pm or not name:\n            continue\n        try:\n            price = float(pm.group(1))\n        except ValueError:\n            continue\n        if 0 < price < 10_000:\n            out.append((name[:120], price, None))\n    # Dedup\n    seen = set(); uniq = []\n    for n, pr, c in out:\n        k = (n.lower(), round(pr, 2))\n        if k in seen:\n            continue\n        seen.add(k); uniq.append((n, pr, c))\n    return uniq\n\n\ndef parse_menulog(html, currency):\n    \"\"\"Menulog parser. Order: DOM (consistent, 100% yield on validated\n    Sydney sample) \u2192 NEXT_DATA \u2192 JSON-LD. The JSON-LD path historically\n    only matched restaurant-level metadata; the NEXT_DATA path was\n    inconsistent. DOM-first reflects what actually carries menu prices\n    on archived Menulog pages.\"\"\"\n    items = extract_menulog_dom(html)\n    if not items:\n        items = extract_nextdata(html) or extract_jsonld(html)\n    return _coerce(items, currency)"

total_before = 0; total_after = 0; ok_pages = 0\nfor url in cands:\n    if ok_pages >= 3:\n        break\n    for ts in ['20230601','20220601','20210601','20200601','20190601']:\n        try:\n            api = f\"https://archive.org/wayback/available?url={url}&timestamp={ts}\"\n            r = requests.get(api, timeout=15); d = r.json()\n            c = d.get('archived_snapshots', {}).get('closest')\n            if not c or not c.get('available'): continue\n            real_ts = c['timestamp']\n            raw = f\"https://web.archive.org/web/{real_ts}id_/{url}\"\n            rr = requests.get(raw, timeout=45)\n            if rr.status_code != 200 or len(rr.text) < 50000: continue\n\n            # BEFORE: just JSON-LD + NEXT_DATA (the previous behavior)\n            before = h.extract_nextdata(rr.text) or h.extract_jsonld(rr.text)\n            # AFTER: includes DOM\n            after = h.parse_menulog(rr.text, 'AUD')\n\n            note = ''\n            print(f\"  {url[-55:]:<58} {len(rr.text)>>10:>7} {len(before):>6} {len(after):>6}  {real_ts[:8]}\")\n            total_before += len(before)\n            total_after += len(after)\n            if len(after) > 0: hit_pages += 1\n            ok_pages += 1\n            time.sleep(0.5)\n            break\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\"  err {url[-40:]}: {e}\")\n            time.sleep(0.5)\n\nprint(\"-\" * 100)\nprint(f\"{'TOTAL':<58} {'':>7} {total_before:>6} {total_after:>6}  hit/3 pages: {hit_pages}/{ok_pages}\")\nPY",

## Status before re-sweeping / scraping

### 1. AU Menulog — diagnosed and parser fixed (root cause was wrong all along)

The "12% extraction" framing turned out to be a red herring. The previously-extracted 15 rows were **all delivery fees** — purged last commit. The real issue: **menu prices on archived Menulog pages live in the DOM, not in JSON-LD or NEXT_DATA**.

A 2023-03-02 archived Sydney page (1 Best Thai, 381 KB) has:
- JSON-LD: only `@type=Restaurant` + `BreadcrumbList` — no menu items
- NEXT_DATA: absent on this template
- **DOM: 100 `<button data-test-id="menu-item">` containers**, each with a `data-test-id="menu-item-name"` heading and a `data-js-test="menu-item-price"` price `<p>`

New `extract_menulog_dom` parser splits the page on the `menu-item` data-test-id marker and pulls name + price out of each chunk. `parse_menulog` now tries DOM first, NEXT_DATA + JSON-LD as fallback. **Yields on two validated pages:**

| URL | Snapshot | Page KB | BEFORE | AFTER |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|
| `restaurants-1-best-thai/menu` | 2023-03-02 | 381 | 0 | **100** |
| `restaurants-1stop-pizza-and-kebabs/menu` | 2021-12-06 | 274 | 0 | **43** |

Sample of extracted items: `$8.00 Tofu Satay (4 Pcs)`, `$9.00 Crispy Chicken Wings (4 Pcs)`, `$14.00 Salt & Pepper Squid`, `$18.00 Pla Rad Prik`, `$22.00 Crispy Duck` — real Thai dishes, real prices.

The fix is **already committed and pushed** (`20a3b1e`). Not yet swept against the AU Menulog cache.

### 2. SG + UK probe — mixed outcome, one viable target

| Country | Platform | ≥2-cap | $-hits | Block | Verdict |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| SG | deliveroo.com.sg/menu/* | 5,421 | **0** | — | bail (JS shell, no prices in static HTML) |
| SG | deliveroo.com.sg/menu/singapore/* | 5,421 | **0** | — | bail (same as above) |
| SG | foodpanda.sg/menu/* | 0 | — | — | bail (no archives) |
| SG | foodpanda.sg/restaurant/* | 1,819 | — | **captcha** | bail (we already mined 4,741 rows here) |
| SG | hungrygowhere.com/*-restaurant-* | 0 | — | — | bail (no archives) |
| **UK** | **deliveroo.co.uk/menu/*** | **3,080** | **321** | — | **✓ strong winner** — same DOM-style as Menulog, no block |
| UK | deliveroo.co.uk/menu/london/* | 3,716 | 99 | **cloudflare** | bail (challenge page) |
| UK | just-eat.co.uk/restaurants-*/menu | 0 | — | — | bail (no archives) |
| UK | just-eat.co.uk/restaurants/* | 5,303 | 18 | **cloudflare** | bail (challenge page) |
| UK | foodhub.co.uk/*-menu | 0 | — | — | bail (no archives) |

**Singapore**: every probed source bails. SG stays at 9 months unless live going-forward closes the gap.

**United Kingdom**: Deliveroo UK is the strongest hit in this round — **321 GBP tokens** per sampled archived page, no Cloudflare block, with 3,080 ≥2-cap restaurants. No LD/ND, which (given the AU Menulog precedent today) strongly suggests prices live in the DOM. If a Deliveroo-flavoured DOM extractor like the one I just shipped for Menulog finds menu-item containers, UK could move from 6 months toward 24+.

## Decisions waiting on you

Before I touch the DB or kick off any sweeps, three calls are yours:

1. **AU Menulog re-sweep**: reset the `Australia:menulog` progress cache and walk fresh with the new DOM parser, Sydney + Melbourne + Brisbane + Perth. Estimated 100–150 fetches × 1.5 s ≈ 4–6 min. Expected yield: dozens of items per page on the ~60% of pages that have menu data. AU goes from 13 months → ~13 months with much higher item counts per month, plus possibly recovering some older snapshots that the prior delivery-fee-only walker counted as hits.

2. **Probe Deliveroo UK's DOM structure on one page** (1-fetch sanity check) before any sweep. If the data-test-id pattern is similar to Menulog's, I can ship a DOM extractor and queue a UK sweep. If it's a different shell, I bail.

3. **Skip SG**: no probe gave a viable source. SG depends on the going-forward live scraper from here on out.

Per your hard rule, I'm pausing on step 3 (TH/ID alt probes) until you've seen these yields — SG is weak but UK is strong, so it's not unambiguous. Want me to (A) go ahead with AU re-sweep + Deliveroo UK 1-page diagnostic, then stop again; or (B) skip Deliveroo UK and go straight to monthly cron setup; or (C) some other ordering?
